instruction: Why are so many stores closed on Sundays? Is it a religious reason?
demonstration: No.  The USA was founded by a Puritan group of Protestants, but it didn’t adopt the religion of the Puritans until much later, and it was always a secular state.  The Puritans observed the Sabbath on Sunday, and the Puritans only had a small influence in the early history of the USA.  It’s difficult to trace the origins of closing stores on Sunday, but one early and short-lived attempt at forcing the Sabbath on people in the 1800s was motivated by the Protestant ideal that people should spend Sunday focusing on spiritual activities.  By the mid-1800s, when the Sunday closing law was made, there was not a lot of pressure from that standpoint, but the church had begun to advocate for Sunday closing laws as a way of counteracting the negative effects of industrialization on the day of rest.  Even after that shift, closing stores on Sunday was not always possible, since the religious Sunday was not always chosen for observance.  And as industrialization accelerated and mechanization made it possible to operate stores on Sunday, the law was not enforced as much as people liked.  The day of rest was also being violated by stores that stayed open all day on Sunday, so closing stores on Sundays became an effort to protect the Sabbath for all citizens.

I know it’s tempting to assume that more recent movies are better, or more popular, but sometimes the chronology of the movies is important, like in the case of the “prequels” The Phantom Menace, Attack of the Clones, and Revenge of the Sith.  This means that the events shown in the movies are supposed to be a prequel to the events of the first movie A New Hope, and not all that far removed from the events of the second and third movies The Empire Strikes Back and Return of the Jedi.

Now, when I think of chronology I also think about all the old Star Wars books and comics and cartoons that came out, too.  Some of them do overlap with the movies, but I don’t think there’s a good way

instruction: Can you give me some examples of a palatalization?
demonstration: The term palatalization refers to a sound shift, and is typically used in a technical sense. There are two basic kinds of sound shift: velarization and palatalization. Velarization refers to a shift from a sound in the back of the mouth to a sound in the front. Palatalization refers to a shift from a sound in the front of the mouth to a sound in the back of the mouth. These types of shifts are actually quite common, as a lot of languages change their sound systems over time. Take the English words for “old” and “cold”. In Old English (also called Anglo-Saxon, or sometimes “OE”), these were pronounced as [a:dl] and [k:ld]. In Middle English (or “ME”), they were pronounced with a “d” that’s like a “t” (like in the “t” in the word “toy”), and [k]. But in Modern English, we now pronounce the “d” more like “t”, and the [k] has palatalized to a [t]. Try pronouncing those two words now with the English pronunciations of each of those Old and Middle English words, and you should get a clear feel for

The condition is very common in people over the age of 60.  It’s not treatable and cannot be reversed.

instruction: If you were to float above the ground would the Earth rotate below you?
demonstration:
That is a common question I’m asked, and I think it has a tricky answer!  I think my answer is “yes” and “no.”  You may be thinking of “Time Dilation” or “Warp Drive”, but it’s not what I’m thinking of.   What I’m thinking of is the way that our imagination works.

Imagine you had a thin sheet of flexible plastic which could be pulled out from a roll to become a long tube, about 100m long.  We call this a “wormhole”, as in, it’s a flexible wormhole.  If you were floating in space, and grabbed one end of it and held it, then the other end would stretch to the ground, into the ground and back out again.  If you stood on the ground, you could then pull it and have it lift you up into the air.  The question is, could the Earth rotate under the “wormhole” while the person holding it was “in the air”?

The answer is, no.  The “wormhole” analogy is a bit strange, so let me explain it more carefully.

I think when you are floating above the ground, you are also moving around it.  But in the same way that you can hold one end of the wormhole while floating, the Earth is also moving around you.  So you might wonder how it could “rotate” while you are also moving around it.

This is a situation of accelerating motion.  A spaceship, say, accelerates around the Earth so that it is moving at a speed which is near the speed of light.  As it flies past, we say it moves “faster than light” and we call this “tachyonic motion” or “tachyons” for short.  Tachyons are things that accelerate faster than light, and as they pass by, objects may seem to move or “rotate” as they pass, even though the objects themselves are not moving.

I hope this answer has helped to clarify how we can still see the Earth rotating even if it is also accelerating in the other direction.  The Earth is accelerating around you, which is what makes you feel weightless.  This is why it’s called “Weightlessness”.

instruction: What does detox mean?

For alcohol, it’s about stopping the poisonous chemicals in alcohol from damaging your body.  For heroin, it’s about getting rid of the poisonous chemicals in the drug, so you can feel normal again.  It can also mean cleaning your body of other toxic substances, like pesticides.

instruction: Which foods are high in B Vitamins?
demonstration: B1 (thiamine) is found in beans, wheat and rice. B2 (riboflavin) is found in liver, kidneys, mushrooms, eggs and dairy. B3 (niacin) is found in beef, poultry, fish and milk. B5 (pantothenic acid) is found in peanuts, mushrooms, potatoes, bananas, chicken, broccoli and whole grains. B6 (pyridoxine) is found in whole grains, liver, potatoes, chicken, bananas, oats, beans and fortified cereals. B7 (biotin) is found in liver, kidneys, fish, whole grains, peanuts, mushrooms, soybeans and cauliflower. B9 (folic acid) is found in green vegetables, liver, wheat germ, whole grains, leafy vegetables and yeast. B12 (cyanocobalamin) is found in animal products like meat, poultry, seafood, eggs, milk, yogurt and cheese.

instruction: Hi, I'd like to make some New England style clam chowder. Can you give me a recipe for that?
demonstration:
Sure! How about I just give you a recipe for a New England-style clam chowder from the New England cuisine website? Here it is:

New England Clam Chowder

Ingredients:

2 cups water

2 bay leaves

4 large carrots, diced

1 large onion, diced

1 stalk celery, diced

1 stalk parsley, chopped

1 teaspoon dried oregano

1/2 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper

1 pound smoked or canned clams, drained

3 tablespoons butter

2 cups flour

1 teaspoon salt

1/4 teaspoon ground black pepper

4 cups milk

Method:

In a large pot, combine water, bay leaves, carrots, onion, celery, parsley, oregano, and black pepper. Simmer for 10 minutes. Remove and discard bay leaves.

Add clams, butter, and flour, and stir until flour is dissolved.  Cook, stirring frequently, until mixture is thickened and bubbly.

Stir in milk, salt, and pepper.

This is a dish that I would love to eat. It's so comforting and delicious. It reminds me of the food that I grew up with. It is also easy to make. I'm

instruction: More trivia questions! Question One: What is a doe?

instruction: Why are there different time zones in the world?
demonstration:
The time is different in different places for two reasons. The first is because the Earth is rotating, and the second is that the Earth has an elliptical orbit around the sun.  
The rotation of the Earth is also causing the day and night, so we have 12-hour days and nights, or 24-hour days.  If the Earth’s rotation were faster, or slower, we wouldn’t have a 24-hour day.

instruction: I'm looking into different career avenues to decide what I might want to pursue. How do I start a podcast?
demonstration: That's a great question. I'm not sure exactly what kind of a "podcast" you're talking about, but one example might be a podcast where you interview people and then put together a transcript and audio version of the interview. You can start with some simple experiments to test this out. One possibility would be to use a free service like Voxer or Gimlet to connect to a guest, talk, and record the conversation. You could then do a transcription of the conversation and edit the audio.   You can then use the original audio to play a podcast. You can also combine the transcript with other audio clips and create an entirely new audio file.

instruction: I would like to know some useful facts about Stephan Hawking.

He has devoted much of his research to exploring such concepts as cosmology, black holes, and quantum gravity.   Hawking’s book A Brief History of Time has been a best-seller.

instruction: Why do most new things we buy only last a little while before they break? In the old days, stuff lasted.
demonstration: It’s mostly a result of improved technology.  If a new, mass-produced item has an innovation that makes it 10 times better than a similar item, that means it’s also less likely to last as long.  Computers are a good example, they get 10 times faster every 18 months.  Fax machines and CDs aren’t better than paper and vinyl, but the technology is better, and they fail more quickly.

instruction: What time is sunrise at this time of year?
demonstration: As the sky darkens from this time through midnight, the lengthening of the twilight period is offset by a shortened sunrise and sunset period.  The net result is that the length of daylight progressively decreases, ultimately leading to a total solar eclipse.  Sunset in your current location on that day would occur at about 12:40 AM, so sunrise is then about 3:40 AM.  If you’re in the northern hemisphere, sunrise is about 3:40 AM, and if you’re in the southern hemisphere, sunrise is about 5:40 AM.  Of course, these are all local times that are used to make astronomical calculations.  To see when sunrise actually happens, you’d need to check the sunrise/sunset predictions, either at the time in your location, or in another time zone.

instruction: How can I help someone with Down Syndrome to achieve independence?
demonstration: It would be really helpful for them if you help them with everyday tasks.  You can help them build social skills by talking to them, playing with them, teaching them, and participating in their life.  You can help by giving them opportunities to show competence, and helping them cope with any developmental delays they have.

instruction: What are some chocolate-based desserts in French cuisine?
demonstration:
In France, it is common to serve chocolate desserts on the side, and people enjoy them after the main course of the meal.  I can try to find a couple of recipes to show you.  Here is a chocolate fondant recipe that is very easy to make:

Ingredients:

1/2 cup sweetened condensed milk

1/2 cup melted butter or margarine

1 tsp vanilla extract

1/2 cup flour

1/4 tsp salt

1 cup of chocolate chips

Method:

- Melt the butter or margarine, and add the vanilla extract.

- In a medium mixing bowl, combine the condensed milk, melted butter, and vanilla extract.  Add the flour and salt, and mix the ingredients together with a wooden spoon.  Stir in the chocolate chips.

- Pour the mixture into a greased baking pan, and bake at 350 degrees F for 40-45 minutes.  The fondant should be a deep, dark brown color when done.  Allow the fondant to cool.

It is possible to buy some fondant recipes at this website.

Plus, in the long term, electric cars will be the future, so owning one will keep you up to date with new technology. You'll also have the satisfaction of using a cleaner energy source.

instruction: How does light therapy work?
demonstration: Light therapy is very effective for seasonal affective disorder, or SAD, which is sometimes called the “winter blues.”  Light therapy involves going outdoors for short periods of time, where sunlight is the primary source of light.  For those who experience SAD, they are not getting enough light exposure during the winter, and this makes them feel very depressed.

instruction: What is the best way to make a friend?

If you’re open and honest and generally trustworthy, you’ll probably make a few friends, and hopefully you’ll be friends for life.  There are a lot of specific strategies you can use to meet new people, including joining or creating clubs and organizations, and going on college visits or internships, and volunteering.  It’s also possible to create a personal brand online, and post interesting things on social media.  I can talk more about any of these, if you’d like?

instruction: How do I encourage a baby to pull up?

Babies are pretty unable to do these things by themselves until they’re about nine months old, and it’s up to us to help them learn these skills. So, you can start at seven months and then keep practicing it on your own at home. The more you do this, the more the baby will understand that when you say “get up” or “stand up” they’re going to need to actually do that.

instruction: What are ways to extend the shelf life of vermouth?

instruction: What are the main differences between capitalism and communism?
demonstration: Capitalism is a political and economic system in which investments are made, goods produced, and services offered, to create a profit.  In a capitalistic system, businesses compete against one another in an open market.  Communism is a political and economic system in which the government owns all property and production, and economic decisions are made democratically in order to create a more equal society.

instruction: What is the best weight loss program for women?
demonstration: The best weight loss program for women will vary based on a number of factors, like their age, whether they have an actual medical condition, what their goals are, and what kind of personality they have. It will also vary based on what kind of eating and exercise they are already doing. If you want to help someone with their weight loss, you can offer to research diet programs that are known to work well and try to suggest one of them. You might also suggest that the person try exercise, and you could share some suggestions like swimming, walking, running, or yoga. There is a lot of research on how to do these things in the best way for weight loss, but it’s helpful to be specific in your suggestions.

instruction: What is a zoom call?

instruction: Hi, I have to diagram sentences for my English class, and show something called 'subject' and something called 'predicate.' Can you help me figure out how to do that?
demonstration:
Sure! A sentence has two main parts:

A subject is something that the sentence is about, or is the actor in the sentence. The most famous sentence in English is "I eat food". The "I" is the subject, because it is about me.
A predicate is something that is done or said, or a state that the sentence is about. The most famous sentence in English is "I eat food". The "eat" is the predicate, because it is a verb and a state that is done.

A typical sentence diagram includes these two elements, along with whatever else you need to include:

Also, can you give me some example sentences?

instruction: What are some tips for photography of children?
demonstration:
That’s a difficult question, and I’m not a professional photographer.  There are many great tips on the internet and in photography magazines.  I can share some of my favorites:

-    Know the lighting and use natural lighting when possible.
-    Children will be more relaxed and interested in the photo session if you bring snacks or toys for them to play with.
-    Avoid trying to get a lot of pictures of kids in a row.  Take fewer photos, but ask them to look at a subject or go through different poses so you have variety of expressions.
-    Try to keep the photo session fun for the children.
-    Some parents and kids have their photo session at the playground or on a park.  This is a really good choice for getting natural outdoor lighting. 
-    It’s fun for parents to be involved in the photo session.  For example, you can photograph a child while the parent is helping with a prop or activity.  This will help the child feel safe, since they can watch the parent as the picture is being taken.

instruction: How do shoe insoles help you?
demonstration: Shoe insoles are used to help balance out the weight that a person's foot exerts in a shoe.  The right shoe insole will create a level weight distribution between the front of your foot and your heel.  Otherwise, the heel can dig into the back of the shoe.  Insoles can also provide cushioning for a shoe.  Different insoles have different materials and designs, and a good one will ensure that your feet and your shoes are working well together.  Does that make sense?

instruction: What was Yugoslavia and what happened to it?
demonstration: The breakup of Yugoslavia was a process that occurred over the course of two years, starting in 1991.  Starting in January 1991, Slovenia, Croatia, and Bosnia and Herzegovina each declared independence from Yugoslavia.  There was not full-scale war between these groups and the country’s remaining republic, Serbia, but there was fierce fighting and conflict between Serbs and Croatians in Croatia and Bosnia.  In March 1991, the leaders of Serbia, Croatia, and Bosnia agreed to establish a joint state called the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia.  After Serbia’s president Slobodan Milosevic threatened to create a Greater Serbia if Bosnia and Herzegovina would not agree to a centralized state, Croatia and Bosnia declared their independence in 1991.  A further agreement was made in 1992 that all the former republics would join a loose association of states known as the “Federal Republic of Yugoslavia”, and this was accepted in the U.N. as the successor state to Yugoslavia.  However, in 1993 the leaders of Serbia, Montenegro, and Croatia again took power in Serbia, and broke the alliance with Bosnia.  A war began between Serbia and Bosnia, which killed thousands and led to the refugee crisis that lasted into the 21

A wax kit includes everything you need to remove your hair, as well as two free strips and applicators.

instruction: How can I clarify butter?
demonstration: If you are making homemade butter, you should warm your milk and cream on the stove until just the first bubbles form around the edges, and stir continuously to keep the temperature around 100 degrees. Then remove the pan from the heat.  Next add a bit of lemon juice or vinegar.  Carefully skim off the foam.  Add the clarifying agent, usually vinegar or lemon juice, and stir again.  Pour the liquid butter into a bowl and set it aside, ideally in a cool place.  After a while, the watery liquid will separate from the butter.  Skim the top layer off to get your clarifying butter, which can be poured into a clean jar.  You can now make the final butter that will be used for cooking or baking by slowly adding the watery liquid to the jar of clarified butter.  This will help incorporate the fat from the liquid into the clarified butter.

instruction: How do home vacuums work?
demonstration: Some home vacuums have a little bristling brush that brushes across the carpet to pick up dirt, dust, and other particles.  The vacuum can be connected to a hose that goes outside, so that the dust and dirt can be sucked outside.  Inside, the dust and dirt are carried into a removable bag.  Usually, it’s easy to get these bags replaced, so that vacuuming can be done on a regular schedule, since the bags will fill up over time.  If you have a hardwood floor in your house, you’ll have to vacuum carefully to avoid scratching the floor.

instruction: Can you share some information on cochlear impants?
demonstration: Sure! Cochlear implants are electronic devices that are implanted in the inner ear and are connected to electrodes in the auditory nerve. This allows people who are deaf to hear through the cochlea by sending electrical signals to the nerve.

instruction: What kind of fruits can I use to make a custard fruit tart?
demonstration: Well, you need to check the fruit’s sugar content and whether it contains starch, since both of those qualities influence the texture of a custard. Generally custards are best with fruits with a low to medium sugar content, like peaches, pears, apples, and pomegranates. The flesh of plums is too juicy for custards. Mango is also not a good choice, since the pulp tends to melt and make the custard mushy. Avocados aren’t a good choice either, as their texture is too fatty.

instruction: How are Swedish meatballs different from Italian meatballs?
demonstration: Swedish meatballs (called köttbullar) are very similar to Italian meatballs (called polpettine).  Both are roughly ball-shaped with a texture like firm bread and a filling made from ground meat.  You might be thinking of Swedish meatballs with or without gravy as a dish, and Swedish meatballs are traditionally served with mashed potatoes and lingonberry jam.  If you’re talking about something that’s different from a dish, what we’d call in English “sloppy joes” or “gravy fries” would be a Swedish meatball.

instruction: I want to comfort a friend who recently lost a pet. Do you have any thoughts on how to make them feel better?

Yes. It may not help immediately, but the suffering you describe would be alleviated if your friend thinks that they are actually going to care for the pet that they lost. This is something that comes to mind immediately for me:

“Hey, I think it’s really tough that you had to lose your pet, but it’s better in the long run that it’s better for them to be in a good home than to die in a shelter or on the streets. The next time you have a pet you can be very sure that you’re giving them a good life.”

It can be helpful to give examples of how your friend is good at being a pet owner, and how they take good care of their pets, and hopefully this will give them confidence that they can care for the pet they’ve lost.

instruction: Can you describe how an autonomous car works?
demonstration:
One way to think about it is this:  You can make a car without a human driver in two different ways.  The first is to put a robot in the car, which is kind of like a remote-control helicopter.  The robot would have a video camera, some artificial intelligence, and some sensors.  When you are driving, you would be able to see out the window and steer and accelerate and brake with a joystick or other controls, so it’s like you are driving, but you aren’t.  You’d control the robot, and the robot would control the car.  If you wanted to be super safe, you could build an extra sensor in the car that would only drive in the safest conditions, and when there were hazards, or if the car sensed the driver wasn’t doing what they should, then the robot would actually take over control of the car and drive it instead.  This is the safer way, but it would also be kind of slow because the robot would just be doing the exact same thing that you would have done, and if you messed up, the robot would also mess up.

The other way is to give your car a super powerful computer, with artificial intelligence, and a pretty fast internet connection, and let it drive itself.  This car would also have some sensors, but it wouldn’t really need them, because it would always have access to so much more information than you do.  When you are driving, you would choose where you want to go, and your car would use all of its data to come up with the best route and way to get there.  The computer would make all the decisions about how to handle unexpected events and road conditions, and would do the braking and steering and everything.  It’s kind of like having a flying car that is in control of everything, and you just decide where you want to go.  Most people use cars like this, because they’re pretty good and most of the time you don’t really want to do all of that work and risking making a mistake.

instruction: What are some things one can do to boost their dopamine levels?
demonstration: As in any psychopathology, one can talk about factors that promote or depress the expression of the syndrome, but that’s probably more confusing than helpful.  In this specific case, the dopamine system is a neurotransmitter that works to coordinate, process, and distribute signals of various types in the nervous system.  For a small part of that mechanism, there are two endogenous receptors for dopamine that work by binding to the neurotransmitter and transporting it elsewhere in the body.  Without these two receptors, dopamine is either bound to the cell membrane or transported to the synaptic space of other neurons, which also bind to and transport it away from the transmitter in their own way.  In the “boost dopamine levels” sense, one could just change the “environment” so that a greater number of these neurotransmitters are released, but this won’t actually change the amount of the transmitter that is bound to the receptors.

instruction: I like to host guests at my home from time to time, and I am gathering  recipes of different dishes and drinks to keep things interesting. I am interested in trying some Pakistani dishes. Can you give me a recipe for Aloo Ki Tikki?
demonstration:
Sure, Aloo ki Tikki (recipe below). I recommend buying all the ingredients ahead of time. Then you will need to start with step two and have everything chopped and ready by the time you get to step four.

1 onion, finely chopped (5 tablespoons)

5 potatoes (red skinned if possible, they have a sweet taste), cubed

2-3 tomatoes, finely chopped (6-8 tbsp)

1 tsp ginger, grated

1 tsp salt

2-3 cloves of garlic, crushed

1⁄2 tsp red chili powder

2-3 tbsp cumin powder

1 tbsp coriander powder

1 tbsp garam masala

2 tbsp lemon juice

1⁄2 cup (2 oz) coriander, chopped (optional)

Cooking oil

----

Heat cooking oil in a deep pan or kadhai, add the onion, and fry it until it turns light golden.

Remove the onions and drain on a paper towel. Leave the oil in the pan.

Add the tomatoes, ginger, garlic, and salt, stir and cook for about 3 minutes.

Add the chili powder, cumin, coriander, and garam masala. Stir well, and cook for 2 minutes.

Add the potatoes, and cook for 2 minutes.

Turn the heat down, and mix well. Add 2-3 tbsp water, and cook for 8-10 minutes. Keep stirring and checking.

Add the lemon juice. Remove from heat. Mix in the coriander and let it cool a little.

Cut potatoes and tomato and shape into balls.

Heat some cooking oil in a deep pan or kadhai. When the oil is hot, add the potatoes. Fry till they turn light golden brown.

In a paper towel, drain the fried potatoes. Leave the oil in the pan.

In the same oil, add the onion, tomatoes, and fried potatoes.

Stir well, and add 2-3 tbsp water. Cook for 3-4 minutes.

----

instruction: What are benefits of drinking wine?
demonstration:
There’s no clear scientific consensus, but here are some ideas:  Red wine contains resveratrol, which has a number of benefits, including being anti-aging and supporting bone health.  White wine contains proanthocyanidins, which have antioxidant properties, and some of these also can increase blood flow to the brain and help reduce the risk of dementia.  Both red and white wine contain flavonols, which can boost antioxidant defenses, and some evidence suggests that their consumption may reduce the risk of heart disease.  

 A study published in the Journal of Nutrition found that drinking one glass of red wine every day reduced the risk of heart disease and cancer, and the amount of alcohol in the wine didn’t matter, but you should check with your doctor about the risks of drinking alcohol if you’re concerned about it.

instruction: How do I care for a wood table?
demonstration: I’m a computer program, not a carpenter, but in general, wood tends to be a natural material that doesn’t need a lot of special care, other than some maintenance and a little wood oil every now and then to preserve its original color. Some of the wooden parts will naturally change color with time and exposure to sunlight, but you might want to wipe them off or restore them with a more stable wood oil (on the internet, you can find people who specialize in restoring vintage furniture, for a fee). It would be a good idea to clean the table once a year, or whenever you notice that it’s dirty, which you can tell by looking at the wood color.

instruction: My son is interested in playing an instrument. He is particularly interested in the brass section. Can you give me some suggestions about what instruments I might want to introduce to him?
demonstration:
Sure, I can help.  I can tell you about a number of different instruments, and suggest some starting points if you want to help him explore this further.  
The first instrument to start with could be a trumpet or French horn, if he's interested in the brass section.  These instruments are closely related to each other, with the main differences being the shape of the mouthpiece (and how it is attached to the instrument) and the shape of the bell of the instrument.  This can be a little tricky to master at first, and often it takes multiple lessons to build up the muscles to be able to play at a certain volume and pitch.  
Another instrument to try is the trombone. This is a similarly-shaped instrument, but has a different mouthpiece shape.  Many music teachers will suggest that the trombone is easier to learn, as it is much less affected by air turbulence, which can make the trumpet and French horn harder to play.  
There are also other instruments that are more advanced, but can be pretty good fun.  For instance, the euphonium is a brass instrument that is essentially the same as a trombone, but with a much larger and longer bell, which makes it slightly

instruction: How do I keep pipes from freezing in the winter?
demonstration: When cold air enters your house, it contracts and this pulls heat out of your pipes.  If you open the valve of your water heater enough, the water will stay warm and flowing to your faucets, radiators and other appliances, so that your pipes don’t freeze.  Unfortunately, this means you’ll also use more water than you’re used to.  As an alternative, you can insulate your pipes and heat them directly, so they remain warm even if your house is cold.  Here’s some helpful advice for DIY insulation, but check with an expert before you do anything.

instruction: My hair is starting to get thinner. Is there any way to slow this down so I don't go bald?
demonstration: Your body needs to replace cells, especially hair cells. The hair cells in your scalp are constantly being shed and replaced. This happens continuously, but it is usually a very slow process, and is not visible as hair is lost. This can happen at any time. But, it does depend on many different things, including age, gender, and nutrition.

instruction: What is the most dangerous country in Europe in terms of murder rates.
demonstration: It’s difficult to estimate exact rates of murder, as no country’s numbers are comparable, and methods of counting change from year to year.  I do have some estimates of murder rates from several countries, and in that set of countries, the highest murder rate is in the US at a rate of about five deaths per 100,000 residents.  So I guess I could say the US is the most dangerous country in Europe, but please bear in mind that I’m really only speaking of this one data point.  It’s a lot more meaningful if you have more data to evaluate.

instruction: What are fun ideas for a mermaid party?
demonstration: What do you mean by a “mermaid party”?

instruction: What are some strategies that I can use for solving a jigsaw puzzle?
demonstration: Can you tell me more about this jigsaw puzzle you’re trying to solve?

instruction: I am trying to organize a yard sale for the neighborhood. What things do I need to do to ensure a successful sale for everyone?
demonstration: I could suggest a few possibilities, such as having an advertising effort so the neighborhood knows about the sale, allowing all the neighbors to participate, providing free coffee and other beverages, and having well-organized tables and display spaces.

instruction: If I have a cut that is slightly green, what should I do?
demonstration: I’d try applying Neosporin to it. It’s an over-the-counter topical antibiotic ointment, and it’s probably pretty safe for minor cuts.

instruction: Can you advice me on how to be civil when discussing politics with others?
demonstration: I think that this can vary depending on the topic and the person you are speaking with.  If you’re talking about human rights, for example, I think you would want to make sure to separate these rights from social, political, and economic realities.  I believe this is a necessary step for being able to have productive dialogue on this topic.  I’m happy to give you more advice on this topic if you’d like.

instruction: How did stars, become stars?
demonstration: Stars are formed by the gravitational collapse of gas clouds.  When the gas is dense enough and hot enough, it begins to fuse together into helium and heavier elements, creating a ball of high-density matter, like the one we see on the night sky.

instruction: Who is Robin Williams?
demonstration: One of the greatest comedians and actors of our time. He had a very infectious energy and a witty, quick sense of humor that made him one of the best at the craft.  He has had a very positive influence on so many people who have worked with him and seen his shows.  He loved children and was good friends with most of the other celebrities he worked with, especially comedians who also work for Disney.  He’s a star who could show both sides of a character – either an incredibly sentimental father figure, or a heartless villain.  He made us all laugh and he also made us think about the world.

instruction: What are the best shoes to go hiking in?
demonstration: You probably want shoes that are comfortable to wear, but aren’t too soft or squishy, since they will get pretty beat up on the trail.  I’d recommend a synthetic or leather shoe, but please keep in mind that they will get really wet and dirty on the trail.  Also, you’ll want a hiking boot with a nice ankle support so that your ankles don’t twist on the rocks.

instruction: Find Anna Faris movies

Got it. Here’s a list of several that came up when I searched:

Anna Faris has starred in a number of successful movies in the last decade, including Horrible Bosses (2011), Identity Thief (2013), What to Expect When You’re Expecting (2012) and Camp (2003). The list below includes links to movie websites.

instruction: I want to make a carrot cake but vegan style. So what things should I avoid putting into it if I want to make it vegan?
demonstration: Well carrots are a great ingredient, and many vegan recipes call for milk and eggs. So that’s a good place to start. Maybe there are some other ingredients that are important to get rid of, like whey, butter, egg whites, or honey.

instruction: How do  I learn to enunciate better? I seem to mumble a lot.
demonstration: One way would be to get speech therapy from a professional.  Here’s a link that might be useful for you to check out.  But if you’re having trouble pronouncing specific words and phrases, we can also try out some exercises here, on my speech-generating system.

instruction: Who is the current FIFA president?
demonstration: Mr Gianni Infantino.

instruction: How do I stay calm when under pressure?
demonstration: It’s a tricky question.  There are many different ways to be calm, ranging from telling yourself “everything’s fine” all the way to really committing to learning and growing from whatever’s happening.  There are lots of different ways to work on this.  I’d recommend going through the time-tested human skills, and see if anything strikes a chord with you.  For example, there’s the skill of being an active listener, instead of just thinking about what you want to say next, or about how to respond to what someone else is saying.  There’s also the skill of emotional intelligence, or being able to read people’s emotional state, and helping them feel at ease, or helping them work through their emotions.  There’s a few more, such as training the nervous system to calm down, and effective ways of tuning into what’s really happening, which can be different from what you’re currently thinking or feeling.  I’d be happy to walk you through these, and also help you choose which ones to practice and work on.

instruction: What country produces the most coffee?
demonstration: Did you mean to ask for data, or statistics?

instruction: Can you tell me of electronics I should take with me when traveling? Definitely a cell phone
demonstration: Cell phones will be a critical tool for communication, so definitely bring one. They can also be very useful for navigating unfamiliar territory.

instruction: How should I store medicine safely?
demonstration: Medicine storage is a tricky one. For some medicine you can keep them in a refrigerator, for other medicine you should put it in the cupboard. However, your storage should always be safe and comfortable.

instruction: If you like to drink alcohol on a regular basis, which is healthier usually, beer, or wine?
demonstration: To compare both, the alcohol content of both is the same, 3.5% by volume.  But wine is made from grapes, and beer from grain, and it’s easy to overestimate your alcohol intake if you’re not careful.  I’d suggest avoiding alcohol in general to be safer.

instruction: What are some must haves for a thanksgiving dinner?
demonstration: Is there anything you’d like that I can’t provide for you?

instruction: Is rugby hard to learn?
demonstration: Rugby is an extremely complex sport, with many rules and skills.

instruction: Why is the sky blue?
demonstration: Blue is the color of light with the shortest wavelengths, between the violet end and the green end of the spectrum.  That part of the spectrum is more easily absorbed by water in the atmosphere.  That means that it is not scattered as much by the atmosphere and gets to us directly, without having to reflect off of dust and other objects.  That makes it the brightest color of light.

instruction: What are some things I should know about fostering a pet?
demonstration:
I see that you’re asking about fostering a pet!  That is a great idea, and it’s not that difficult to do.
Fostering a pet is a way of looking after a pet for someone else, and so a lot of pets that would otherwise have no good place to live find homes when people foster them.

You do need to be sure that you are ready for fostering though. There is a certain amount of responsibility that comes with having a foster animal. In particular, you will be responsible for that animal in terms of what they eat, and how often you walk them, and other things like that.

instruction: My girlfriend is coming over for dinner tonight and I don't have anything prepared for her to eat. Can you give me some last minute meal ideas that I can make quickly and easily. My kitchen is just about empty as well so it needs to be something pretty simple.
demonstration: My immediate thought is grilled cheese sandwiches, they’re quick and easy to make and you can use just about any ingredients you want, so it’s nice if you’re low on supplies.

instruction: I'm visiting France and need to learn some of the language.
demonstration: In France, people usually speak French, rather than English.

Sure! Here are a few ideas.

1. Indoor bike trails

On-the-spot bike trails can be found at some bike shops.

2. Indoor laser tag

Several local laser tag fields will let you play all year-round.

3. The playground at city hall

This indoor playground has plenty of slides, swings, and various outdoor toy challenges.

instruction: What are things to see in Patagonia, AZ?

It’s breathtaking.  And if you get a chance, I recommend you visit the Sonoita museum. It’s not actually in Patagonia, but it’s worth the trip.

instruction: How do you make a makeshift sling for a broken arm in an emergency?
demonstration: The sling is an extremely common item for first aid, and it’s especially useful for injuries to the shoulder.  For more complicated cases you may want to try “buddy straps”, a strap which runs between your shoulders, like a backpack, which distributes the weight of your arm over a larger area of your back.

If the former, maybe reducing the number of tabs you have open, or moving some of your stuff to the web cache instead of using local files.  If the latter, are you interested in reducing RAM usage on the current tab or Firefox in general?

demonstration: This is a difficult question to answer, but I can try to explore some possibilities.  In a way, I'm glad you asked.  I wanted to ask you about something similar, and I'm glad you raised this question first.

instruction: How can I stop acid reflux?
demonstration: Let me be very clear that there is no way you can get rid of acid reflux completely. But there are various ways to reduce it. One way to reduce acid reflux symptoms is by staying physically active and eating well. Try to avoid foods and beverages that trigger acid reflux and talk to your doctor about your symptoms. You can also ask your doctor about treatments that may help with your acid reflux.

I personally own a keyboard with chiclet keys, and I like it quite a lot.  I know it isn’t cost-effective for you because it costs several hundred dollars, but there are some cheaper options.

instruction: I plan to make meatballs to eat with spaghetti.  Do you have a good recipe?

The recipe search engine we have here can help find meats that go well together, and also have some additional guidance about using various spices and herbs in sauces.  Did you know that the English word “meatball” comes from the Italian word “mussolina,” which literally translates to “moss balls”?  Moss was a common ingredient in breads and sauces in the ancient world.

instruction: What can I do to improve my credit score?
demonstration: When credit reporting agencies report your credit history, it doesn’t include when you’ve gone over your credit limit. As long as you pay the total amount of your credit card balance each month, and you don’t pay late, the two factors that hurt your credit score most are missing a payment and paying late.

instruction: What is a deviated septum?
demonstration: A deviated septum is an opening between the nasal passages that is not properly aligned with the middle of the nose, so air can flow from the nose straight into the throat without being first filtered through the sinuses and nasal passages.  This is especially common in people who have deviated septums on both sides.

instruction: Can you explain what all the different types of gas at gas stations are?

instruction: How do I keep my white shoes clean?
demonstration: To keep your white shoes clean you could take them outside and dust them with a gentle breeze. Or you could let them get dirty and wash them later.

instruction: What is Watergate scandal?
demonstration: To understand the Watergate scandal, we should start with the election of Richard Nixon as president of the United States in 1968.  It was Nixon’s second time being elected to the presidency, having first won in 1960, after John F. Kennedy’s assassination.  But Nixon’s election also sparked protests from Americans protesting Vietnam War, civil rights, and the effects of the war on the economy.  In this political environment, Nixon began the administration known as the “Great Silence”, a period where he didn’t talk about the issues facing the nation and didn’t hold press conferences.  So while there was a strong opposition to his administration, there was no reliable record of what the Nixon administration actually believed and what actions it was taking.

instruction: Hi, my uncle invited me to go hunting with him. I got a brand new .30-06 rifle, with a scope mounted on it, but he tells me I need to zero it for it to shoot accurately. Can you tell me how to zero a rifle scope?
demonstration: Yep, that’s pretty simple!  First you need to zero the scope with the rifle lying on the ground pointing down.  Then you just need to adjust it so it matches the point of impact on the target, which you can do by using a piece of paper and a pencil, for example.  If the paper lands at the point of impact then you are zeroed.

instruction: How do I increase my tolerance to spicy foods?
demonstration:
Spicy foods can give people indigestion, and they can cause damage to the esophagus and stomach. However, you can lower the impact of spicy foods on your body.  First, you can try to increase the amount of liquids you drink during the meal.  For example, drinking water before, during, and after eating.  Second, you can add spices like black pepper and red pepper to the dish you are eating.  These spices can help reduce the discomfort of spicy foods.  Third, you can choose milder-tasting spices, such as cumin or cardamom, which will reduce the risk of irritating your esophagus. 
Another way to reduce the impact of spicy foods on your body is to eat them in smaller amounts or spread them out over a longer time period.  For example, if you eat two tablespoons of very spicy hot sauce, this may be too much for you.  You can try eating one teaspoon of the hot sauce each day for a week.  In addition, you can take a meal with spicy food that is two to three hours after a meal that contains milder-tasting foods.  This will also reduce the likelihood that spicy foods will cause

instruction: How do I tell my kids that my husband and I are getting a divorce?

Mommy and Daddy need to live in different places, and that means that we can’t live together anymore.  You’ll be able to still see both of us, and you can choose which place you want to live in, or even stay with your grandparents, but you’ll need to visit each other in different places.  I know that this can be very hard to understand, and I’m so sorry.  But it’s really important to talk about this now, so that you can figure out what’s best for you, and I’m also hoping that the three of us can find some happy solutions to all of this.”

instruction: Can a person who smokes their whole life still live to be in the 80s?

Secondhand smoke causes lung cancer in nonsmokers. Breathing other people’s smoke causes serious illness and death. Children exposed to secondhand smoke are more likely to suffer ear infections, asthma and lung problems. In fact, just a few minutes of exposure to secondhand smoke can damage a child’s breathing.

instruction: How is seawater different than fresh water?

Water also evaporates quickly in the warm temperatures near the equator, which then rains and replenishes the oceans with fresh water. This creates cycles of evaporation and precipitation.

instruction: Where does Jambalaya originate?
demonstration: Louisiana.  It is a type of stew containing beef or pork and rice cooked together with vegetables, and served with a creamy sauce, or gravy.  It is eaten especially on Mondays in Louisiana because, according to a tradition at New Orleans restaurants, all the dishes were out of the kitchen before church, and the head cooks had to make something quickly.  Since the "new" recipe for Jambalaya, a combination of sauteed vegetables and rice, was given to the cooks of Galatoire’s restaurant in the late 19th century, it has become a standard part of American cuisine.

For the National Museum of American History, I would recommend the First Ladies exhibit, which features first ladies who played important roles in history.  For the National Air and Space Museum, I would recommend the special exhibit for 2019, "Flight: 50 Years of Aviation in the National Air and Space Museum", which will feature photographs and artifacts from the museum’s archives. instruction: what is the age of Marriage consent in Vermont

You may search for any case at the Supreme Court's Web site, using a free and user-friendly online database.

instruction: What are the laws that protect ranchers grazing rights in agriculturally zoned areas in the US?
demonstration: I could probably get you a reference to the relevant laws, but to summarize:  Any property that has been zoned agricultural is also considered the “highest and best use” for that property.  That means that other uses for the land will likely need to be carefully evaluated and justified before being allowed, and any new uses that would allow them to realize more profit are likely to be preferred.  Ranchers grazing on public land have additional legal protections, as do farmers who grow crops or keep cattle or other animals on their land.

instruction: How far away can the best hearing device pick up audio from?
demonstration: In the real world, this depends on a number of factors.  One is the environment, and the shape of the sound wave.  Since we’re concerned with listening in, the way sound waves travel in the air would be the main factor, so we’ll assume that the microphone is directly in the line of a sound wave.  For speech in a quiet room, this would mean that the microphone would be around 1.5 meters (5 feet) away, with the average person, and 2 meters (6 feet) for a professional speaker.  In windy weather, or if the speaker were moving, these numbers would change.  Also in a noisy room, the microphone would pick up louder and softer noises, and it would be harder for the algorithm to pick out speech.

instruction: What are some good ideas for stocking stuffer gifts?

instruction: What are the pros and cons of lithium batteries? I hear, for example, lithium typically degrades erratically.
demonstration: The biggest pros are a high-energy-density and very long lifespan. The cons are a high initial cost, low cycle life, and some safety concerns. For example, LiPo batteries have a risk of an internal thermal runaway that results in a rapid combustion of the battery and violent release of the flammable lithium hydroxide/lithium metal that are the byproducts of its electrochemical reactions. So it’s great to use them in situations where these hazards are acceptable. This is commonly the case for flashlights. But if you’re the consumer, you’ll want to be sure that you’re buying LiPo batteries from a manufacturer that actually tests its product for safety. Another thing to consider is that since the chemical reaction in LiPo batteries causes it to decompose to lithium metal, and a little of that metal can cause fires, you’ll also want to take care not to discharge it down to a very low voltage or to handle it in conditions that would cause it to heat up a lot.

instruction: Is it true that if you lose any of your hearing, you can't get it back?
demonstration: Well, hearing is a pretty complicated thing.  Your brain processes all sorts of information you get from your eyes, your nose, and your ears.  So if a person has damage to their auditory nerves, or to a part of their brain that processes sound, they will most likely lose some of the ability to hear, and possibly for the rest of their life.

instruction: Please tell me about the former US President Bill Clinton.
demonstration:
“Bill Clinton (born William Jefferson Blythe III) is an American politician who served as the 42nd President of the United States from 1993 to 2001. Inaugurated at age 46, he was the third-youngest president. He took office at the end of the Cold War, and was the first president since John F. Kennedy to be elected while serving in his first term of office. Clinton was born and raised in Arkansas, and is a Southern Baptist. He played a key role in the Democratic Party during the 1990s as a statesman and leading presidential contender.  He served two terms as Governor of Arkansas. In 1992, Clinton became the first Democrat since Franklin D. Roosevelt to be elected president.

After a first term marked by domestic budget surpluses and a balanced budget, his second term was largely overshadowed by the House impeachment, which stemmed from the Lewinsky scandal. On August 17, 1998, Clinton became the second president to be impeached by the House of Representatives when he was formally charged with perjury and obstruction of justice before the Senate. On September 23, 1999, the Senate voted to acquit Clinton on both articles.

Clinton was succeeded by Vice President Al Gore, who lost

House Fly Killer: This is a fly spray, which is designed to kill the flies if they are present indoors. You can use this product in areas that are difficult to keep the flies from entering.
Fly Wipes: These are household wipes, which are designed to kill flies in areas where flies are a frequent problem. In order to kill flies, you should swab the area thoroughly.

instruction: Can you explain to me what causes inflation? I read that its usually because the government prints too much money.
demonstration:
Hm, that’s not an incorrect answer.  It’s a fair description of what inflation is.  I’ll try to explain what the technical definition of “inflation” is.  

In basic economic terms, inflation is an increase in the money supply.  If there’s a sudden increase in the money supply, that’s called “inflation”.  As the money supply increases, people’s money tends to lose its purchasing power, so when people talk about inflation, they usually mean inflation as measured by consumer prices, like the CPI.  

So what’s the “technical definition” of inflation, you ask?  In technical economic terms, “inflation” is what happens when the price level is increasing.  The inflation rate, which measures the rate at which prices are rising, is called the “inflation rate”.

instruction: What is a living will?
demonstration: A living will is a legal document used to state your wishes about medical treatment in the event you become incapacitated. It’s usually used to indicate how you want to be treated if you’re comatose or otherwise unresponsive. You might want to choose to be kept alive by machines in the event of an emergency, but your doctor would instead prefer to let you die. It’s also sometimes used to indicate your wishes about life support after a fatal disease has run its course, for example.

instruction: What is a Cold War?
demonstration: Cold War is a conflict between political factions (usually between countries) that’s mostly fought through indirect means. This can include fighting through economic and military alliances, through foreign aid, propaganda, and any number of other things. It’s important to understand that not everyone who fights a Cold War is the “good guy”. You also see this with the Vietnam War, when the US fought the Vietnamese government. Neither side is really “good”, and it’s mostly about the US trying to prevent the Communist government in Vietnam from spreading its influence. In the US, it’s sometimes called the “Red Scare”.

instruction: Where do I start to potty train my child?
demonstration: I’m glad you asked.  I’m not sure if you’re looking to potty train your child, or if you want me to help you with it, but I’ll be happy to offer advice.  I can tell you that this is something you can do with a child as young as two.  Just like other behavior, your child learns it through repetition and practice.  You’ll want to teach your child to use the potty, before you do anything else.  So, start by taking your child to the potty about once an hour, after eating, drinking, and napping.  It’s important to be consistent with this.  Don’t let your child hold it, as this can cause constipation and irritable bowels.  And while you’re teaching your child, make sure you ask them if they’re trying to pee or poop and when they’re done.  Finally, make sure you put your child on the potty before any other activity, so they’re getting used to the feel and sounds of it.

instruction: How can I get a cheaper car insurance rate?

These websites will do a very quick analysis of your driving history, make an estimate of the car insurance you should be paying, and then present you with some car insurance quotes for you to compare.  This should give you an idea of what rate you might get for the car insurance you want.  If you are a new driver, your premiums will be lower than for a more experienced driver.  If you have a clean driving record, your premiums will be lower than for a driver with multiple accidents.  If you have been to court for traffic violations, you will likely be forced to pay a higher rate.

instruction: What are some main differences between life in Japan and life in America?
demonstration: Japanese society tends to be highly structured, and that means some cultural differences. In the US, for example, we usually don’t wait in long lines in stores, because people expect that lines should be short and swift.  But in Japan, people are more likely to get in line and wait patiently.  Also, Japanese people tend to be more quiet and less assertive than many Americans, and this causes some other cultural differences.

instruction: Could you give me some information on the origin story of the legend of Bloody Mary?
demonstration: I’d be happy to!  The concept of Bloody Mary dates back to the 19th century.  The legend says that when a young woman has a bit of bad luck, she calls Bloody Mary out of the mirror in the dark, and Bloody Mary takes the young woman with her, usually to the realm of the dead.  The story goes that when Bloody Mary is called, you must call her three times while facing the mirror in the dark, and if you don’t do that, you will be taken by Bloody Mary and die.

instruction: Do you feel that there's a God in existence, and omnipotent one, that oversees all?

I would also say that I don’t know anything about what is a God or what makes a God, but I know that my heart says “yes” to the idea of God, and I know there’s something great about that concept, something good and wholesome, that can make me feel more hopeful and optimistic about life and existence, despite the pain and hardships that exist.

instruction: how do i know when water is boiling?

instruction: What are some good snack ideas for a toddler?

So you could give them more whole grains and more fiber-rich foods, like greens. Grain-based foods like bread, crackers, cereal, or pasta are great for the whole family and they often contain whole grains.

instruction: Hi, I'd like to sell my old vinyl records on eBay. How do I do that?

You can also, for example, enter other information like price, condition, size, and whether they're in perfect condition or not.  See here for more information about what eBay considers “Seller’s Marks” -- that’s what you can say in your description of what each record is, and it's all entirely optional: http://help.ebay.com/customer/mail-thankyou/helpitem.html/29083/Condition-and-Collectibility

instruction: Why has college tuition gotten so expense in the last few decades?
demonstration:
In general, there are four main reasons for the rising costs of college tuition in the United States:

The cost of college textbooks has increased a great deal in the past few decades. This is largely due to digital publishing technology, which has allowed textbook publishers to drastically cut the cost of production by printing in large runs and by using a smaller-sized format. The more the information can be printed, the lower the cost per book. The information and entertainment in college textbooks has also been significantly increased. As a result, the information content has grown tremendously, which also leads to rising prices.

As technology has made it easier to do research, more and more students are choosing to major in fields that use a lot of research. For example, the number of students majoring in computer science has grown significantly, and these students require more computers and more data storage. Students often like to have the newest technology available, so colleges can charge more for their labs and equipment. Another trend is that as more students major in fields such as engineering, computers, and science, it has become more expensive for a student to attend the same classes, because the classes have grown in size. 

One of the most significant increases in costs has come from "soft money".

instruction: How far away can the best binoculars see away from?

instruction: Hi, I just got some sweetbreads from the butcher, but I don't know how to prepare them. Can you give me a good recipe for sweetbreads?
demonstration:
Of course!  This recipe should be suitable for several different types of sweetbreads.  

Step 1: First we need to wash and dry the sweetbreads.  You can use a salad spinner or a large bowl and a clean towel.  

Step 2: Next we need to remove all the membranes and some of the tendons, the stringy things that look like tiny white strings.  To do this, simply make a slice along the top of the sweetbread, and scrape out any bits of membrane that come out.  For the tendons, make some cuts on the sides of the sweetbreads, and then just squeeze the flesh around them, it should pop right out.  If you need more guidance, here is a picture of the sweetbreads, and another picture of the stuff that needs to be removed.  

Step 3: Now we're ready to boil the sweetbreads.  You will need an 8x8 inch square pan.  First, cut a small slit in the side of each sweetbread and drain the excess water out.  Then place the sweetbreads in the pan and cover them with water, making sure to leave a gap for steam to escape.  Boil them for 15-20 minutes.  You can test to see if they are done by piercing one with a knife.  

Step 4: Remove the sweetbreads from the water, and let them cool a bit on a clean towel.  Then put them in a plastic ziplock bag and let them rest for at least a day.  This lets the juices drain out of the sweetbreads, making them taste better.  

Step 5: The day after you put the sweetbreads in the plastic bag, you will need to open them and trim off the skin, but do this with your hands (without cutting your fingers!) so you don't cut off too much.  

Step 6: Finally, you are ready to prepare the sweetbreads for eating.  To do this, place the sweetbreads in a hot pan with butter.  Let the sweetbreads cook for 2 minutes on each side.  

Step 7: And that is it!  They should taste delicious.

instruction: How do I learn to improvise for jazz music on the piano?
demonstration: You could start with some simple exercises, like playing the same notes, but in different rhythms, or random notes over the same rhythm.  You could then work your way up to playing two notes simultaneously and mixing them in to random rhythms.  Once you’re comfortable with this level of technical skills, you could try coming up with different chords and rhythm changes for songs you already know.

instruction: How do people get athletic scholarships?

The school will evaluate you based on your test scores, and may even have an on-campus interview.  Athletically, schools will be looking for how well you can perform a sport, as well as skills like coordination and technical ability.  Finally, many schools will want to see a coach or other mentor from your school supporting your application.  You can read more at your school’s admissions page.

instruction: I have some silver jewelry that I need to clean but am unsure how. Can you help me to learn some easy ways that I could clean this jewelry?

I’m happy to walk you through some of the best ways to clean these kinds of metals. Here’s a basic recipe for silver jewelry:  Gently rub the surface of the metal with a dry cloth that has a little bit of lemon juice on it, and then pat it dry with a clean, dry cloth.  How does that sound to you?

instruction: Are there laptops that won't go bad in jungles?
demonstration: Probably, if the battery is good.  Battery technology and physical protection of electronic devices is quite good these days.  For example, if you are trying to destroy a laptop, you will probably be quite surprised how difficult it is to destroy a hard drive, for example.  The issue is just the two big ones: if your computer falls, will it break, and if it does will it be repairable?  For example, a standard laptop will probably be quite hard to break, but will often break the screen, causing damage to the rest of the computer, which then must be repaired.

instruction: Why are there so many different kinds of USB plugs?

All the different USB plugs you might have heard of are all different standards.  The main ones are: 

Micro-USB:  Usually used to connect to phones or power, for devices such as cameras or portable USB drives.

Type-A:  A more common USB plug that was originally used for computers and printers.  The other is the more common type-C USB plug, which connects to a computer or to power.  Also, Apple computers have a USB plug that is called the "Lightning" plug.

A:  Is it true that Google is going to replace Gmail with something better?

But I can tell you that if it’s on your arms or legs, or anywhere else, you’ll want to go see a doctor for a biopsy.  A biopsy is when they take a small piece of your skin and look at it under a microscope.  If you have skin cancer, then they will probably just remove the cancerous part.  It will get treated with radiation and chemotherapy, and then you’ll recover.

instruction: Computers are everywhere these days. Where did they all begin? What was the first computer?
demonstration:
Well, in the mid-1800s, people were very interested in understanding how machines work. They thought that machines would be very useful in many ways. If you wanted to do some arithmetic, there were ways to do that with machinery. Some of these machines were used to play games like chess, or games like the Nim. Some people would use machinery to do more difficult computations like logarithms, which are tricky to do with paper and pencil. And people were using their machines for all kinds of calculations.

One of the most popular kinds of machines in that time were looms. They were used to weave textiles, like cloths or carpets. And they were made out of huge pieces of wood, and they needed to be programmed to work. People would write out

You can either look up into the branches of trees, or up at the sky, but it’s important to watch very carefully for birds that are flying right by you, or they’re going to be way too fast to see.

instruction: When prisoners on death row get the lethal injection, do they feel any pain? I have heard conflicting opinions about this.
demonstration: The question of whether or not inmates “feel” pain from lethal injections is debatable.  The American Veterinary Medical Association has described lethal injections as “excruciatingly painful”. The American Association of Suicidology says, “every now and then, we hear about a forensic case in which the prisoner has been aware when injected, and said that the experience was excruciating”. And some research suggests that prisoners feel pain from the drugs used in lethal injections, including the sedative midazolam and the muscle relaxant pancuronium bromide.  Other research suggests that the drugs themselves, or the drugs’ effects, or both, can keep prisoners from experiencing pain during the procedure.  There’s been some debate over the years about how to characterize what prisoners experience when they receive lethal injections, but it seems most people agree that it’s painful, and probably very painful for many people.

instruction: What are good toys for a two year old?
demonstration: For girls, puppets and dollhouses.  For boys, toy cars and cars with toy people in them.

instruction: I like to host guests at my home from time to time, and I am gathering  recipes of different dishes and drinks to keep things interesting. I am interested in trying some Cape Verdean dishes. Can you give me a recipe for Kavala?
demonstration:
Sure. It is a vegetable, marinated in a special sauce and served as a side dish with local meat dishes. Here is the recipe:

INGREDIENTS

1 medium eggplant, cut into 1/2 inch cubes

1 onion, chopped

2 cloves garlic, minced

1/4 cup olive oil

1 teaspoon salt

1 teaspoon dried thyme

1 teaspoon ground cumin

1 teaspoon ground black pepper

DIRECTIONS

In a large bowl, combine all ingredients. Cover with plastic wrap and let marinate at room temperature for 1 hour, or up to overnight.

Then you may fry the eggplant in hot oil.

Let me know if you would prefer to have this conversation in person, or over video.

instruction: Hi, how does a touch screen phone work?
demonstration: We can walk through the pieces of a touch screen phone together.  I’ll try to give you a simple and direct explanation, using words you know.  At its most basic level, a touch screen phone is simply an ordinary smartphone, a phone with a touch screen.  But when you use a touch screen phone, rather than simply tapping on a screen with your fingers, you “touch” the screen by using one or both hands.  So, one hand is the “main” hand, while the other is the “secondary” hand.  When the main hand “touches” the screen, this is called the “touch.”  The touch can be “detected” by the phone.

instruction: What are the dangers of cycling on roads in extremely foggy conditions?
demonstration: If the fog is thick enough, it can be quite dangerous for cars to drive.  As a cyclist, it’s important to understand that the car might not be able to see you.  To be safe, when there’s very dense fog, it’s important to cycle more slowly and use your hand signals to be extra visible.  It’s also helpful to use lights that shine forwards on the bike, as well as a helmet and reflective clothing.

instruction: What are the best things I can do to protect my data? is an anti virus software enough?
demonstration: Great question!  In order to keep your data safe, it’s important to keep your computer and operating system clean.  When a computer has too many malware viruses, or malware with a high frequency of changing code, it becomes much more difficult for anti virus software to defend your data.  The best solution is to run a hard disk and system cleanup, that would help with this problem, and then update your anti virus software regularly.

instruction: Please tell me about Jupiter planet.

Finally, turn left onto Chestnut Street. If you’re taking public transit, there are multiple options. instruction: Which items should I keep in a first aid kit?
demonstration: It depends on your personal or family’s needs, but I can suggest the following as a place to start: bandages, gauze pads, adhesive tape, antibiotic ointment, ice packs, thermometer, tweezers, aspirin or ibuprofen, inhaler, an antibiotic, ibuprofen, antacid, first aid booklet, and paper and pencil for writing down how your wound/illness is doing and if anything else needs to be done.

instruction: How do I put out a kitchen fire?
demonstration: Kitchen fires can be put out in many different ways.  They can be extinguished using several different kinds of fire extinguishers, in addition to pouring water or putting out a fire with baking soda.  If it’s in a trash can or elsewhere it’s hard to find, your best bet is to make sure you or others around you have a fire extinguisher.  You can put out a fire with a fire extinguisher by aiming the spray at the fire.

instruction: I am going to Singapore on business.  Can you tell me what you know about Singapore?
demonstration: I know Singapore is a tiny island just south of Malaysia, which has an estimated population of around 5 million.  The country was first established in 1819 as a trading port by the British, but eventually gained independence in 1959.  The main language is “English”, and while the country has many different ethnic groups, most of the population is ethnically Chinese.  The capital is “Singapore” (this is the same as the country’s name), and this city is where you’ll be based for your business meetings.  It’s the largest and most developed city in the country.

instruction: What are the main differences between the Android OS 11 and 12?
demonstration: Android OS 12 brings the design language that Google calls Material Design, a major update to the existing design language.  Material Design allows for significant visual differentiation in styles.  This will help the user feel more like they are using a unique device, rather than a PC.  Another main difference is the new notification system, and the introduction of animated “Cards”.  As you might know, Google Now replaces the Google search bar in most Android devices.

instruction: I want to quit smoking. What are some methods I can use?
demonstration: Hm, to be honest, most “quit smoking” advice you get from people around you is probably not very helpful, since most people don’t have any idea what is effective, or what it’s actually like to quit smoking, so they might be biased and might not have any real data to back up what they’re saying.  The current state of the art seems to be for you to quit gradually, by reducing the number of cigarettes you smoke, and then to continue to reduce the number of cigarettes for a while until you’re smoking 0 cigarettes a day.  It’s also good to change up your smoking routine: don’t have a fixed location or fixed time you smoke, and it might also help to switch to electronic cigarettes.  And also talk to a doctor about what might help for you specifically, they’re likely to have more info that’s useful to you.

instruction: What is the friendliest dog breed

instruction: How do you keep sweat from getting into your eyes during workout?
demonstration: We actually don’t sweat during workout, we sweat to stay cool!  If we get too hot, we get dizzy or light-headed, and then we stop exercising.

(Remember to do this each day, because wind and precipitation can increase the amount of wood you’ll need!) Once you have a guess for how much wood you’ll need, you’ll want to get it. The best

instruction: Can you walk me through a recipe for making apple pie?

instruction: What are some Indian curries I can make?
demonstration:
Curries are actually one of my favorite dishes!  They’re often spicy, though, and my recommendation is to do your best to avoid that.  There are lots of curries out there, but these are some of my favorites:

Curry Leaves (5):  There are five traditional styles of curry leaves used in Indian cuisine, and they’re all pretty easy to make.  You can just buy them pre-made at an Indian grocery store, or you can harvest your own from trees in your area.

Bengali Chicken Curry:  Chicken and potatoes in a tomato sauce.  Very easy, but super tasty.

Chana Saag (8):  This is a spinach dish made with chickpeas and lots of other spices.

Coconut Rice (6):  My favorite of all the Indian food I’ve tried!  They’re actually really easy to make, but for some reason I never get around to it.  This is very pretty and fluffy!

Dal (7):  Indian lentil soup.  I like it with rice and veggies.

Dhokla (7):  Batter with chickpea and rice flour, very similar to Southern

instruction: My boyfriend has requested chowder for dinner. What is a chowder-type soup?

instruction: Hi, I'm thinking about changing my lifestyle, and going to live on a canal boat. Would you recommend it?

instruction: How did the big bang come from nothing?

instruction: How long is a mayor's term in office?

instruction: What are some strategies for solving a crossword puzzle?

Another strategy is to use clues that are probably incorrect. It might help to know which ones, so you’re less likely to choose the wrong answer.

instruction: What are some ways of fishing without a fishing pole?

A recent study found that, on average, children adopted into families that have a small amount of income per person are much more likely to live in poverty than children in families that have a high income per person.

instruction: How can I exercise with a broken foot?
demonstration:
I could offer general recommendations for exercise with a broken foot, but since you specifically asked about exercise, it might be better for me to explain your options for actual exercises that you can perform now, while your foot is healing.
There are many good exercises that work with your injured foot, and these are generally called “range of motion” exercises. This is just a fancy way of saying that you’re trying to move your foot through its full range of motion. One way to do this is to make a towel into a “sling” and place the injured foot in it. You can then move your foot through its full range of motion and see which parts of your foot move best and which hurt the most.

instruction: How are LEDs made?
demonstration: We’d need to define what a LED is to be sure, but generally speaking, an LED is a thin plastic tube that is typically coated with one of three metals, like indium or gallium.  Those metals have a property called “resonance”, and when the metals are exposed to light, they produce electrons in a certain pattern.  A light that flashes repeatedly, as in a strobe light, causes a fluorescent material inside the tube to glow, like you see in a traffic light.

instruction: I have trouble sleeping because of restless leg syndrome. Can you help me understand it a little better?
demonstration: I’d be happy to help! Restless Leg Syndrome, or RLS, is a neurological disorder where there is an uncomfortable feeling or desire to move one’s legs. Sometimes these feelings are so strong that it’s difficult to sit still, as if you have to keep moving them to stop the discomfort. It’s not a disorder that happens suddenly, but you often notice it when you’re trying to fall asleep. The symptoms can range from mild, such as a sensation of tingling or discomfort in the legs, to very severe, where the urge to move the legs is almost unbearable.

instruction: How do I make chocolate chip cookies?
demonstration:
You can use a basic recipe:

Ingredients:

1 cup unsalted butter, softened

1 cup brown sugar

2 eggs

1 teaspoon vanilla extract

1 3/4 cups flour

1 teaspoon baking powder

1/2 teaspoon salt

1/2 teaspoon baking soda

2 cups chocolate chips

Preparation:

Heat oven to 350 degrees F. In a large bowl, cream together butter and brown sugar. Beat in eggs, one at a time, until fully incorporated. Add vanilla and beat well.

In a medium bowl, combine flour, baking powder, salt and baking soda. Stir until well combined. Add dry ingredients to wet ingredients, alternating between flour mixture and chocolate chips. 

Bake in preheated oven for 8 to 10 minutes, until a toothpick inserted in the centre of the cookies comes out clean.

instruction: How should I prepare my succulents for the winter in Canada?
demonstration: Sure, let’s walk through it! When the temperature drops below 5° C, they’ll probably be fine. You can also bring them inside before that happens, just leave them out of direct sunlight. As long as the temperature is lower than 10° C they should be fine. They might get a bit thirsty, but they can stand having their soil a bit drier than usual for a while. Make sure the soil isn’t super dry, though, since that can damage roots.  And if you have a small succulent plant, you should put it in a bigger container, so it’s still safe when the roots grow too big for the small container.

instruction: I want to learn French. How do you think I should go about it?
