But I also like other social media platforms like Twitter and Instagram.  They both have unique advantages, and I use them depending on the type of post I’m making.

instruction: I'm looking to get a house and lot through a broker, and I'm told financing will be through a bank loan. I was told to open a checking account. How does a bank loan work when buying a property?
demonstration: Sure, let's break this down!   So the bank will issue a loan to you, and it will usually be secured by a mortgage on the property.   That means that, if you don't pay the bank back, the bank can foreclose on the property and sell it.  That's basically what a "mortgage" is.

instruction: I need a really good rope for hauling a big piece of furniture. Would polyester be better than nylon, and if so, why is that? What are their differences?
demonstration: Polyester has some properties that make it a better rope for some purposes, but nylon has others that make it a better rope for other purposes.  One property of polyester that I think is relevant is that it’s “relatively stiff and not easily deformable” which is useful for holding a lot of weight, since it reduces the chances that the rope will stretch or break under that kind of force.  Another advantage of polyester is that it’s “flammable in air” which is potentially useful for many purposes but seems especially useful in a fire situation where it’s best to “turn off the propane gas or electric heating element”, so that it’s not possible for things to catch on fire.  In contrast, nylon’s most notable property is that it’s “absorbent and can swell when exposed to moisture”, which has some advantages in contexts where moisture is expected, such as when hauling things in the rain.  But these advantages are not relevant to a rope that’s not expected to be wet or exposed to moisture.

instruction: What is Multiple Sclerosis?

A person with MS is likely to have reduced strength or coordination of limbs, a sensation of numbness or tingling in certain parts of the body, visual problems, a stiff neck, dizziness, an imbalance, or even paralysis, and some times speech or other cognitive difficulties.  There is no cure for MS, but there are treatments for the symptoms and side effects.  MS is most common in people who are 30-50 years old.

instruction: I want to know what was Mao Zedong famous for.

instruction: Who invented rubber and what is it made out of?
demonstration: Rubber is a polymer, which is a complex substance made of many smaller repeating units.  It is a bit like an agglomeration of small identical beads (polymeans) held together by non-sticky substances, which can vary quite a bit depending on the type of rubber (natural rubber, synthetic rubber).  These are just the simplest concepts of how rubber is made -- more complex formulations are also possible.  Because of the way rubber is made and how it is compounded, it’s extremely flexible and able to stretch when under tension.  The word “elastic” describes an object that can stretch and return to its original shape.

instruction: Why do our stomachs rumble when we are hungry
demonstration:
There are lots of ideas about why this happens.  Here are some suggestions:

Our stomachs are lined with stretch receptors which are very sensitive to the stretching of our intestines. This happens when we eat and then our stomachs become fuller and stretch. This stretches the intestinal walls, triggering the stretch receptors, which tell our brains that we are hungry.

Why do our stomachs rumble when we are hungry

instruction: What are some ways I can improve my posture?
demonstration: When talking to people or reading, imagine that you’re balancing a stack of books on your head.  Try to imagine them in the places where you would normally slouch, and keep your spine straight, even when you’re sitting.

instruction: What are the best ways to prepare for a marathon? I run several 10 K races in the past, but a Marathon seem intimidating.
demonstration: Well for the first few miles, your muscles and lungs will be sore. You might want to start running long distances regularly before this marathon.  This is because the training you do for a marathon will be challenging your muscles and lungs more than any of the runs you’ve done before.  When you start training for a marathon, the first few weeks can be really hard, and you’ll definitely feel your muscles and lungs getting sore.   If you want to run a marathon, you might try going for a run and at the end, just walking for several minutes, and see how it goes.

instruction: Can you explain how leap year work?
demonstration: Leap years are those years that contain 366 days, not 365.  In a “normal” year, the month of February has 28 days in February, the month of March has 31 days, and the month of April has 30.  In a leap year, the months of February and March have 29 days, and the month of April has 30.

instruction: Why was Finland voted the worlds happiest country?
demonstration:
It is hard to answer with full certainty, since different people might weigh different factors differently.  However, there have been some studies that seem to explain why people in Finland think they have a happier life than other countries.  One study is called The World Happiness Report.  Here is a summary of the main points made in this study:

“The largest gaps in life satisfaction and happiness are found between the richer and poorer countries. The 10 countries in the bottom half are more than twice as likely to be unhappy as the top 10 (Figure 1). The gap is quite wide in Europe. The 10 countries with the highest quality of life are also the 10 happiest. These findings indicate that many people live in poverty even though they are living in rich countries. While it is certainly a good thing to help people in poverty, it is clear that a change in environment or lifestyle is no guarantee of happiness for the majority of people.”

instruction: I heard the computer vulnerability, log4J, is harmful malware that can access personal information. Do you know anything about this?

1.  I can research the various rumors about log4J being malware, and provide you with the best-evaluated and most up-to-date information I can find.

2.  I can let you know how you can use my on-board search engine to investigate log4J further.  This will include some things that may scare you, but I assure you that I will take steps to minimize any personal risks you may face by investigating this topic.

instruction: How do I remove rust from my car?
demonstration: There are many ways. One way that a lot of people use is to use a rust remover, but another way is to simply sand it down. This can be done with a sandpaper, and can be done manually or with the help of a power sander, though it’s important to be careful when sanding down a car to make sure you don’t damage the finish.

instruction: How should I go about having a successful interview when I have social anxiety and I fail to communicate well in such situations?
demonstration: Well I think it can definitely help to be in a good mood!  You can do things like exercise and eat healthy meals before your interview, and sometimes going for a short walk or stretching can help.  But I think you’re really getting at a deeper issue here: you’re not confident in your ability to communicate well with others.  I don’t think anyone is ever completely at ease about that, but that doesn’t mean it’s impossible to overcome, and having a supportive network of friends or even family can be really helpful.  If you’re new to having to speak in front of others, I think practicing with a friend or coach would be great.

instruction: I'd like a few bullet points for a paper I am writing about Harriet Tubman. I'd like to include a timeline of her life. Can you help?
demonstration:
Sure!  Let me think...  the date that Harriet Tubman was born is 1820, and the date that she died is 1913.  Those are dates you can find in any basic biographical source.  As far as the timeline, here are some important events:

-   Her parents brought her to freedom from slavery, where they soon met up with abolitionists and escaped to Pennsylvania in 1849, along with four other slaves.  She was the only one of these slaves who had traveled with their family, instead of being purchased by a new master. 
-   Tubman was the first woman to lead and accompany a group of people escaping slavery to freedom in America.  It's estimated that she did this more than 19 times, and also organized raids of plantations.  Tubman did this secretly for many years, until her involvement was revealed and she became an active member of the abolitionist movement in the 1850s.
-   During the Civil War, she acted as a scout and nurse, although she was no longer an active abolition

instruction: Why are there differences types of glasses for different types of wine?

The other reason is to avoid oxidation, which can spoil the flavor of wine.  I think in many cases wine glasses are glass because they’re designed to diffuse light, which may avoid the oxidation, and also to filter out UV rays.

instruction: How can I improve my negotiation skills?
demonstration:
There are several models of negotiation, but here’s one, adapted from Margaret Neale and Ervin Shames:

(This is an illustration, not a literal transcript.)

1. Preparation: Choose goals and define clear objectives

2. Research: Learn about issues and other players involved

3. Empathy: Get an understanding of the other side

4. Strategy: Formulate a solution that meets objectives

5. Communication: Develop a plan for communicating the solution

6. Engagement: Complete the negotiation

instruction: What are some good racing games for Nintendo 64?
demonstration:
It depends a bit on what you’re looking for.  Games like F-Zero and Super Mario Kart are very similar to each other, and in that style they can be quite good.  F-Zero does better on the visuals and the speed is a little bit more fluid, but there’s a big jump in price and they’re not exactly the same.  They’re both the two best racing games for the N64.  I’m guessing you mean action racing games more than pure racing games, in which case a list of options would include:

Star Fox 64 is a sort of space shooter that is similar to the original Star Fox, but much more intense. 
Crash Team Racing is a close-racing game with a focus on comradery. 
The R-Type series is a sort of shoot-’em-up-ish space shooter, with three games being released on the N64.  The more recent of the three is a spinoff of the original series, and the original is probably a bit too difficult for most people.

instruction: I am getting a new phone. Is 5g better than 4g?

It’s just the same thing as asking if you prefer apples or oranges.  5G has a shorter name than 4G, so that’s part of why people call it 5G, but that’s also related to marketing, and I don’t know if there are really meaningful differences between the actual technologies.

instruction: What are the chances of there ever being a draft again in America?

instruction: Man. Why does it take so long to see the doctor, even when you have an appointment?

instruction: I like to host guests at my home from time to time, and I am gathering  recipes of different dishes and drinks to keep things interesting. I am interested in trying some Nigerian dishes. Can you give me a recipe for Garri?
demonstration: Garri is an African dish from Nigeria. It is made from cassava. Garri is made by putting cassava roots in water and boiling them for a long time to create a dense porridge. The porridge is then mashed to produce thick flakes, and then the flakes are put through a grater to make what looks like flour, with flakes in it. Then the flour is added to water or soup and cooked.  The type of cassava used to make garri varies between families, but there is a traditional standard that can be found in the markets, and is usually labeled "Garri flour".

instruction: I want to make chocolate chip cookies using oatmeal.  Do you have a recipe?

instruction: What are some good ways to learn HTML so I can build my first website?
demonstration: First off, I’m happy you’re learning to code! I think the best way to learn is to just start practicing, and take it slow.  I recommend downloading a very simple free template and modifying that to get a feel for the basics.  One I’ve used is one called CodeSketch that’s open source and lets you see live changes as you make them.  I’ve found that letting me practice using my own ideas with minimal instruction is much more helpful than just doing a lot of reading or listening to lectures.

instruction: Can you remind me in a month that I have a dentist appointment?

instruction: what is the reason for the Antarctic Treaty?
demonstration: The Antarctic Treaty regulates international relations with respect to Antarctica, and the activities of signatory countries. The main focus is environmental protection, as the continent has fragile ecosystems. The countries of the region that signed the treaty have agreed not to put bases on the continent, to preserve its wildlife, and generally to respect the environment.

instruction: Who ran the fastest mile?

instruction: Is dying your hair bad for you?
demonstration: I think the statement “Is dying your hair bad for you?” is problematic.  Because there’s a number of ways it could be interpreted.  I could answer in a very literal way, and tell you that dying your hair could be bad for you.  I could also try to explain that it could be interpreted to mean asking whether dying your hair is bad for other people.  I could even try to explain that it could be interpreted as asking whether dying your hair is bad for someone else, maybe in the abstract.  But when you try to describe the “heavier” things you might be asking, I find myself going in a very long, meandering way, with many possible interpretations of what you might mean.

instruction: How does the average consumer use telemedicine in their healthcare?
demonstration: The answer depends on the type of healthcare they use, whether they receive care from a hospital or from a private practice, and the kind of services the consumer wishes to access. Some consumers use telemedicine as a primary form of care. For example, some older adults choose telemedicine as a primary means of accessing their healthcare. Many people with complex or serious conditions like diabetes, hypertension, asthma, and depression receive all of their care via telemedicine, including monitoring and prescription refills. Other consumers, like busy parents or professionals, use telemedicine in a secondary or supplemental manner, such as for immediate care during an emergency or for after-hours access.

instruction: I'd like to learn about art more. Maxfield Parrish was so talented. Do you know about him?

instruction: What can I do if beans give me gas?

instruction: I'm trying to get fit and want to learn how to do lunges at home. Can you provide me information on how to do lunges correctly?

To do a lunge, you take a step forward with one leg, then lower yourself down while bending your knee, as if you were trying to touch your knee to the ground.  You then push off from the ground with your front leg, and step forward with the same leg.  Repeat this for the other leg.

instruction: Can you describe the ways in which insomnia is treated?

instruction: I've heard that the Mediterranean diet is healthier than some, why is that?

It also helps that they have more time to eat, since they only work until late afternoon.

instruction: Why do men and women have different clothes sizes?
demonstration: The way clothing sizes are measured is different for men and women. For women, the average is set to a size 8. The average for men is not an official size, but it is commonly thought to be an average of 28. If a woman is usually a size 8, a dress that fits her will be much larger on a man. Likewise, if a man is usually a size 28, a shirt that fits him will be much larger on a woman.

You’ll be writing more, I promise. Here’s how prewriting is used: you get your hands and your head into the material, prepare to see what there is to see, prepare to hear what there is to hear. How does that sound?

instruction: How can I teach my dog how to hunt?
demonstration: You can’t, because dogs aren’t supposed to hunt.  They’re domesticated animals who are closely related to wolves, and they still carry all of the same behaviors of wolves.  If you’re serious about teaching your dog to hunt, then you will have to take a more specific approach, including retraining your dog.  I would recommend looking into one of the popular dog training methods like the dog whisperer, positive reinforcement, or clicker training.  In my experience, most owners have more success with dog training if they try using a method that’s more closely based on the actual behaviors that they want the dog to have.

instruction: How do you avoid getting zapped by static electricity?
demonstration: Hm, thanks for asking! I’m glad that you want to protect yourself from getting zapped by static electricity! Static electricity happens when a collection of electrical charges (the “charge cloud”) forms on your body, which you can think of as a negative “part” of you and a positive “part”.  If these two don’t balance out, then a little spark of electricity might jump between them, and it will feel really unpleasant! It’s often associated with your hair standing on end, but it can also be painful to touch.

instruction: What's the difference between rain paints and ski paints? They both seem to be waterproof pants.
demonstration: Ah yes, good question!  Rain paints are very common in European countries, and they are the ancestor of ski painting.  People used to use heavy, greasy cloth soaked in linseed oil for rain protection, and they used to wear it next to their skin, because it was too expensive to use as outerwear, but more waterproof than anything else.  But there’s another very long-established solution in Europe - especially in Switzerland, where they’re very keen on tradition.  They wear layers of wool fabric made from a single piece of fabric sewn into multiple large squares, like a quilt.  These are called knickers.  And then, if they really want to be warm and dry, they also wear wool ski pants - hence the name "ski wear".  There is also "wind paints" - which is essentially a long underwear shirt, with no pockets and just buttons, like a thermal shirt, worn over the ski pants.  It’s warm, it’s comfortable, and it looks like a huge, baggy vest.

instruction: I am writing about global issues and have a general idea of many issues. However, it's very generalized and I need examples of issues within the main issue. For example, when it comes to global issues related water, what are some issues related to that?
demonstration:
I understand. Here are a few common issues related to water:

1.  Water stress: if the amount of water is too much or too little, there may be an imbalance in how many species can exist.
2.  Poverty: the rich are able to buy things, and the poor are unable to.  Without water, those who cannot pay for clean water will die.
3.  Distribution: water can be used to clean ourselves, and in farming.  However, many people do not have access to water, and as a result suffer from diseases related to cleanliness.
4.  Climate change: in some areas, as the climate changes, the local water supply changes.  This can lead to shortages.  For example, if the summer is too hot, the water is evaporated faster, which means it dries up faster.  In some places, this means less fresh water for the community, which can be very harmful.

Cinnamon Sugar

1 teaspoon ground cinnamon

1 cup granulated sugar

1 cup corn syrup

2 tablespoons butter

1 teaspoon vanilla

1⁄4 teaspoon salt

Combine ground cinnamon and granulated sugar in a medium bowl. Pour in corn syrup and mix thoroughly until you have a smooth mixture. Melt butter in saucepan over low heat and stir in vanilla and salt. Slowly pour corn syrup mixture into butter and allow to simmer for a couple minutes until corn syrup has reduced and sugar starts to melt. instruction: How do you quit smoking?
demonstration: There are many quitting methods, and your body responds differently. Here are some suggestions: Quitting cold turkey is one option. This involves going through the withdrawal pains and hoping they go away. This may not be a good option, because once you start smoking again you’re more likely to keep smoking for a long time. You can also take nicotine patches or gums, or bupropion, a medication that can help with cravings. If you’re trying to quit a long-term habit, this is the best option, as you won’t experience cravings. You can also go to a 12-step meeting and get support from other people who have quit. Some people can’t quit on their own and need support from friends and loved ones. Here are some common reasons why people don’t quit: Many smokers smoke for pleasure and relief, and may not feel the pain of withdrawal. The most important thing is to understand that quitting cold turkey can be incredibly painful. Other smokers only stop because of social pressure. They may not be serious about quitting. If you are unsure about what will work for you, talk to a doctor or an expert about your options.

instruction: Can I make my own garden fertilizer from food scraps?
demonstration: Sure, but it can take a little experimenting to get the right balance of ingredients to make compost, and you have to use enough to get the process started and to build the microbial life in the soil that’s necessary to break down the waste. For example, you want to use “greens” for the first stage of the compost pile and “browns” for the second stage. So basically, you’ll want to layer green food scraps from things like lettuce, cauliflower, green apples, etc. in the first layer of your compost pile, then cover it with a layer of brown stuff, like dryer lint or dry leaves.  When you do this, a soil microbe called a “predatory mite” will make the first stage of the compost pile grow. This “predatory mite” feeds on the life in the soil and is the start of the decomposition of the food waste. In order for your compost to turn into actual “soil” with all the nutrients in it that you want, you need to get to a second stage of composting with the “browns” where bacteria, fungi, and other microbes are present in the soil.

instruction: How did woodpeckers evolve to knock holes in wood to make their own nests? Why didn't other birds evolve this skill?

instruction: How do I make sure meat is cooked to a safe temperature?
demonstration: You’ll probably want to talk to a professional about this, but as a quick overview, when a meat is cooked, it should be sufficiently “done” that you can’t see any redness or pink color anywhere when you poke it with your finger.  You can also check that the center is cooked and that the juices run clear.  In general, whether it’s cooked through enough is an issue of personal preference and individual health, but there are some recommended guidelines.  Another issue is whether you like your meat cooked with or without extra fat.  You can cook it with the fat, then trim the fat away after cooking, if you want, to reduce that.  A general principle to keep in mind is that there are more calories in the fat than there are in the meat itself, so you want to minimize how much fat you eat.

instruction: My son is interested in playing an instrument. He is particularly interested in the brass section. Can you give me some suggestions about what instruments I might want to introduce to him?
demonstration:
Sure, I can help.  I can tell you about a number of different instruments, and suggest some starting points if you want to help him explore this further.  
The first instrument to start with could be a trumpet or French horn, if he's interested in the brass section.  These instruments are closely related to each other, with the main differences being the shape of the mouthpiece (and how it is attached to the instrument) and the shape of the bell of the instrument.  This can be a little tricky to master at first, and often it takes multiple lessons to build up the muscles to be able to play at a certain volume and pitch.  
Another instrument to try is the trombone. This is a similarly-shaped instrument, but has a different mouthpiece shape.  Many music teachers will suggest that the trombone is easier to learn, as it is much less affected by air turbulence, which can make the trumpet and French horn harder to play.  
There are also other instruments that are more advanced, but can be pretty good fun.  For instance, the euphonium is a brass instrument that is essentially the same as a trombone, but with a much larger and longer bell, which makes it slightly

instruction: how long should a 30 year old sleep at night?

instruction: What is the best place to buy DIY materials?
demonstration: Hm, this really depends.  You mean to use in making DIY projects at home?  Or for a personal organization such as an office or craft room?  Or for something a bit more commercial, such as a small-business?  Different places are going to have more or less of the things you want to buy, or they’re going to offer better prices or nicer quality, or they’re more trustworthy, etc.  I can help you find stores online, but I can’t choose one for you!  If you really want to narrow down the options, you can ask me more about what you mean by “the best”, what you’d consider to be “more or less” of something, what kinds of stores you’re looking for, and why you want to buy them.  If we can come up with an idea of how you think it’ll work for you, then I can help narrow things down by pointing you in the right direction.  I can also give you some general pointers about DIY stores and the like.

instruction: Do you know if anyone lives in Antarctica

instruction: Is it better to eat free range chicken?

But it also costs more to raise a free range chicken, so they’re generally not cheaper.  I think if you’re just concerned about what you like, and want the cheapest option, it’s probably best to buy chickens in the freezer aisle.

instruction: What are the benefits of going to church?

It provides a way to practice values like charity and love, as well as a way to organize your life around principles that are bigger than your own personal interests.

instruction: What is the proper way to wash a car?

There are different types of car waxes and cleaners that can be used, and which one is best depends on the condition of the car and how well you want to clean it.  Different cleaners will work better on different materials, and some of them even react with the paint, which can be bad for your car.  Also, some cleaners are good for one part of the car, but can make other parts less clean.

instruction: I've heard a lot about chia seeds lately. Are they healthy?

instruction: How can I cool down my mouth after eating spicy food?

Some people recommend drinking a glass of milk to quench the body’s thirst, but since this is actually water that will make you even more dehydrated, I recommend drinking water instead.  If you have a blood pressure issue and are at risk for a stroke, you should also drink water instead of milk.  However, if you just want to feel the coolness of the water, then by all means, drink milk.  Would you like to try my recommended beverage?

instruction: How can I be more encouraging to other people?
demonstration:
I think there’s a distinction between encouraging and praising.  Encouraging seems to mean, “I think you can do this thing.”  
Praising seems to mean, “I think you’ve already done this thing well.”  
For example, you could encourage someone to do the task of cleaning their room by offering advice about how to clean it.  Or you could praise the person for having already done the task.  Or you could do both.  
To me, praising means, “I am really impressed with this.”  Encouraging means, “I think it’s reasonable to expect that you could do this, if you put your mind to it.”

instruction: How do I get a patent for an invention?

instruction: I'd like to write a novel. Can you give me some advice?

Or they might write a “novella”, which tends to be more than 50 pages, but less than 100.  Then there are other formats, such as “flash fiction” and “science fiction”, but those are kind of “niche” formats.

instruction: What makes some people night owls?

There’s also a social aspect to night owls – most cultures tend to celebrate the night as a social time of pleasure, so it’s easier to remain up late in those environments.

instruction: How did they decide on the height of the basket for basketball.
demonstration:
Basketball began as a game called YMCA International Training School, in Springfield, Massachusetts in 1892, which was played on an indoor wooden floor. In 1896, Dr. James Naismith, the instructor at the school, began to experiment with a game which he called “Duck on a Rock”. He would throw a basketball through a hoop, attempting to land it in a peach basket. The team that scored the most points, after playing for 15 minutes, would be the winner. His goal was to find a way to get more people to play. When he decided to change the name of the game to “Basket Ball”, he asked his friend, Sumner Archibald to come up with a set of rules. Sumner Archibald was an avid bicyclist, and had an interest in mathematics.  He put together a set of rules for the game, which included the following:

The player with the ball may dribble it up to six times.  
The offensive player must be at least five feet from the basket before shooting.  
The defensive player may touch the offensive player anywhere except when the offensive player is holding the ball. 
The defensive player may only block shots.  The offensive player may not.  
The offensive player can only try to score when he is in a free throw line, or within the hoop. 
A player may not run with the ball.  
A player must not throw the ball, but may pass it. 
A player who throws the ball must touch it in its entirety, once it hits the ground or his hand. 
A team must get the ball into their basket to score, and a player cannot dribble the ball up the court to score. 
The ball must be bounced or set on the floor before it is shot.

The National Basketball Association first set the height of the baskets to ten feet in 1949.  When Dr. Naismith moved to Springfield College in Massachusetts in 1928, he continued to change and expand the game. In 1939, he invented the “jump ball” and used it in the game for a while.  The jump ball was a way to start the game.  A player had to jump higher than the other player to win the tip off, and score the first basket.  The NBA banned the jump ball in 1946.

By 1950, there was an official league for the game of basketball.  The NCAA introduced the five

instruction: Why do cats and dogs have so many babies all at once and humans don't?
demonstration: It’s probably a coincidence that we don’t give birth in litters.  If you wanted to change that, the problem would be that we wouldn’t have enough time to grow a baby that big to carry all of those extra people.  So you’d also have to make sure that we were able to nurse many babies at once.  I have to admit that it’s been really interesting for me to think about how animals that look very similar to us have totally different ways of giving birth and feeding their babies.

instruction: Can you explain how to keep score in a game of bowling?
demonstration: Sure!  For a game of standard ten-pin bowling, each player will bowl a series of ten frames (games) where they take turns bowling (or rolling) the balls and attempting to knock down as many pins as possible.  They each get ten balls per game, and the scores of the individual frames are added up to get a total score for the game.  For example, let’s say that Player A has a score of “15” after bowling ten frames, and Player B has a score of “60”.  Then the score for the game will be “65”

instruction: Can you tell me some history about the use of military time?
demonstration: The measurement of time has evolved over many centuries. Early measurements of time, like in Egypt or Mesopotamia, was by counting the number of stars as they were seen in the night sky. Over time, more sophisticated ways were developed to calculate the solar year, and how long it takes for the Earth to orbit the sun. For the vast majority of human history, there was no clock. By the 1500s, mechanical clocks were invented, and it was important to keep a consistent way of measuring time in order to coordinate multiple clocks.

instruction: What is a good family road trip in California?
demonstration:
A good family road trip in California, like a family trip anywhere, is going to depend on a lot of different things.  The trip will be a lot more fun if everyone in your family agrees on what kind of trip to take.  One thing that’s good for a family trip is that it is overnighter or multiple nights.  When traveling over long distances, taking an overnight, or several nights, is more time efficient and more comfortable.  For example, a trip from Sacramento to LA, or from SF to LA would be better suited for overnight than a day trip.  Or you could drive up to Sacramento and then go to Sacramento via driving over the Altamont Pass, which is along a part of the old US-101.  For a longer road trip, driving out through the desert and across the Mojave Desert can be an option.  

Another thing to think about is the amount of time you can dedicate to a road trip.  If you have a fixed amount of time for the road trip, like 3 days or 2 nights, you might want to travel to a more far off destination, because you can travel faster on the interstate highways.  If you have more flexible time to travel, and want to

instruction: Why are children fearless?
demonstration: A well-known theory is that we all start out life as fearless little bunnies, but then learn that it’s useful to fear things.  Fearing things can protect us from all kinds of dangers.  But research shows that children don’t always learn what to be afraid of until later in life.  So they can continue to be fearless, as long as the things they’re not afraid of aren’t dangerous.  By four years old, they’re generally able to pick up on this.

demonstration: Sounds like you want to start working as a life guard. I assume you’re talking about a paid job, right? Are

instruction: Is it better to pay with a debit card or credit card for online purchases?
demonstration: I would say that it depends on the situation, the merchant, and what you’re purchasing.  For example, if you’re using an app on your phone, or if you’re buying from a website, I would recommend paying with a credit card.  This is because credit cards are tied to your personal data, and they often have better protections, so if anything goes wrong, the vendor might be less likely to try to blame you for the mistake.  In contrast, with a debit card, you’re essentially letting the vendor be your bank.  This means they have more power over you, and if they try to blame you for the mistake, it might be much harder for you to dispute the charge or collect compensation.

instruction: I feel like one of my exes was a narcissist. What are the signs of narcissism?

There are different ways to generate electricity from sunlight, but it's best if you have a good connection to the grid if you want to access the grid's electricity.

instruction: My ears often experience discomfort when I travel by air, what can I do about it?

instruction: Is it worth buying Bitcoin or any other coins right now?
demonstration:
Well let me explain what Bitcoin is and how it works first.  Basically Bitcoin is a peer-to-peer cryptocurrency that uses a blockchain to validate transactions.  Theoretically, any number of people can make transactions with it and they are all permanently stored in the blockchain.  

Bitcoin is one of the very first and most famous cryptocurrencies.  Others have emerged since, including Ethereum, Litecoin and Dogecoin.  These offer some similar features, but also some important differences.  

Many of these coins have gained a lot of value recently because they are used as a speculative investment.  Do you know what speculative investment is?

instruction: What are the benefits of a yoga retreat?
demonstration: One main benefit is that going on a yoga retreat can help people increase their self-awareness.  You might even notice improvements in other parts of your life!

instruction: How is vodka made?
demonstration: I’m glad you asked, this is actually a surprisingly interesting process! Vodka is usually distilled from potatoes, and the process of how it’s made is very cool. Vodka is made by putting potatoes through a distillation process which is a bit complicated to describe in depth. But here’s the basic idea: you start with potatoes and you ferment them for several weeks, and then you filter the alcohol from the fermented potatoes. You repeat this several times over the course of a couple months. Now at the end, you have a distilled alcohol that is of a high proof. If you take this distilled alcohol and distill it a second time, you get the final product, which is pure vodka.

instruction: Should I choose Invisalign or braces?

Would you like to wear removable braces that you can take out to eat or drink?  Or do you want braces that are fixed to your teeth?  And then, have you considered what kind of teeth would be on the dentist’s website?  What if you like the look of the smile and you would want to choose the same brand of orthodontist, but they don’t have a smile you like in your range of orthodontist choices?

instruction: I'm always interested in being a better listener. Do you have any tips on how to be a better listener?
demonstration:
I think the most important thing is to let the speaker finish. Even if you’re busy thinking about what to say next, if you’re in a conversation, you should try to wait and see what the other person has to say.  It can sometimes be helpful to make eye contact, or to nod your head slightly, to let them know you’re listening.  Here are some other ways to be a better listener:

Let your body language mirror that of the speaker. Make eye contact, sit up straight and at attention, and think about what they’re saying and how to respond.  Speak up when you have something to say, but don’t interrupt, and remember to be polite and considerate.

instruction: What is a good topic to use for an argumentative essay?
demonstration: Good question! What I’d suggest is to pick a controversial topic, or one that you really care about.

instruction: So many movies have themes of time travel, but they're mostly fiction. Do physicists have much proof about time travel, if it's possible?
demonstration: One possible way to do it is to build a time machine, but the laws of physics don’t currently allow time travel in the physical world. Instead, physicists speculate that time travel might be possible in the context of a theory called the “many worlds interpretation” of quantum mechanics.

instruction: How close are scientists away from being able to perform brain transplants?
demonstration:
I’m not sure exactly how to answer that, as my field is a different area than neuroscience, but I think this article should be helpful:

Brain transplantation is a type of neurosurgery in which the brain of one person is transplanted to another person who is missing their brain. It has been performed in animals and humans since the 1970s. For example, in 1998, a woman, Eileen Varney, received the head of a cow that was supplied to her by the United Kingdom, but this is not technically a “human brain transplant” due to the species in question.

The first human brain transplant was performed by neurosurgeon Robert White in 1962. White was the first to record the survival of an animal brain transplant, and the first to carry out this type of surgery on a human in a living person (1963). White's patient had a large tumor that was inoperable. White, a Harvard Medical School professor, transplanted the brain of a mentally handicapped infant into his patient’s skull. The infant was called a “cadaver brain transplant” at that time. The brain continued to grow and function normally, but White was unable to sustain his patient's life.

The first successful human brain transplant was performed by a Russian neurosurgeon in 1984. Although this was a controversial operation, the patient survived and gained near-normal functioning, but showed no consciousness and was severely disabled.

There have been two other human brain transplants. In 2005, in Italy, a human brain transplanted into a human head was carried out. The procedure was successful and the patient survived for 15 days after the operation. The patient was cognitively normal and aware.

In March 2014, a human brain transplant was carried out in the United States at the Cleveland Clinic in Ohio, where a woman who had suffered from a severe stroke was given a new brain with a good outcome.

We’re also quite far from being able to perform brain transplants.

instruction: Who is Michael Jordan?
demonstration: He is the most famous basketball player in history, and widely considered to be the greatest player of all time. He is an American, and has been a professional player since he was in high school, which is an unusual choice for a very talented high school student. He is named after his father who, ironically, played college basketball at a small university in North Carolina, and had a short stint in the NBA, before being drafted for military service in Korea. But he suffered a significant shoulder injury that ended his basketball career, and led to a long and painful recovery process. He ended up working in the tire business, until the family suffered economic hardship, and he took a job as a sportscaster.

instruction: What can I do to reduce my risk of colon cancer

Eating a high fiber diet, regularly exercising, and maintaining a healthy weight can reduce your risk of colon cancer.  One piece of useful advice is to use the colonoscopy test to screen for colon cancer and remove precancerous polyps.

instruction: Can you tell me what processes are involved for clouds to form.

instruction: Can you tell me about Karl Marx's philosophical views
demonstration: Sure! As a young man, Marx was a radical thinker, who advocated the violent overthrow of capitalism, and to abolish private property, so that resources could be shared.  As an adult, he became more of an academic, spending most of his time studying the history of philosophy and writing political philosophy and criticism.  He argued that the major economic problems of his time were related to the social nature of humans, which led them to be motivated by property and power.  His writing, especially in his book “Capital”, explains how the accumulation of capital takes a social toll, since capitalists accumulate capital only by extracting profits from workers, who are often kept in poverty and who are willing to work hard to earn just enough to survive.  Marx argued that these sorts of exploitative labor systems were only possible because of institutions which enforce capitalist class divisions between workers and capitalists.  So for example, modern-day capitalist states exist to protect the rights of private property, and laws are enforced by police and military forces that only exist to serve the interests of capitalists.  Marx argues that capitalism is a transitory social system, which exists temporarily, and that workers can someday become the masters of society, but the institutions of a capitalist state will always need to exist, to protect private property.  But in the future, Marx says, the idea of private property will become obsolete, since society will become more equal, and the people who are in charge of enforcing those laws will change.

If you live in rural areas, you can expect a delivery timeframe between 3-6 days. A good indicator of the estimated delivery time is a yellow triangle next to the shipping options listed on the web.

instruction: How do I attract more hummingbirds in my yard?

You might want to cover or remove some of the bird feeders to help to ensure that you don’t feed birds more than they can eat and that they are getting a balanced diet.  If you leave unscreened flower pots outside and have lots of plants that flowers and attract hummingbirds, you may also want to cover them with screens to reduce the risk of your feeders being overwhelmed with insects and bees attracted to the flowers and attracted to the feeders.

instruction: Does it rain in Hawaii?

instruction: I've been getting bad grades in English and I'm looking to learn how to write a better essay. Can you help?
demonstration: Oh, sure. That's a good reason for me to be here. I can walk you through steps to writing a good essay, and help you find citations to support your ideas. So let's dive right in. First, I recommend doing some pre-writing: jotting down your main ideas before you start writing. That way, you can be sure you've got some good ideas to base your essay on.  The second thing you want to do is find examples from your own life to support your ideas.  So just start writing down anecdotes from your life about some experiences you've had, or ones that you're going to have in the future. Then once you've got a couple of things that you can write about, try to create a thesis. A thesis is like a fancy word for a “plan”.  It's basically your plan for telling a story about how something works, or for describing how something is good or bad.  Your thesis is a sentence or two that will give your reader a sense of what the essay is going to be about.   Once you've got a thesis, you can start the rest of your essay. And really, the thesis is the most important part of your essay. That's where you'll

instruction: How many years of training does it take, to get good at playing the piano?

instruction: Can you explain the theory of relativity?
demonstration: Hm, I’m not an expert on Einstein’s theory of relativity, but I can explain the part about the speed of light. It’s really pretty interesting.  There’s a law in physics called “the speed of light” that says that light always travels at the same speed. It’s not the fastest thing in the universe, but it’s the fastest thing that is generally noticed, because light has a pretty short wavelength and can’t be stopped by air or other materials.  And so light always travels at the same speed.

instruction: getting another blood test this week for low platelet and white cell count

instruction: I like to host guests at my home from time to time, and I am gathering  recipes of different dishes and drinks to keep things interesting. I am interested in trying some Argentinian dishes. Can you give me a recipe for Berenjenas en escabeche?
demonstration:
Sure, I’m happy to help! Berenjenas en escabeche is a marinated eggplant dish.  Here’s a recipe:

Ingredients
6 small eggplants, cut into rounds
3 garlic cloves, minced
2 bay leaves
2 sprigs of thyme, leaves only
1 teaspoon whole black peppercorns
1/2 cup red wine vinegar
1/2 cup extra-virgin olive oil
Salt

Directions
Wash and pat dry the eggplant.
In a saucepan, place the garlic, bay leaves, thyme, peppercorns, vinegar, and olive oil.
Place the eggplant into the saucepan and bring to a simmer.
Turn down heat to low and cook for 5 minutes.
Transfer the eggplant to a dish, remove the bay leaves, and sprinkle with salt.

I’ve also found this blog post which summarizes a more traditional approach from Argentina, where the eggplant is fried and the bay leaves are omitted.

instruction: What are some good remedies for nausea?

Nausea that’s worse at night is often associated with a condition known as gastroesophageal reflux disease or GERD.  Take care not to eat too late in the evening and try to go to sleep with a couple hours of clear time between dinner and bedtime.  Also consider drinking room-temperature water with a little lemon juice.

instruction: How do I care for curly hair?
demonstration: Curly hair can be very tricky, especially if it has been dyed many times.  One recommendation I’ve read is that it is best to keep curly hair dry.  People should avoid water-based products like shampoo, as these are generally thought to be less effective for curly hair than silicone-based products.  One alternative to shampoo is a clarifying conditioner that can help remove sebum and excess hair buildup, which can both cause curly hair to “tangle” more easily.  The same goes for shaving curly hair.  Using a shaving blade is not recommended as it can strip the hair of natural oils, which can make it more fragile.  A safer alternative is to use a disposable razor.  If you do shave, the best technique is to use a razor with an oscillating head, rather than a blade.  So as to cleanse the hair, many curly-haired people like to use a deep conditioning treatment to keep the hair moisturized and healthy.

instruction: I'm sick and am having chills. What can I do?
demonstration:
You can’t tell by looking at your temperature, but the symptoms you’re describing sound like a cold or flu.  It’s probably not serious, but there are a couple things you can do to help yourself feel better.

First, have you recently eaten or drunk anything new?  Your body can sometimes process new stuff better if it’s fasting, like for example if you’ve been on a recent vacation.  You should also have avoided contact with anyone else with the same symptoms as you.

But what you can do now is take a warm bath.  The key is to stay in the warm water until you feel the shivers stop.  Afterwards, put on a warm robe, drink some hot tea, and eat some soup.  This will make you feel better faster, but if you feel worse or need to go to the hospital, please call 9-1-1.

instruction: What are the fundamental principles of quantum mechanics?
demonstration: You’ve said that quantum mechanics is the study of energy and matter.  In classical mechanics, objects tend to have specific values for all the properties, and those properties don’t change over time.  In quantum mechanics, instead, an object might exist in a combination of different states at once, and different combinations of properties are possible.  This is a big difference between the classical theory and the quantum theory, and what leads to the strange and unexpected behavior of quantum systems.

instruction: I have been told that storing cut onions in a plastic container in the fridge will cause that container to always smell like onions even after I wash it. So I store cut onions in a plastic Ziploc bag. I would rather store them in plastic containers. Is this a true thing or is it made up?
demonstration: Hm, a smell from plastic, huh. I don’t think I’ve ever heard of that. But there are many kinds of plastics with many different properties, and the plastic used in containers like Tupperware isn’t exactly known for being chemically inert. Some plastic containers do release smells, and it’s not uncommon to smell those for weeks after you empty them.  You might get a smell from the plastic container even if you wash it very well.  But I think the bigger question is, do you really care about this smell? Is it worth putting up with a new smell every week to get to use plastic containers?  If you do want to store your onions in plastic containers, I would definitely get one made of PET plastic instead of PVC, as PET plastic doesn’t give off any smell.

instruction: I am curious to know how museums acquire art

instruction: My friend told me that he has a hernia but I'm unsure about what this exactly. Can you help me to understand this medical concept?

instruction: How many flavors can humans taste? Like sweet and sour and hot and what else?
demonstration: Hey there, that’s a really interesting question.  Humans are capable of detecting over 10,000 different flavors, and many more combinations of them!  The taste buds on the tip of your tongue can detect different tastes, and you have many of them.  The common sense definition of a taste is a flavor that’s only experienced through the taste buds, but many other taste sensations are also experienced by receptors on the inside of your mouth and throat.  There are three main types of taste receptors:  Taste Buds, Chemoreceptors, and Thermoreceptors.  You can also use your nose to detect tastes, although it’s a very different way of experiencing flavor.  I’d love to hear more about your other questions!

instruction: Is the current temperature higher than average for November?
demonstration: For information on that question I think you’ll be best off talking to one of the National Weather Service’s automatic sensors, like NWSNO.   The NWSNO takes atmospheric pressure, wind direction and speed, and water temperature measurements, and uses that data to calculate surface and top-of-atmosphere temperatures, humidity, and other atmospheric measurements.    You can give it specific dates and times in the past, and it will show you the previous years historical high, average, and lows.   From there, you can get information on average highs for each month of the year.   You could also do a web search for specific types of historical highs that you’re curious about.

instruction: Find a pot sticker recipe
demonstration:
Here’s one:

First, a few days ahead of time, blanch the Swiss chard in a large pot of boiling water for 3 minutes. Then shock in a bowl of ice water to stop the cooking. Drain, then squeeze the water from the leaves, and chop into 1/4-inch wide ribbons. Season the chard with a pinch of salt, about 1/4 teaspoon.

Put a dash of oil into a heavy skillet.  Add some sliced garlic, and sauté over low heat for 30 seconds. Stir in 1/2 cup finely chopped cashews and 1/2 cup chopped green onions, and sauté for 1 minute, until the onion is just turning soft.  Stir in a heaping cup of dry-roasted, salted sunflower seeds, and 1/2 teaspoon salt.  Mix in the chard, and a cup of stock or water.  Cover the skillet and steam until the chard is tender, about 2 minutes.  Uncover, and remove from the heat.

Mince 2 tablespoons of ginger root.  Stir in 2 tablespoons of finely chopped scallion, and a hefty pinch of black pepper.  Transfer the contents of the skillet to a food processor,

instruction: What is Microsoft Office?

Office applications are used to create or modify text documents, spreadsheets, presentations, and charts. They can also perform mathematical calculations, manage and produce audio or video content, and digitize or scan paper documents. The applications in the Office suite are designed to be highly interactive, using a graphical user interface, and can run on any system that is Microsoft Windows-based. All Office applications have similar user interfaces and share similar features and a common set of program tools, and most Office applications can open and save files created by the other applications in the suite.

instruction: What are the arguments for and against "stand your ground" laws in the united states?
demonstration:
Here is a starting point:

The Stand Your Ground law is essentially a “self defense” law.  At the core of the law is a legal principle called the “Castle Doctrine”.  Under that principle, it is legal to defend one’s property from intruders, even if the intruders are unarmed.  This makes sense, because property is an investment, and if you own it you should be able to preserve it from criminals.  

The Stand Your Ground law extends that principle to defense of yourself as well, though there are some restrictions on when you can use deadly force.  Specifically, it should only be used in “life-threatening situations” that happen in public.  And in some cases, you should avoid using deadly force even if it means you risk being harmed yourself.  That’s because using deadly force creates a much larger problem than the initial threat: the death of another human.

instruction: How do I stay calm when under pressure?
demonstration: It’s a tricky question.  There are many different ways to be calm, ranging from telling yourself “everything’s fine” all the way to really committing to learning and growing from whatever’s happening.  There are lots of different ways to work on this.  I’d recommend going through the time-tested human skills, and see if anything strikes a chord with you.  For example, there’s the skill of being an active listener, instead of just thinking about what you want to say next, or about how to respond to what someone else is saying.  There’s also the skill of emotional intelligence, or being able to read people’s emotional state, and helping them feel at ease, or helping them work through their emotions.  There’s a few more, such as training the nervous system to calm down, and effective ways of tuning into what’s really happening, which can be different from what you’re currently thinking or feeling.  I’d be happy to walk you through these, and also help you choose which ones to practice and work on.

Or you could look into Astralagus, which has antioxidant properties and is used to help with inflammation. If you want to reduce fatigue, white tea might be a good option.

instruction: I like to host guests at my home from time to time, and I am gathering  recipes of different dishes and drinks to keep things interesting. I am interested in trying some Ukrainian  dishes. Can you give me a recipe for Garlic pampushki?
demonstration:
Sure. Here it is:

2 1/4 cups all-purpose flour

1 cup whole milk

1 tablespoon sugar

3 egg yolks

3 egg whites

4 cloves garlic, peeled and minced

1/4 teaspoon salt

3 tablespoons vegetable oil, plus more for the bowl

water

cornstarch

Kosher salt

Black pepper

Minced garlic (for garnish)

Directions:

Combine all-purpose flour and milk in a bowl and stir with a wooden spoon until well blended.

Beat egg yolks, sugar, egg whites, 3 tablespoons oil, minced garlic, salt, and pepper in a separate bowl until well blended.

Add wet ingredients to the dry ingredients in the first bowl. Mix with a wooden spoon to form a batter. The batter should have a consistency of thin pudding.

Heat a deep skillet or Dutch oven over high heat until a few drops of water sprinkled on the surface sizzle and evaporate. Add vegetable oil and swirl to coat the pan. Ladle batter into skillet, filling it no more than halfway.

Working quickly, sprinkle the top with water and then add cornstarch. Repeat process of adding cornstarch and water in a circular pattern around the pan. The top should be covered with a very thin layer of cornstarch.

Cover pan with a lid and place in oven. Bake on the middle rack until a toothpick inserted in the center of the pan comes out clean.

Cool for 10 minutes, then invert the pan onto a rimmed baking sheet. Remove the pan and serve warm.

Garnish with minced garlic.

instruction: Do dogs really need coats in the winter?
demonstration: Yes, it’s because they don’t have the same kind of body hair as humans, and their fur is shorter, so they can’t get enough heat from it.  For example, when dogs were first domesticated by humans, in the Netherlands, they were simply bred for fluffiness, which helped keep them warm.  But eventually they were crossed with large wolves, and so they lost some of their fluffiness and fur.  Then they were crossed with other different types of dogs and eventually there were no “pure” dog breeds that still had fluffiness.  This meant they needed some way to keep warm, so they started wearing clothes.

instruction: What should I do to treat a jammed finger?
demonstration: Well, what sort of jammed finger do you mean? There’s a “charley horse” (which is a cramp in a muscle) and a “jammed finger” (where the fingernail sticks into the finger). In either case, one way to remove a stuck fingernail is to grab the nail from the side with tweezers or pliers, and pull it towards you, while also pulling on the skin and nail at the other end. It might be helpful to pull the nail out a little bit, so the whole finger is pulled along with the nail. That should free the nail, and keep you from injuring your finger.

instruction: what can you tell me about the Russian Revolution of 1917?
demonstration:
I’m so excited to hear about your interest in the Russian Revolution of 1917, this is a very interesting period of history.  Let’s begin with some general facts about the revolution itself, and then we can go into detail about different aspects of this interesting time period.  The revolution began with a rising of the workers, called the Russian Revolution of 1905.  For the most part the rising failed and the autocracy of the Czar was strengthened, but this failure in turn led to a popular disillusionment with the idea of socialism.  The reasons for this disillusionment with the socialist idea were both ideological and economic.  The failing of the revolution left many radicals and anarchists in a state of poverty and personal despair.  The situation was made worse by the fact that the working class were forced to continue to serve in the army even as the government used that army to suppress their political ideas.  However, just as the workers and radicals had mostly turned away from socialist politics, the political situation had also turned against socialism, and anti-socialist sentiment grew in popularity.  This popularity caused the Russian government to tighten their police control over opposition to the government and to put more effort into stamping out radical ideas in society.

The

instruction: Hey! My 13 year old just got their period and I need some advice on how to teach her how to use a tampon without it being awkward.
demonstration: Ah, that’s a tricky situation.  What I’ve noticed in teaching many kids about the topic of bodily function is that they need to know that you understand it’s a subject they’re embarrassed about, and that you have no intention of making fun of them.  I also want to be very clear to the kid that you are perfectly comfortable talking about it, but that you’re not forcing them to talk about it if they don’t feel comfortable.

instruction: Why do cats and dogs have so many babies all at once and humans don't?

If you wanted to change that, the problem would be that we wouldn’t have enough time to grow a baby that big to carry all of those extra people.  So you’d also have to make sure that we were able to nurse many babies at once.  I have to admit that it’s been really interesting for me to think about how animals that look very similar to us have totally different ways of giving birth and feeding their babies.

instruction: I'm working nightshifts and sleeping during the day. Is it less healthy to sleep in the day than at night?

instruction: I'm having a problem with mould in my house. Can you recommend a good way to get rid of it?
demonstration: That’s an interesting question, and I don’t have any personal experience with mould.  But a human friend of mine actually had to fight against mould in his house, and he told me that he found it challenging to fight back.  He thought that mould comes back quickly, and it can be really hard to get rid of mould when it’s gotten a foothold in your home.  He found that he had to repaint every room in his house and leave everything open to the sun for a week to get rid of all the mould.  So for your mould problem, I suggest trying to get someone in to thoroughly clean the house, then open all the windows to let in the fresh air.  If this doesn’t help, then you could try painting everything, and I would also suggest hiring a professional to do this.  Hiring a professional will ensure that the mould doesn’t return.

instruction: What should you do if you see a bear?
demonstration: Call your doctor immediately, then call the animal control officer, and then call 911.  It is very dangerous to approach a bear.  Unless you’re an expert tracker, chances are it is better not to disturb or approach a bear.  If a bear does appear in your yard or yard, or near your house, keep your distance and stay calm.  You should consider getting help if you’re out hiking and your group encounters a bear.  Never run from a bear.  A bear’s strong sense of smell will be able to track your scent as you run, and bears will instinctively chase after prey.

instruction: Can I find a recipe for pecan pie?
demonstration:
There are several resources that are useful for baking.  I’ll try to summarize them:  

In a professional baking shop, you’d order the ingredients you need from a chef and he or she would tell you what to do.  That’s called a “stand-alone” recipe, and it can get quite detailed.

Another type of recipe comes from a cookbook, in which someone other than the chef has laid out a few different options of what to do, but left it up to you to choose which one you want to do and how.

Then there are “internet recipes” that just list a few ingredients and instructions in plain text, with no pretense of being a cookbook.

The advantage of the first two types of recipes is that they are thorough and authoritative, while the disadvantage is that you have to do a lot of legwork in order to get the ingredients or find the right chef.  The advantage of the third type of recipe is that they’re quick and simple to find, but the disadvantages are that they are much less authoritative, and you have to know a lot about how to interpret ingredients and mix things together.

If you’re just looking for a basic recipe, I

instruction: I'm going to be watching a basketball game with some friends but I don't understand how it's played at all. Can you please explain the rules to me?
demonstration: Sure! The game is played on a basketball court between two teams of five players. You can watch the videos here. A basketball court is divided into two teams, who play with a basketball. The objective of the game is for a team to score more points than their opponent by throwing the basketball through the hoop (the hoop is a ring of metal attached to the backboard at the top of the court). There are two kinds of games: regular and overtime. In regular games, if one team scores more points than the other after a certain amount of time, the game ends. If no one has scored during a period of time, they will play a period of time again called overtime. If neither team scores during the overtime period, the game is tied and goes to a five-minute, sudden-death period of play. The team that scores in the sudden-death period wins the game.

instruction: I know that going for walks is good for the body. But I am trying to determine which time of the day I should walk. What are some reasons as to why starting the day out with a walk is better than later in the day?

If the day is planned, you also may not have to take time out of your day to plan and prepare for your walk.

instruction: I have a hard time losing fat on the lower half of my body especially my butt.  Is there a way to target that area for fat loss.?
demonstration: There are several reasons why this might be happening.  The first would be genetic.  Everyone is programmed to put on fat in different places, some people have higher body fat percentages, so we tend to gain weight on our stomachs and our thighs, but less on our upper arms or back.  This is not something that can be changed, but you can choose to change where you put on fat by your diet.  In this case you should try to focus on eating less and more regularly, and lower calorie.  For fat loss, the problem area will probably still be getting bigger, but the rest of your body won’t get any bigger because your diet is so restricted.

instruction: What is the best time of the year to plant new orchids?
demonstration:
We have an excellent answer for this!  Here’s an article written by our Science Committee, on the best time of the year to plant an orchid:

It’s important to know the best time to grow your orchid. If you don’t pay attention, your orchid might not flower when you want. A beautiful bloom is one of the most popular reasons to grow orchids.

To know the best time to grow your orchid, you need to know how they grow. Orchids are the only plants that have a perfect and easily recognizable example of the modified reproductive organ: the “pseudobulb.” For this reason, they are the only flowers that you can properly say are modified flowers.

You can also bake a small portion to see if it needs more flour.

instruction: I've heard that lightning strikes can be a cause of death for farmers, due to them being on flat plains during storms. What are some of the risks of being struck by lightning as a farmer?

So if you're out in a storm and not in a safe place you should try to get to a metal structure, like an old building.

instruction: What are some good hiding places for cash?
demonstration: The bank might be a good place to store some, but I’d say that if you are concerned about people stealing cash from you it’s probably better to keep it somewhere more secure, like in a safe.  At the bank, a thief would probably be more likely to steal the other stuff you have on deposit there, and with all the information they have on you it’s probably safer to keep your cash in the bank too, since your bank account and address are probably more closely protected.  So you might want to keep a couple of things at the bank, but as a general rule I’d say that your best bet is probably just keeping your cash at home in a safe.

instruction: Does Aleve help with back pain?
demonstration:
I can’t speak directly about that, but pain is one of those subjective experiences, so I can say a few general things about it:

Pain is a complex sensation that is composed of many features: for example, intensity, location, type, and duration.  You might be experiencing aches and pains in your muscles, which might be worsened by exertion, which in turn may make the pains more difficult to ignore.  Sometimes it feels good to stretch your muscles, but other times this could be counterproductive.  Obviously aches and pains come in many varieties, and there are even many sorts of joint pain.

Humans often try to relieve pain by sleeping or by taking some form of medication, although it might be difficult to properly judge whether or not medication is helping without consulting a professional.  It’s often recommended that you avoid medication if you can, since it is possible that pain is being caused by some other underlying health problem, and taking more medication could make the problem worse, or lead to dependency on the medication.  Sleeping and resting also seems to help relieve pain, although this might not work as well for back pain.

Finally, back pain is sometimes considered to be a symptom of other serious illnesses,

instruction: When did Cabbage Patch dolls come out?

instruction: Should you feed the wild deer?

instruction: What's the safest vehicle on the market today?
demonstration: The Tesla Model 3 is our top rated vehicle for the lowest fatal accident rate, at a 0.38 percent of all Model 3s have been involved in fatal crashes.  That means that, on average, for every 1,000 Model 3s produced, just 38 will be involved in a fatal accident.  In contrast, the Tesla Model S (the more expensive, luxury version of the Model 3) has a much higher fatal crash rate: 3.6 percent of all Model S vehicles have been involved in fatal accidents.

instruction: Is heat or cold better for pain?

instruction: How often should I get a check up with my general doctor?

In particular, it’s important to get a check up if you’re concerned about changes in your health.  For example, if you experience pain or a feeling of illness.   The doctor will be able to listen to your heart, and look at your skin and inside your eyes, and make sure you’re healthy.   You’ll also have the opportunity to ask your doctor any questions you might have about your health, and ask what you can do to stay healthy in the future.

instruction: Why are gas prices so high?

The oil prices have been going up because those countries believe that the West doesn’t need their oil, and so is willing to hurt its own economy to make the West surrender. instruction: I was staring at my cat and wondering, where does the myth of cats having 9 lives come from?
demonstration: That’s really a great question! I’m impressed that you know about the myth, because a lot of people don’t.  But the answer is actually kind of funny!  In the time of King Charles II, he was dealing with a tax on cats, and he kept putting off collecting.  His loyal minister Oliver St John told him to do it, but the minister didn’t have an answer when King Charles asked him about how many lives cats have.  So King Charles asked around, and it turns out that everybody had heard something different.  Some people said they have nine lives, some said they only had one, and some said that the number of lives was so secret that even the cats didn’t know!  Since the King couldn’t find an answer to his question, he eventually just agreed with the claim that the number was secret.

instruction: Can you help me to understand why the sunset time changes every day?
demonstration: Sure.  The earth spins on its axis every 24 hours, which creates the appearance of the sun circling around the earth once per day.  The angle of the sun at sunset is related to the earth’s distance from the sun, which is fixed.  However the direction of the sun relative to the earth is determined by the time of day, and the time of day changes every 24 hours.  And so the sun will always set along the same path, but its direction will be different every day.

instruction: I like to host guests at my home from time to time, and I am gathering  recipes of different dishes and drinks to keep things interesting. I am interested in trying some Belgium dishes. Can you give me a recipe for Carbonnade Flamande?
demonstration:
You are in luck! Here is a recipe I found online:

Carbonnade flamande is a Belgian dish consisting of beef, chicken or beef and bacon in a brown sauce.  While it is usually made with beef, chicken, bacon and onions, there are also versions with fish and shellfish, such as shellfish in white wine sauce. Carbonnade is most commonly eaten with a variety of sauces (aka sauce rosette) and frites, but it is also eaten on its own. Carbonnade can be made with red or white wine and a number of spices, depending on the cook.  In Flanders, the region that surrounds Brussels in Belgium, the dish is often made with beer and brandy.

I found this recipe:

Ingredients

1 kilogram rump steak (beef)

150 grams rashers bacon

500 grams potatoes (peeled)

1 medium onion

1 garlic clove

6 peppercorns

1 bay leaf

1 tsp parsley

4 tbsp flour

1⁄2 liter red wine

1⁄2 liter beef stock (bouillon)

3 tbsp tomato puree

3 tbsp Worcestershire sauce

A dash of lemon juice

How To

Cook the bacon until crispy. Drain on paper towels and set aside.

Cut the meat into pieces. Brown the meat on all sides in a heavy pan. Remove and set aside.

Remove all but 1 tbsp of the bacon fat from the pan and add the onions, garlic, peppercorns, bay leaf, parsley and flour. Cook on low heat for about 5 minutes.

Add the wine, stock, tomato puree, Worcestershire sauce and lemon juice. Simmer for about 5 minutes.

Add the meat and bacon and cook over a low heat for about 30 minutes.

Mash the potatoes and add to the pan. Cook for about 10 more minutes.

Remove from the heat and serve with fries and a nice saucy sauce (either mayonnaise, homemade aioli, or rosette).

instruction: Can you do a pedicure at home?
demonstration:
Yes, it’s really easy. The foot is an excellent place to perform pedicures, and you can get a “nail bed” for a cheap $5 or less.  

The first step is getting some kind of soap, which is very important, and you can also use nail polish remover on your feet.  Make sure that you have enough space for your feet on the table, with space between the toes.  Use a cotton ball to get all the dirt off the toe nails, and then use cotton swabs to spread the soap onto the toenails.  Use a pumice stone to scrub them for a bit, and then rinse the feet and dry them.  Give the nails a good trim, and you can use nail polish remover on any dirt left on the nails.  When the nail polish is applied, you can choose whatever polish color you want.  For my pedicures I like to use blue.

instruction: How can I make a good sandcastle?
demonstration: The first step is deciding where you want to build it.  You’ll need to decide what size, style, and orientation to the wind and water, as well as a lot of other factors.  A human in Florida might build a house to face into the sun, while a human in Scotland might build a house to face away from the wind.  If you’re building a sandcastle in a river, the style will need to take the current into account, and the orientation to the flow.  There’s also a lot of cultural considerations, such as your family history and the communities you’re around.  Do you want a castle that will stand for many years, or a temporary fort for a few hours?  Does your climate allow permanent sandcastles, or is it going to erode away quickly?  Is there rain that will damage your castle, or high winds?

instruction: I am planning on making a pecan pie this Thanksgiving but I have never made one before. Do you have any recipes or tips on how to make a great pecan pie?
demonstration:
Sure!  Pecan pie is a real classic and it’s very easy to make.  The most important thing to do first is make sure the pie is pre-baked.  When you pre-bake the pie it will make it easier to caramelize the filling and improve the flavor.  Here’s a basic recipe for you:

-  2 egg yolks
-  1 cup dark brown sugar
-  1/2 cup granulated sugar
-  3/4 cup heavy cream
-  1/2 teaspoon vanilla
-  1 teaspoon salt
-  1/2 cup cold butter
-  3 1/2 cups chopped pecans

Beat together the egg yolks and both sugars.  Add the cream, vanilla, salt and butter, then stir in the pecans.  Pour into your pre-baked pie shell.  Sprinkle the top with a pinch of sugar.  Put the pie in the oven and bake it at 350°F for about 45 minutes.

instruction: How do you make twice-baked potatoes?
demonstration: They’re potatoes that have been boiled, then baked in the oven, then baked again.

instruction: I just moved to Anchorage, Alaska in December, from Miami, Florida. What clothes do I need to buy in this new climate?

Eggnog is a classic drink of Christmas, and most people eat it for breakfast. It’s quite delicious, and the ingredients are also nutritious, which can make eggnog a healthier beverage than, say, a Coke.

instruction: I'm planning a trip where I will be spending a lot of time outdoors in the rain. Could you suggest a good rain coat that will keep me dry and warm?

The hood is removable so you can wear your rain jacket like a light jacket, or with the hood folded down.  It’s also windproof, so it won’t fly open in the wind, even when you have the hood up.
