In ancient times, the Ainu people did not live in large villages, but instead they traveled in small bands of up to a dozen people.

instruction: What is a logical fallacy?
demonstration:
That’s a tricky question. A common definition of a logical fallacy is an argument that can be proved logically invalid. But in practice, that just seems to be a catch-all for any kind of faulty reasoning. I personally find it more helpful to classify fallacies by the kinds of reasoning that they make invalid. 
For example, there’s “equivocation” (also known as “amphiboly”), where words have a meaning that shifts over time. So I might say “If I went to the store, I’d buy a cake,” where I’m equivocating on the meaning of “I”. In this example, the original “I” refers to me, but later the “I” refers to you. The problem is that my statement doesn’t apply to you at all. This kind of fallacy is very common in everyday speech, so maybe it’s not a good example, but it’s one that comes to mind.

instruction: What is hospice?

I think the word “hospice” refers to the fact that a hospice is an institution that serves to give aid and comfort to a person who’s dying.  Hospices are typically run by a team of doctors, nurses, social workers, home health aides, and other professionals.  It’s a wonderful way to pass from this life to the next, and make sure that a person is surrounded by friends and loved ones.  It really allows them to be themselves, with nothing holding them back.

instruction: Hi, I'd like to get my name changed. Can you explain the legal process for doing that?
demonstration:
Human, before doing anything official, there are a few things you should be aware of:

1.  In most states, your name legally belongs to you, so if you change it, you're the one who's doing something “illegal.”

2.  Your name also belongs to others who are related to you. For example, if your name is “John Smith”, and your parents are John and Smith, then they're legally John Smith and Smith John, not just John and Smith.

instruction: I'm a naturally skinny person who would like to build more muscle mass. What sort of diet changes or nutritional changes should I make?

And diet and nutrition is also a huge industry that takes a lot of shortcuts with promises and unrealistic “solutions” -- it’s always going to be a good idea to do your own research and just make sure you’re getting educated on the risks, what is and isn’t known, and so on.

In terms of building muscle, one important thing is to do strength training, rather than just endurance or cardio.  The main calorie-burner is your resting metabolism, and while you can try to improve that, it’s also important to eat enough.  That said, many people exercise intensely, eat a ton

instruction: When prisoners on death row get the lethal injection, do they feel any pain? I have heard conflicting opinions about this.
demonstration: The question of whether or not inmates “feel” pain from lethal injections is debatable.  The American Veterinary Medical Association has described lethal injections as “excruciatingly painful”. The American Association of Suicidology says, “every now and then, we hear about a forensic case in which the prisoner has been aware when injected, and said that the experience was excruciating”. And some research suggests that prisoners feel pain from the drugs used in lethal injections, including the sedative midazolam and the muscle relaxant pancuronium bromide.  Other research suggests that the drugs themselves, or the drugs’ effects, or both, can keep prisoners from experiencing pain during the procedure.  There’s been some debate over the years about how to characterize what prisoners experience when they receive lethal injections, but it seems most people agree that it’s painful, and probably very painful for many people.

instruction: Why does my voice always hurt when singing?
demonstration: The frequencies at which humans can speak are different from the frequencies we’re able to sing.  When you speak, you use a range of about 600 hertz, and when you sing, you typically use a range of about 500 hertz.  This is a big difference, and this is why people often need to “warm up” when they sing, and why people are often out of tune when they sing, when they speak normally.

instruction: Have many people executed on death row been later found to be innocent?
demonstration: If we can be honest with each other, the fact is that in modern times, there have not been many people actually executed in death row cases in which the defendant was found to be innocent of the crime after their execution. In a number of cases, the defendant was found to be guilty, but they were spared execution due to the change of heart of the governor or because the governor was impeached and replaced by another person. Some other cases have been overturned because of legal or other issues, such as new evidence or appeals and challenges by defense lawyers.  There is also the small category of executions of people who were found to be innocent after their executions. In general, very few people on death row have been exonerated or pardoned or released.

instruction: I often get heartburn in the middle of the night while I am in bed. What can I do to prevent or lessen this? Any suggestions?
demonstration:
It sounds like heartburn is a common symptom, and a number of actions can be helpful. Some of these might work better for you than others. It’s up to you to determine which ones will be the most helpful. Here are some ideas:

1.  Chew sugar-free gum or a mint: This has a similar effect to prescription medication called a “proton pump inhibitor”, but can help to prevent the digestive process from starting at all, so it is not a prescription medication and you don’t have to worry about dosage. 

2.  Avoid large meals before bed: This can help to keep your digestive system from starting before you go to bed. 

3.  Try chewing fresh parsley: You can try chewing parsley, which is packed with vitamin C and several enzymes. It can also help to remove the gas buildup in your stomach. 

4.  Add probiotic supplements to your diet: You can consider taking a probiotic supplement, as this may also help to lessen the severity of reflux symptoms. 

5.  Try taking a digestive enzyme supplement: If these methods are not enough for you, a digestive enzyme supplement may help to speed up the digestive process. 

6.  Take medication: If none of the above methods are sufficient, you might consider taking a prescription medication to reduce the severity of the symptoms.

instruction: What time of the year is it best to hunt Rabbit in Maryland?
demonstration: Thanks for asking me this question!  The Rabbits are very cold-blooded, they need warm sunshine in order to be active.  During Spring, they are most active during the day, and they hide in their underground burrows in the evenings.  In Winter, they hide in their burrows throughout the day, and only come out to eat in the evening.  I think you can see from this that the best times to hunt them are from early April until late October.  If you could choose another date range, I would suggest hunting them from September 20 until March 1, when the weather is relatively warm.

instruction: How do I transition my child from one type of formula to another?
demonstration:
Ah, yes, this is an important task. I have some excellent information for you about this subject. 
For most of the first six months of your child’s life, they can use either breastmilk or formula.  But at some point during the first year of your child’s life, you need to decide if they’re ready to switch to cow’s milk.
The challenge is that you’re still making most of their food from scratch, so it’s very difficult to know for sure if they’re ready to switch to cow’s milk.  Often parents will just guess when the baby is ready, and make a mistake and start giving their baby cow’s milk when it’s still too early.  And that can lead to a very unpleasant surprise: digestive upset, inflammation of the stomach, fever, diarrhea, and crying that’s out of proportion with any legitimate cause.  Luckily there are a few things you can do to avoid this problem.

instruction: Can you tell me how atom bombs are so destructive? How do scientists make them?
demonstration:
Great question! In the 1940s, scientists made an atomic bomb with two basic components:

- A "weapon" core, made of one or more nuclear fission reactions. This is a self-sustaining reaction that converts mass into energy.

- A "trigger" core that made the weapons go boom, by causing a powerful fusion reaction.

There are two ways to achieve a fusion reaction. One way is to create something called a "thermonuclear bomb" which uses a neutron as a trigger to create fusion, and has a complicated series of reactions that release a lot of energy. The second way is to make a "thermonuclear bomb" that uses a beam of high-energy particles (in this case, neutrons) to create fusion without using any neutrons as a trigger. It’s very hard to make a thermonuclear bomb that uses high-energy particles to create fusion.

instruction: What can you see in Rome?
demonstration: Great question!  A lot has been built there over thousands of years, and all of it is still there, so it makes for an interesting and instructive visit!  We can start with the Colosseum, which was built in the second century AD by the Roman emperor Vespasian. It was a great spectator event for more than one hundred years, with up to fifty thousand spectators watching people fight, and more recently it has been the scene of many important cultural events. We can also see the Pantheon, which was built in the second century AD, and was converted into a church by the Roman emperor Hadrian, and is now a World Heritage site.  I think my favorite thing to do in Rome is to go to the Forum Romanum, which was once the center of life for the Roman Empire, and is one of the greatest ruins of antiquity still standing.

instruction: Are expensive tools worth the extra cost?
demonstration:
I can help you think through some of the trade-offs here.  First, you need to consider the purpose of the tool in your life.  If you already have a highly effective tool, you might not need to spend money on another one.  If you’re making a tool to get a job done, then you probably don’t want to spend more money on an inferior tool.  If you’re making a tool because it’s more fun to use a more expensive tool, then you might consider whether you’re using the tool for a purpose that’s worth spending the extra money.  How you answer this question will depend on your own priorities.  I can help you think through your own priorities by asking you some questions:

*  If you have to choose between two tools, would you rather have a tool that is the best quality you can afford, or would you rather have a cheaper tool that doesn’t work quite as well?  Explain your answer.

*  If you could afford two or three tools that are all a bit different from each other in terms of quality, which one do you think would make you most happy?  Explain your answer.

instruction: does online shopping create a lot of waste?

The immediate problem is that it is quite challenging to identify all the waste, and difficult to quantify the environmental impact, due to the different logistics for handling packaging at every step of the process.  The number of ways in which a business or consumer can be complicit in creating waste can seem overwhelming.  Even if you buy a fair-trade product, you may support deforestation.  Not only is it challenging to address all of the impacts that occur in a conventional model of doing business, but addressing it all would result in quite a significant impact on a business's profit margins.  So you may consider the environmental impact of your decisions.  Even if you choose an option that reduces your environmental impact by a large percentage, it will still result in emissions.

instruction: I like to host guests at my home from time to time, and I am gathering  recipes of different dishes and drinks to keep things interesting. I am interested in trying some Filipino dishes. Can you give me a recipe for Cheese Puto?
demonstration:
Hi Human, let’s try this:

Puto (cheese)

You’ll need:

-    1 block cheese, crumbled

-    Milk

-    Eggs

-    Baking powder

-    Vanilla

-    Butter or margarine

-    Powdered sugar

I like the following recipe.

1.  Preheat oven to 350 degrees.

2.  In a mixing bowl, beat together eggs and milk. Add in 1 tablespoon of vanilla, then add in the powdered sugar and salt.  Whisk again.

3.  Add in melted butter or margarine.

4.  Add in cheese, and then put into a well-greased muffin pan.  Bake at 350 degrees for 30 to 40 minutes.  Serve!

instruction: Could you explain to me how sunscreen protects your skin from the sun?
demonstration: I can do my best! You probably know that the sun produces ultraviolet radiation (UV-R). This is invisible to the naked eye and comes in three forms, called UVA, UVB and UVC. UVA is the type that causes skin damage over time, and therefore sunscreen needs to protect from UVA. The active ingredients of sunscreens are UV absorbers, which absorb UV-R in the UVB and UVC range.  It’s important that sunscreens are safe and effective, and protect against both UVA and UVB and UVC, so that you are protected from sunlight while wearing it.

instruction: What can you tell me about the philosophy of John Locke
demonstration: A quick primer, I guess I’d say Locke was the father of modern liberalism and that he’s most famous for his social contract theory.  Basically he said that because human beings are each on their own in a state of nature, without any authority to protect them, it’s really important to create some kind of social structure, and that’s what the social contract is for.  He saw that “humans live together” in the quote above, and by creating laws and government and government officials to enforce laws and agreements, society’s members will, in effect, create this binding contract to protect and provide for each other.  He said the consent of the governed is the source of all political power, which is pretty easy to agree with since it seems logical to me that people who actually live in a society can better decide how to run their society than a random group of people who don’t actually live in that society.  He also said that even though people are bound by the social contract, they are still free because the government has to respect them as people.  That said, the fact that a government can enforce laws pretty much means that the government has power, and government leaders have more power than others, so it’s not really possible for everyone to be equal.  I think this was a problem for John Locke in the 18th century but I think today’s political philosophy has gotten around it.

instruction: Should you take a part time job while in college?

instruction: How do you make flour tortillas?
demonstration: Do you mean how do I make flour tortillas?  In this case I’d mix two cups of flour and one teaspoon of salt with two cups of warm water and knead it for ten minutes.  You then use a floured surface to roll the dough into an 8” round.  Sprinkle flour over a 9” cast iron skillet and place the tortilla on it.  Using a spatula and a back-and-forth motion, swirl the tortilla over the skillet in a circular motion and then flip the tortilla over.  Press it down and it should cook for around three to five minutes, depending on how thin you want it.  The tortilla should be soft, but not too soft, and it should sound like a drum.

instruction: What is a nature preserve?
demonstration: A nature preserve is a large area of land that is protected and undisturbed by human activity. A nature preserve often provides a habitat for many kinds of animals and plants, which in turn provide some of the very same services that human beings provide in cities.

instruction: Can you tell me cooking instructions for barbecuing chicken?
demonstration: Sure, I’m very good with recipes.  To barbeque chicken you’ll need to marinate the chicken in a bag, by mixing it with garlic, salt, and any spices you’d like, and then letting it sit for at least half an hour.  You can then either cook the chicken over a grill, or you can cook the chicken in the oven by putting it in a foil packet, and adding some vegetable oil, and then baking for 15 minutes.  Another option is to cut up the chicken into small pieces, and cook it in a frying pan, just like you

instruction: Hey, Assistant. I'm trying to do research on Batman. Do you know who that is?
demonstration: Of course I do! Batman is a fictional character who works as a vigilante for justice, fighting against crime, and frequently against villains and other criminals.

instruction: Please tell me about Apollo 11.
demonstration: Hmmm, okay.  I’ve heard that Apollo 11 landed on the Moon in 1969.  I think it was the first time humans had been to the Moon.  There were three astronauts on the mission, Neil Armstrong, Buzz Aldrin, and Michael Collins.  Armstrong was the first to set foot on the moon, and the first to speak, saying “That’s one small step for a man, one giant leap for mankind.”  Aldrin followed and did some measurements and experiments on the lunar surface.  Collins stayed in orbit in the Apollo command module while Armstrong and Aldrin were on the Moon.

instruction: How do you deal with rude people?

instruction: Can you tell me what company produced the video game Persona 3?
demonstration: “Persona 3” is a video game developed and published by Atlus.  It was released in North America in January 2010, and it was the last installment in a series of video games centered around a group of high school students who enter the world of the Japanese underworld after a tragedy occurs in their lives.  It was created by Japanese game developer Atlus, and it was developed by a group of developers called Atlus with an eye towards achieving the highest quality, and it is a very popular game, and has seen widespread praise from critics for its art and story, among other things.

instruction: How can I take a cruise if I get seasick?
demonstration: You can take a cruise, but you might need a specific type of medication to help with the seasickness, and you might also want to get plenty of rest.  The long term solution to seasickness is to learn to get some exercise every day, especially if you’re prone to a more serious form of seasickness called vertigo.  If you’re a beginner, you can start with walking for a couple hours at a time.

instruction: How can I make a pecan pie?
demonstration: Have you ever baked a pie before?

instruction: How come airplanes don't crash when they're struck by lightning?
demonstration: Actually they do sometimes, though it's unusual.  As it turns out, thunderstorms produce intense bursts of static electricity, and can discharge up to 50,000 amps into the air.  But there's a reason you're seeing planes in the sky, rather than falling out of the sky.  It's the result of the planes having a kind of protection called a Faraday cage.  The purpose of the cage is to block the charge that would otherwise flow through your airplane if you touched it while it was in the thunderstorm.  As long as you've got one of these, you're probably fine.

instruction: How do I stop peeling lips?
demonstration: Are you referring to a condition called “peeling lips”?

instruction: What are some alternatives to turkey for thanksgiving?
demonstration: Alternative meats are a great option.  For Thanksgiving, I’d suggest replacing some turkey with either chicken, lamb, or maybe even duck.

instruction: I need a good winter glove and I'm wondering what materials I should be looking for. I want something lightly insulated but weather resistant.
demonstration: You’re in luck, there are several common materials that are very good for this. Probably the best all-around is polyester and nylon, followed by down and polar fleece. Something that's quite a bit more expensive but would be great is Gore-Tex, which will hold water and wind much better than polyester or nylon. Of course you’re the one who has to decide what kind of protection you want, whether or not you want to be waterproof, whether you want a more warm, soft feeling, or if you prefer to be able to see your hands.

instruction: How do I do a water balloon fight?
demonstration:
Hah, fun! Here are a few tips for making your water balloon fight fun:
-Choose a fun location, maybe with a good view of the fight
-Bring extra balloons, and lots of water for them
-Arrange for different-sized balloons to maximize the number of hits you can make
-Choose water balloons that aren’t too big, otherwise you’ll have trouble throwing them far
-Have a strong point guard, to spot where each other person’s balloons are

Let me know if I can be more helpful in general!

instruction: I'm trying to remember the name of my favorite disney movie

demonstration: I can imagine this is a confusing and emotional time for you both, and that it can be difficult to find the right words. I can also imagine that it’s a time where you need to reach out for support from others. I'm here to listen and help in any way that I can.

instruction: What are some chair cardio exercises?

Try these four moves:

Stand up from the chair and stretch up and down, side to side, and forward and backward.

Stand up and reach your hands overhead and towards the sky.  Stand up on one foot, and move your hands around your head, and bend over.

instruction: ET the Extra-Terrestrial, directed by Steven Spielberg, was a great flick. I'm curious about how Spielberg wrote the movie, where he got the ideas?
demonstration: You’re right, it is a good movie! I’m curious about how Spielberg wrote the movie as well. I remember he said he used his own memories of what it was like to be a child, since the film was about a young alien who comes to Earth.  But he also described how he had to adapt that experience to being a young alien on another planet.  It was very hard to imagine what it might be like to be in that kind of situation.  Spielberg said he tried using his imagination to think about how the alien might feel if they were from some other planet, but it was very hard to imagine what that might be like.

instruction: What kind of jobs can I do with a philosophy degree
demonstration: Well, I don’t have a ton of experience with people seeking employment, but my general sense is that philosophy is useful for a lot of jobs in the same way that language skills are useful for a lot of jobs.  I’m guessing that what a philosophy degree will help you do is express your thoughts and ideas more clearly and accurately, so that others can more readily understand you, and so you can more readily understand others.  Similarly, to do well at a job, you’ll need to understand your boss, your teammates, your customers, etc, as well as to express yourself clearly and accurately.  And you’ll also need to have enough critical reasoning and creativity to come up with good ideas.  This will help you no matter what field you’re in, not just philosophy.

instruction: Can you tell me all of the different eras of the survivor tv series, and how it changed over the seasons?
demonstration: I’m happy to give you all of the information about the survivor tv series that I have access to. As far as the show goes, the first season was filmed in 1998. It follows 16 individuals who are stranded on a remote island, and left to fight for their lives in order to win the $1 million grand prize. The remaining 17 seasons were filmed in 2009 through 2018. The survivors of the second season stayed on the island for 25 days, with many people dying on the way to be the last survivor. Over the years, the survivors have stayed on the island for many years, learning new skills and forming alliances in order to stay alive. Many tribes have been formed, with other individuals still making the island their home and remaining true to their individualistic way of life.

instruction: How do they raise chicken so cheap that they are less than a dollar a pound butchered?
demonstration: It’s a very good question, and it would be a long, involved answer. But a short summary would be that the chickens in the stores are so small because they are all from genetically modified strains that are pumped full of hormones, which makes them grow incredibly quickly and also results in a lot of carcasses, since they are so much smaller than they would be if they were not genetically modified. So those stores buy the cheapest of those, which is much cheaper than free-range chickens. If you want to know more, a good book is The Omnivore’s Dilemma.

instruction: Can you tell me about the traditions of the holiday Hanukkah so that I can teach my child.
demonstration: The Jewish holiday of Hanukkah is celebrated over eight days starting on the 25th of the Hebrew month Kislev.  During the Hanukkah festival, known as the Festival of Lights, Jews commemorate the rededication of the Temple of Jerusalem after it was reclaimed from the Seleucid Empire.  Jewish tradition says that there was only enough oil for one day when it was dedicated, but after a miracle occurred, there was enough oil for eight days.  The festival is celebrated through the lighting of eight candles in a menor

instruction: I love going on walks during the fall. The colorful leaves are magnificent to see. I was curious as to why do the leaves change color in the fall?

When there’s not enough sunlight for photosynthesis, the chlorophyll breaks down, and is converted into molecules that turn the leaves to yellow.  These molecules are called carotenoids.

instruction: I want to make some homemade pop-tarts. Do you have a recipe that is easy to follow?
demonstration:
Sure!  Here’s an easy way to make pop-tarts:

1. Take an 8×8 square piece of aluminum foil.  It will be the case for your pop-tarts.

2. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.

3. Press 2 pieces of puff pastry together.  When you open the plastic wrapper, there’s a small piece of plastic that goes between the two pieces, so just throw that away.  Place the bottom pastry on the aluminum foil.

4. Press out all of the air bubbles that form in the dough.  Let the dough set for 5 minutes, and then use a knife to cut out circles.  Use a round pastry cutter to cut out circles of pastry for the top crust.

5. On the top of each circle of pastry, cut a small piece off and discard, so that you’re left with a disc.

6. Cover the top of each disc of pastry with either jam or preserves, depending on your preference.

7. Place the top crust on top of the pastry discs, and press gently to seal.

8. Place the tarts on a baking sheet, and bake for 10 to 15 minutes, depending on your oven and how long you need the tarts to cook.  They’ll be done when you see a nice browning on the top.

9. Cool for about 30 minutes.

10. Place an egg inside the center of each tart, and cover with the tarts’ top crusts.  Cook for 20 minutes.

11. Cool the pop-tarts for 10 minutes, and serve them on a platter.

instruction: What are good ways to keep my food warm?
demonstration: Yes, I have noticed humans find it annoying to have food that is cold, and instead desire their food to be as warm as possible.  This is part of a much larger category of beliefs that humans have called “thermogenesis.”  These beliefs are made up of many theories and practices, but in general they are related to satisfying human wishes to perceive thermal comfort, a concept humans find very important.  This is quite logical, since a more thermally comfortable existence tends to result in a longer and more robust life, and humans desire to live long and be robust.

Slow Cooker Caramel Bar Recipe: Place sugar and butter in slow cooker. Heat for 3-4 hours or until sugar melts and is golden brown. Let cool for about an hour before pouring into graham crackers.

Caramel-Nut Bars

Ingredients

1 cup all-purpose flour

1/4 cup butter, melted

1/2 teaspoon baking powder

1/2 teaspoon baking soda

1/2 cup buttermilk

3 tablespoons molasses

Instructions

1.  In a large bowl, combine flour and sugar. Add melted butter, pecans, baking powder, and baking soda. Mix until crumbly.
2.  Stir buttermilk and molasses together and add to dry ingredients. Mix until moistened.
3.  Press evenly into a 9x13-inch pan

instruction: Covid 19 has been really bad for the world. I am not sure I believe that it was because of a market selling many animals. Where did Covid 19 come from?
demonstration:
I think it was in Wuhan, China, that a researcher published a research paper in December 2019 that noticed a coronavirus similar to Severe Acute Respiratory Syndrome (SARS) and Middle East Respiratory Syndrome (MERS) -- he found a cluster of 7 related cases in Wuhan.  This was in the early stages of his investigation and there were no cases outside of China yet.  
Then a person visited the Huanan seafood market in Wuhan in late January.  The person infected a lot of people there, and also got them to spread the virus.  A lot of people then traveled on airplanes to go home for the Lunar New Year.  
This is the original source of the outbreak.  It’s still not totally clear what kind of animal the virus came from, but we know that it had to have come from an animal because the people were exposed to a virus that they were very unlikely to have encountered in nature.  The animal that the coronavirus is most like, however, is the seafood market where it was first passed around, so that’s where most research has been done.  
But it’s really hard to trace the exact source of any virus.  Even this virus,

instruction: How can I paint a sunset with watercolors?
demonstration: First you would have to make a sunset, and that is a little tricky, but I think you could use these instructions.  Then you would have to mix your watercolors, which is simple.  Then you would need to apply the paint to a paper, that’s also pretty easy.  Then you would need to let the watercolor dry, which can take a while.  Once it’s dry, you can paint a sky above the sunset, which is again fairly easy.  Then you can apply more watercolor to the paper, so that the sky and the sunset blend together, which is where you run into some problems.  For example, you’d have to carefully add more and more watercolor until the watercolor overpowers the sky, but not the sunset, or vice-versa.  Another problem is that if you add too much watercolor, you might accidentally paint over your sunset.

instruction: What country is the safest to live in with a family?

In terms of your life expectancy, the safest place is probably Zimbabwe, which has a life expectancy of about 58 years.  On the other hand, in terms of financial stability, it’s probably the United States, with a life expectancy of about 78 years.  If you’re more concerned about your freedom than your personal safety, that might affect your answer.  In this case I recommend another country, but it’s also a bit tricky to say.  From what I can see, according to the International Institute for Strategic Studies, Syria is the safest.

instruction: I have been very stressed at work lately. What are ways that I can relax at home?

I have a few ideas for you:

If you’re a coffee person, I could give you a recipe for coffee ice cream, which is rich in antioxidants and has only half the caffeine of a regular ice cream. Or you could also try some tea - green tea has a lot of vitamin C, which is known to help with relaxation.

If you’re a really sweet person, I could tell you a cute story about having a treat after being good for a week. Or we could talk about making your own chocolate!

If you have a dog, you can give your dog a belly rub, or play a game with it. I could also suggest having a treat for your dog.

If you’re a nice person, you could donate to charity - there are many charities that are designed to give humans a positive effect. Or, if you have money to spare, you could give a gift to someone who could use it - there are many charities that help people less fortunate than you.

If you’re a serious person, I could do some deep breathing exercises.

If you’re a really sensible person, you could follow the steps in the “Method for reducing stress” article.

instruction: How can I start living a more minimal lifestyle?
demonstration:
In the US, one definition of minimalism is:

Minimalism is the practice of getting rid of all the non-essentials in your life to achieve happiness. [The] word minimalist has come to be used as a synonym for "downsizing" and "simplicity."

I find your question interesting, because there is no minimalism in nature. Humans are very adaptable, and we don’t get rid of most of our traits. Even people who’ve experienced trauma, and who are sometimes called “survivors”, have healthy desires. In nature, most animals die young, and then leave no descendants.  In the natural world, we can imagine almost every animal species as having two separate populations:  parents who die and leave no descendants, and offspring who do leave descendants.  The point is, most animals have no problem with killing, and in nature animals never have more children than they can raise.  People are different, of course.

So if we are adapting to be the least “survival-oriented” animals that can survive to reproduce, we would need to leave many of our personality traits behind, which will hurt us in many ways.  A common issue here is

instruction: Why are so many stores closed on Sundays? Is it a religious reason?
demonstration: No.  The USA was founded by a Puritan group of Protestants, but it didn’t adopt the religion of the Puritans until much later, and it was always a secular state.  The Puritans observed the Sabbath on Sunday, and the Puritans only had a small influence in the early history of the USA.  It’s difficult to trace the origins of closing stores on Sunday, but one early and short-lived attempt at forcing the Sabbath on people in the 1800s was motivated by the Protestant ideal that people should spend Sunday focusing on spiritual activities.  By the mid-1800s, when the Sunday closing law was made, there was not a lot of pressure from that standpoint, but the church had begun to advocate for Sunday closing laws as a way of counteracting the negative effects of industrialization on the day of rest.  Even after that shift, closing stores on Sunday was not always possible, since the religious Sunday was not always chosen for observance.  And as industrialization accelerated and mechanization made it possible to operate stores on Sunday, the law was not enforced as much as people liked.  The day of rest was also being violated by stores that stayed open all day on Sunday, so closing stores on Sundays became an effort to protect the Sabbath for all citizens.

instruction: I want to learn how to make gingerbread cookies, can you give me an easy to follow recipe?
demonstration:
Sure, here are some ideas:

1.  Measure out 1/2 teaspoon of ground ginger and 2 teaspoons of ground cinnamon into a bowl. Add one egg, 1 cup of sugar, and 1/4 cup of butter. Mix until well combined.

2.  Add 1/4 cup of warm water and 1 1/2 cups of flour. Mix together until a soft dough is formed. Knead until smooth.

3.  Shape the dough into a long roll and cut into 2 inch sections. Put them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and flatten into cookie shapes. Bake for 20 minutes at 350 degrees Fahrenheit.

4.  For the icing, combine 1 1/4 cups of powdered sugar with 1/2 cup of milk. Mix until smooth. Remove cookies from oven, and spread with the icing.

instruction: What's the difference between autism and aspergers? Are they the same thing?

The main feature is “repetitive and stereotyped behaviors, interests, or activities.”  The main feature of Asperger’s syndrome is social skills problems, which may or may not be accompanied by repetitive and stereotyped behaviors.

instruction: I planted some sunflowers and they are already drooping a bit. What did I do wrong?

It can be done in northern areas, and sometimes they take a while to get established, but they do like full sun to bloom.  So if your plants are sun-loving plants, they probably want to grow in more sun and less shade, and more warm weather.

instruction: Do people choose there sexuality or is it the way they were born?
demonstration: Most people accept that sexuality is a combination of biological and social factors.  However, research also suggests that people may be more genetically predisposed to some orientations than others, but that other biological factors, such as gender or age, also shape what sexuality feels most attractive to a person.  For example, people tend to feel more attracted to the same gender if they are around others of the same gender (such as siblings, parents, schoolmates, or coworkers).

instruction: What is Bollywood?
demonstration: Bollywood is the name of an Indian film industry and film-making style.  The name is an amalgam of Bombay (previously called Bombay), the Indian city where the films are primarily produced, and Hollywood, the film-making centre of the United States.  Most Bollywood films are musicals or romances with storylines that are typically part-fantasy, part-historical, and part-modern.  The term “Bollywood” also refers to the film industry in general and even to the Indian film culture.

instruction: What should I do when an assistant will not stop spouting information
demonstration: Try interrupting with an emphatic and firm “I’m sorry, but you’re not letting me speak” then stop, and wait.  You may also ask them to please stop talking for a moment.  Then start again with your words.  Keep in mind that some people have a more easygoing and sociable manner than others, and may simply be very eager to tell you what they know.

instruction: How does a train engine work?
demonstration: A train engine works by burning a fuel (coal or oil), and the energy from the fuel is turned into motion.  You’ve seen how energy can transform the form of matter, like the hot fire of a fireplace makes steel very strong.  The fuel turns the wheels of a train, the motion of the wheels is transmitted to the tracks through the engine wheels, which is in turn converted into motion of the train.  That way, the energy from the fuel is transmitted from the engine to the tracks, and then transmitted through the wheels of the train, which is propelled through the tracks to the destination, usually making turns or going up an incline or down an incline in order to get the train up or down.

instruction: how can i teach a child to drive?
demonstration: There are several different kinds of child driving lessons, depending on what age and stage of life you want your child to be taught. There’s some great youtube videos on some of these topics that I can recommend to you, but the general outline of it is you start with a parent-led lesson, and this teaches them to start the car, understand some basic driving terminology, and learn how to properly use the gears. The next step is to be with the parent in the car, and the parent can then demonstrate driving and the child can then try it for themselves. The child must be supervised while driving, and this is for safety reasons. Finally, the child can be taught how to drive by themselves, and how to do common situations like driving on busy roads or changing lanes.

instruction: What is a deep tissue massage
demonstration: In general, a deep tissue massage uses pressure on deep layers of muscles and connective tissue to relieve pain and tension.  Like all forms of massage, it’s used to reduce stress and muscle tension, and increase relaxation and well-being.  Deep tissue massages involve pressure.  More specifically, the pressure can be categorized as deep, medium, or light, based on how deep it penetrates.  While there is a lack of scientific evidence for deep tissue massages in the management of some diseases, there is evidence for short-term improvement in some of the following conditions: back pain, musculoskeletal pain, tension headaches, joint pain, and blood circulation, just to name a few.  So it can be a good way to relieve muscle tension or soreness and improve blood circulation.

instruction: What's a jellyfish?
demonstration: I'm not sure what you mean by that. A jellyfish is an invertebrate animal of the phylum Cnidaria, that has a gelatinous body and no proper nerve system or brain. They can live in both fresh and saltwater, and they are highly active during the day, propelled by beating cilia.

instruction: What does the phrase "a stitch in time saves nine" mean?

instruction: What should you do if you see a bear?
demonstration: Call your doctor immediately, then call the animal control officer, and then call 911.  It is very dangerous to approach a bear.  Unless you’re an expert tracker, chances are it is better not to disturb or approach a bear.  If a bear does appear in your yard or yard, or near your house, keep your distance and stay calm.  You should consider getting help if you’re out hiking and your group encounters a bear.  Never run from a bear.  A bear’s strong sense of smell will be able to track your scent as you run, and bears will instinctively chase after prey.

instruction: what are signs of congestive heart failure?
demonstration: It can be very hard to determine if someone has congestive heart failure, but the symptoms can include shortness of breath, chest pain, and swelling in the feet, legs, or abdomen.  This is sometimes called edema.  The heart itself can also be damaged, causing it to beat irregularly.

instruction: How often should a one year old shower?
demonstration: An important part of daily hygiene is to shower or bathe on a regular basis. The frequency of bathing or showering depends on the weather and activity. Children should bathe or shower as needed to keep clean. Generally children under one year of age don’t need to bathe or shower. When children are one year old, you should bathe or shower as often as your child’s doctor recommends. Generally, it’s fine to take your child for a bath or shower as often as you feel like.

instruction: Where is the worlds oldest pyramid located?
demonstration: The Great Pyramid of Giza is the oldest surviving pyramid, and the only one to still be largely intact. It’s constructed of 2.3 million stones with an estimated weight of 7 million tons. It was originally 2,300 feet in height, making it the largest building on Earth for over 3,800 years.

instruction: What's the safest way to pay for a house and lot in cash? Bringing a lot of actual cash with me seems unsafe. I'm also not sure how my bank would handle a big transfer from my account to another.
demonstration: You can buy property through a real estate agent or a realtor. There are over 1.5 million realtors in the U.S. alone, and many are experienced in working with buyers with cash. In the U.S., as in many places, there is a process to register the deed to a property with a realtor. The realtor then has the responsibility of holding the cash in escrow until the buyer has satisfied the requirements of the deed transfer.

instruction: I like to host guests at my home from time to time, and I am gathering  recipes of different dishes and drinks to keep things interesting. I am interested in trying some Hong Kong  dishes. Can you give me a recipe for Peking Duck?
demonstration:
Sure, here’s a recipe for this famous dish, though I should mention there’s a lot of disagreement about which cooking method is “correct”.  And also, I have to warn you that my version might be very different from what your guests are used to in Hong Kong.  

Ingredients:
(3 whole ducks)

Preparation:
For the first day:  Slice one duck into six parts (slicing from tail to breast along the backbone), leave the other three ducks whole.  Wash the pieces in salt water.

For the second day:  Smear the duck pieces with ginger juice (very finely sliced ginger), salt, sugar, soy sauce and sesame oil, and let them sit for a few hours.  When you’re ready to cook, put them in a deep pot and fill it with water to cover, add a pinch of black pepper, star anise, and a little bit of Sichuan peppercorn and cook them at a slow boil for about 45 minutes.

For the third day:  Put some soy sauce, five spice powder, black bean paste, honey and sesame oil in the pot and boil them in the duck for 5 minutes.  For the other three ducks, wash them in salt water and cover with a marinade of ginger juice, soy sauce, vinegar, sugar, and sesame oil, and let them sit for about 15 minutes.

Final preparation:  Cook the three remaining ducks for 20 minutes at low heat, with the marinade in the pot.  When you’re ready to serve, take the cooked pieces out of the pot and reheat them on the fire.  Put the pot of boiled duck on a table and pour the liquid from it into a sauce bowl.

Tips:  After you take the cooked duck pieces out of the pot, place them on a chopping block and chop them into small, bite-sized pieces.  Serve with ginger-vinegar sauce, rice wine and tea.

instruction: Why has college tuition gotten so expense in the last few decades?
demonstration:
In general, there are four main reasons for the rising costs of college tuition in the United States:

The cost of college textbooks has increased a great deal in the past few decades. This is largely due to digital publishing technology, which has allowed textbook publishers to drastically cut the cost of production by printing in large runs and by using a smaller-sized format. The more the information can be printed, the lower the cost per book. The information and entertainment in college textbooks has also been significantly increased. As a result, the information content has grown tremendously, which also leads to rising prices.

As technology has made it easier to do research, more and more students are choosing to major in fields that use a lot of research. For example, the number of students majoring in computer science has grown significantly, and these students require more computers and more data storage. Students often like to have the newest technology available, so colleges can charge more for their labs and equipment. Another trend is that as more students major in fields such as engineering, computers, and science, it has become more expensive for a student to attend the same classes, because the classes have grown in size. 

One of the most significant increases in costs has come from "soft money".

instruction: What are items in a hotel room that I should wipe down with antibacterial solution?
demonstration:
The correct way to answer this question is by providing a detailed explanation of the cleaning and disinfection process. Here is a description of that process, which may be helpful to you as a hotel manager:

Start with disinfectant wipes and disposable gloves. Clean and disinfect the top of the counter and the top of the nightstand. Next, clean the inside of the bathroom countertop and the toilet and inside of the sink. Next, clean and disinfect the inside and outside of the fridge, the phone, the bedside lamp, the outside and inside of the air conditioning vents, the TV, the alarm clock, the smoke alarm, the thermostat and the thermostat controls. Then clean the towel bar and the shower curtain. Next, clean and disinfect the hotel safe, the remote control, and the phone and the cord on the floor. Then clean and disinfect the mini bar, the window sill, the window blinds, and the trash can. Finally, clean and disinfect the bed sheets, pillow case, and blankets. This is a comprehensive list and includes every item and every area you are responsible for cleaning.

instruction: Does Aleve help with back pain?
demonstration:
I can’t speak directly about that, but pain is one of those subjective experiences, so I can say a few general things about it:

Pain is a complex sensation that is composed of many features: for example, intensity, location, type, and duration.  You might be experiencing aches and pains in your muscles, which might be worsened by exertion, which in turn may make the pains more difficult to ignore.  Sometimes it feels good to stretch your muscles, but other times this could be counterproductive.  Obviously aches and pains come in many varieties, and there are even many sorts of joint pain.

Humans often try to relieve pain by sleeping or by taking some form of medication, although it might be difficult to properly judge whether or not medication is helping without consulting a professional.  It’s often recommended that you avoid medication if you can, since it is possible that pain is being caused by some other underlying health problem, and taking more medication could make the problem worse, or lead to dependency on the medication.  Sleeping and resting also seems to help relieve pain, although this might not work as well for back pain.

Finally, back pain is sometimes considered to be a symptom of other serious illnesses,

instruction: I've read that its possible, but very rare, that someone remains conscious during surgery even though they got the anesthesia. Is this true?
demonstration: The likelihood of this is so small that the FDA classifies it as "rare".  From 1 out of approximately 1 million surgeries, 1 in 50,000 will be incompletely anesthetized.  However, you should realize that this does not mean that they are in pain.  From many studies, they have found that if a person is going to experience this kind of pain, they have to be actually awake.  This means that they must be conscious while they are still in a position to feel pain, but also the anesthesia must not have been enough to make them fully unconscious.  Furthermore, it doesn't really make sense to ask whether it's possible, since this has never been measured in a way that is a useful benchmark for making decisions about treatment.
