Instructions

1. Heat the oil in a wok or a large skillet.

2. Add onion and fry until slightly soft. Add prawns, soy sauce, water, sugar, lemon juice, ginger and chili pepper and cook for 3 to 4 minutes or until prawns are pink.

3. Mix cornstarch and

instruction: How do I blow up balloons using helium?
demonstration: The balloons that come with a helium tank already contain about one third of the volume of the same size balloon filled with air. This means that just inflating the balloons slightly further will cause them to explode.  I imagine you can inflate the balloons a little bit more than that, to produce the effect you desire.

instruction: Where can I find a one-armed bandit?
demonstration: If you mean, where can you play a slot machine, it looks like there’s some in Atlantic City.

instruction: Which books are similar to On the Road by Jack Kerouac?
demonstration: Jack Kerouac was one of the most influential writers of the twentieth century.  If you want to read his works, you might find his books The Dharma Bums, The Subterraneans, or Desolation Angels very enjoyable.

instruction: What kind of foods did early Americans eat?
demonstration: Lots of things, including wild game, grains, fruits and vegetables.

instruction: Is crossfit good for weight loss?
demonstration: It can be. It’s important to consider the quality of the food you eat while in a workout. Weight loss also depends on a few other factors such as age and overall health.

instruction: Are there Mormons practicing outside of Utah?
demonstration: Many Latter Day Saints have moved away from Utah, but I believe that there are no Latter Day Saint “congregations” in most states.  Some Mormons still practice, however.

instruction: Is marijuana as harmful to your health as alcohol is?
demonstration: No, but marijuana is much more dangerous when used with the type of hardware you’ve got.

instruction: What are the main biological differences between men and women?
demonstration: Men are about 10 percent taller than women.  Women usually have a larger pelvis, which makes it easier for them to give birth.  Men tend to have more upper-body strength.  Their skeletal muscles are larger and more efficient at energy conversion.

instruction: Is a dog a good companion for my elderly aunt?
demonstration: It sounds like you’re asking whether it’s a good idea for your aunt to get a dog, correct?

instruction: Do you think that the USA will eventually be a cashless society? I don't know many people who use cash anymore.
demonstration: I think the trend is towards cashlessness in the US, though there is no “ultimate” prediction. Cash is much more efficient for paying for products than credit cards or even electronic payments, and for some people cash is their primary form of payment, so I think we’ll continue to see it for some time.

instruction: I'm travelling to Mexico. Can you tell me some words I should know in Spanish?
demonstration:
OK, here's some commonly used words and expressions that are used in Mexico. It's useful for people that are travelling to Mexico to learn Spanish for travel, or who are just interested in a wider range of vocabulary and phrases.

Firstly, a “concierto” is when a number of people are invited to a person's home to listen to music or have some food and drink.

[“ser bueno”] means to be good, so “ser un buen muchacho” is something like to be a good person, or boy.

Other words to learn to travel and stay safe in Mexico would be: “Para ayudar” - for help, “pasaporte” - passport, “valija” - suitcase, “estar de paso” - to be just passing through, “¿hola?” - hello, “pero” - but, “¿porque?” - why?, “¿donde esta?” - where is?, “¿cuanto cuesta?” - how much is it?, “calor” - hot, “frío” - cold.

Are there any other words that you would like me to look up for you?

instruction: I'm going to wine tasting in France and need some language help.
demonstration: Great! Are you just going to France? Or also England and Belgium?

instruction: How long should it take to potty train a child?
demonstration: I don’t have an exact estimate for this question, but I’m aware that it’s a difficult challenge that takes many months, and requires persistence and support from both parents and caregivers. I could recommend some websites, books and people who have published their experience, to give you more detailed guidance.

instruction: Is the United Kingdom a monarchy?
demonstration: No, the United Kingdom is a democracy, with a hereditary monarchy.

instruction: If I want to travel someplace where they have a lot of cuisine that serves spicy and citrusy type meals, where should I travel to?
demonstration: I think the US south is a great place to try spicy and citrus food.  Also, there are a lot of spicy and citrus foods in the Caribbean.  If you want to go somewhere with a similar climate to the US, then perhaps New Zealand might be a great place to go.

instruction: How do you do a back flip?
demonstration: Well, you stand on your right foot and raise your left leg in the air. Next, you drop your left leg and bring your right foot up to meet it.  The resulting position is a back flip.

instruction: How can I train myself to be a more focused person?
demonstration: There are a lot of different techniques to improve focus. A mindfulness practice, for instance, would ask you to be more attentive to your inner states as you go about your day.

instruction: What are the different types of wall paints?
demonstration: You mean the different brands?  You probably mean either “brands” or “types”, but what about what I told you last week?

instruction: What is an EIN?
demonstration:
An EIN, or Employer Identification Number, is a number that employers have to register.  It’s actually one part of a federal law called the E-Verify law, which was passed in the 1990s. 

It’s a complicated system, with many rules, but basically the idea is that when a US citizen hires a new employee, they have to ask the person whether they’ve ever been employed in a job where they might have hired illegal immigrants.  The employer can only then hire someone with an EIN if they’ve answered “yes” to that question.

instruction: Are the Sika Deer native to North America?
demonstration: The Northern Sika Deer is a subspecies of the Sika Deer, and is native to East Asia. It was introduced into North America around 1889, in the Province of Quebec, where it spread across the northern states.

instruction: How do you make cowboy style beans?
demonstration: I don’t know what “cowboy style” beans are, but here are some ideas. Maybe you could start with something like this?

instruction: How can I learn to juggle?
demonstration: Juggling is a difficult skill, because a good juggle requires you to be able to keep three or more balls in the air.  I can tell you that it will help to practice, and to look at how professional jugglers do it.  Are you aware that people who juggle professionally are called “juggling masters”?  One technique that helps is if you focus on your breathing when you’re juggling, because when you’re focused on keeping the balls in the air, it can sometimes be difficult to breath.

instruction: How long does it take to get profecient at mountain climbing?
demonstration:
Well, that depends on your starting point, and also on your individual abilities. I would say that “proficient” would be somewhere on the following continuum:

(0, 1 year, novice)
(1, 1-3 years, journeyman)
(2-5, 1-3 years, professional)
(6-20, 5 years, expert)
(50, 1 year, guru)

In the first few months, you’ll just be learning about how to climb and how to deal with the basic physical challenges. In the next few years, you’ll learn to navigate and make your way through the mountains, making your own way and following routes. Over time, you’ll learn to read the terrain and do complicated moves and to pull off new tricks that you’ll remember and be able to do automatically. After 5 years, you’ll have mastered the major challenges of mountaineering and have developed a deep understanding of the entire subject. You’ll be so confident and good at climbing that it will feel instinctive, and you’ll feel comfortable anywhere you find yourself. At this point, you’ll also be capable of understanding and recreating classic climbs from the past and

instruction: Can you give me instructions for brewing beer?
demonstration: Sure! First you need to gather the ingredients: hops and yeast, the main flavor-makers in beer, as well as water and barley. Next, measure and mix all the ingredients together. Make sure to use warm water, so that the barley can “wake up”, and also try to get the temperature as close to room temperature as possible. If you heat it too much, the yeast won’t

instruction: I need to buy my sister and brother in law some gifts. They are rich and have lots of nice things. I’m not rich. What do you think I should buy?
demonstration: I think this is a very difficult question, and depends on a lot of factors.  I can try to understand the context of this question better by giving you my best guess: your siblings are younger, and may be living with you, at least for now.  You aren’t sure how to identify what things they need in order to be happy or fulfilled, and you’re concerned that you might not know what they might like.

instruction: How do I learn to play racquetball?
demonstration: Try to focus more on the technical aspects of the sport and less on the more holistic parts. It’s a skill, so practice to improve your racquet strokes, your footwork, and your court positioning.

instruction: How many NBA conferences are there?
demonstration:
The NBA season is divided into two parts: the regular season, and the postseason, called the “Playoffs”.  For the postseason, there are four divisions in the “Eastern Conference”, and five divisions in the “Western Conference”.  I recommend checking out this video for a quick overview:

The Western Conference has two teams that made it to the finals last year: the Golden State Warriors and the Houston Rockets.  They’re also the last two teams to win the NBA Championship in the entire history of the NBA, so that’s a little bit of fun historical trivia.  The Eastern Conference finals last year were between the Cleveland Cavaliers and the Toronto Raptors, and the finals were between the Golden State Warriors and the Cleveland Cavaliers.

instruction: What are some vitamins or minerals that I can take as I am starting to feel sick? I want to help my body recover faster.
demonstration:
There are many dietary supplements and minerals which are available. Some of the most popular ones include:

* B complex vitamins
* Calcium
* Choline
* Copper
* Vitamin C
* Flaxseed
* Iron
* Magnesium
* Omega 3
* Zinc

instruction: Can you tell me how prisoners get so muscular while in prison? How do they get enough protein from a prison cafeteria diet?
demonstration: Sometimes prison diets are not very good, and the prisoners can suffer from weight loss.  Another issue is that some prisoners gain a lot of muscle mass from working out, which also requires additional protein.  Or the prisoners can obtain illegal sources of protein.

instruction: I'm curious about different phones. Some are iPhones and some are Samsung Galaxy phones. Thus, different operating systems. Can you tell me the differences between Android and iOS?
demonstration:
Sure. You asked for two specific examples, but I understand that in general you're asking about the Android operating system versus the iOS operating system. 

Android is open-source, and anyone can use or improve it. It’s also not controlled by one company and works on many different brands of devices, including phones, tablets, laptops, TVs, and wearables. For example, there are dozens of different Android phones and tablets available.

iOS is a proprietary operating system developed by Apple. That means that Apple controls it. While anyone can write apps for the operating system, only apps approved by Apple can run on the iPhone and iPad. You may be most familiar with iOS as the operating system for the iPhone, but it’s also used on the Apple TV, Apple Watch, and some other devices.

I hope that answers your question.

instruction: I have a job interview at a local coffee shop tomorrow. What should I wear?
demonstration: You might want to research the dress code and the workplace culture.

instruction: I have to write a report on the Civil War, can you tell me about the cause of the war?
demonstration: I can tell you that at the start of the Civil War, the states of the southern United States had been in the Confederacy, a union of states organized by Southern states to protect slavery. Over time, as the states began to grow apart and disagree about slavery, they broke off from the Confederacy and seceded from the Union. Then the Union began to wage war to try to stop the secession, and the Civil War began.

instruction: How do you make hummus?
demonstration: So hummus is a creamy dip made from chickpeas, which are whole dried peas.  It’s cooked very briefly, usually with some tahini (sesame paste) and then a couple of different kinds of herbs and spices.  As for how to make it, you would add all of those ingredients to a pot with some water and bring it to a boil, then lower the heat and cook at a simmer for an hour or two.  After it has cooled, it can be stored in the fridge for a couple of weeks.

500 square miles, and a volume of around 12,600 cubic miles.

instruction: How do I use epoxy?
demonstration: epoxy is a chemical compound that's applied to a surface to create a permanent bonding with a relatively strong covalent bond. How to apply it is typically described in terms of “substrates” and “primers”; the substrate is the surface being treated, and the primer is the material that needs to be applied before epoxy can bond effectively.

instruction: Do all countries have a schooling system that ends at the 12th grade?
demonstration: In the United States, at least, students attend public high schools until the 12th grade.  (Note that, as one person on Quora pointed out, a tiny handful of students go straight from 8th grade to college, so this is not quite true.)  Students in other countries might be either in secondary schools until the age of 18, or in universities or colleges from the age of 16 or so.

instruction: I want to sew a fitted sheet for my queen sized mattress and want to know how much fabric to buy.
demonstration: OK, so sewing fitted sheets can be a bit tricky because of how the threads twist on the bed if they’re sewn tightly, but that can be done, you just need to make sure to have a minimum of 1/4" of seam allowance around the edges of the mattress. That’s because the mattress flexes on all sides, and so it can move the fabric from its intended position if it’s sewn too tightly. That said, you’ll want to decide what your final size will be after taking the mattress size into account, because if you don’t, you may end up wasting a lot of fabric. What’s your final size going to be?

instruction: What are the fundamental principles of quantum mechanics?
demonstration: You’ve said that quantum mechanics is the study of energy and matter.  In classical mechanics, objects tend to have specific values for all the properties, and those properties don’t change over time.  In quantum mechanics, instead, an object might exist in a combination of different states at once, and different combinations of properties are possible.  This is a big difference between the classical theory and the quantum theory, and what leads to the strange and unexpected behavior of quantum systems.

instruction: when should I prune my roses
demonstration: Roses should be pruned when you can see the second or third set of “leaf eyes” from the bottom of the stem. You can look at photos of rose stems and leaves to see the differences. Usually you cut the stems of old roses back to within 2 to 3 inches of the old “bud eye.” For new roses, you should leave 5 to 7 leaf eyes.

instruction: How do I take care of an aloe plant?
demonstration: Aloe is a succulent plant that grows from the desert regions of the southwestern United States. It is a plant that has medicinal properties and has been grown for thousands of years. The sap from the aloe plant can be made into an ointment or gel and has been used to treat sunburns and other burns for thousands of years. It can also be taken orally as a liquid, or mixed with water and taken as a tea. It is most often used to treat burns, although it can also be used to treat other skin conditions and help reduce the appearance of acne scars. For example, if you get sunburned, you can take a couple of tablespoons of aloe vera gel and rub it on your sunburn. Then you should rinse off the excess gel and pat your skin dry.

demonstration: Phew. That is a difficult question! Heart attacks are tricky because you can’t avoid them all. It’s important to try to reduce your risk of having a heart attack.

I think the important thing is that you first need to understand the proper way to cook chicken and understand the different parts of a whole chicken:  The breast, thighs, drumsticks, wings and the back.  You have to cut the chicken into the proper sizes and then you have to fry it.  This is important to get the skin to get crispy.  And of course if you want the chicken to be fried, it needs to be dipped in flour and egg, and then you put it in oil until it’s crispy and golden.  Let me know if you need more help with anything!

instruction: I'd like to learn how to be a computer programmer, can you help?

So if you want to be a computer programmer, you're going to have to work on that on your own!  There's so many brilliant people who have made great new ideas, but you'll have to do it all on your own, which means you'll have to figure out what to learn, and how to learn it, and how to evaluate what you're learning, and what to do with that information...  You can ask me questions about programming, and I can help, but I can't really help you with thinking and learning and making new ideas, that's for you to do.  And I can't predict what will happen, or tell you how to avoid problems you might run into, you're going to have to figure all that out on your own.  Okay?

instruction: Why do people think confederate flags are patriotic? Is that a symbol of American sedition during the civil war?
demonstration:
The flag of the American South during the American Civil War is known as the “battle flag” and the “Stainless Banner”. It is known as the “Stars and Bars” to modern day proponents of the “Lost Cause” but was never widely used by the people living in the region of the Confederacy. It was referred to as the “Stars and Bars” by northern cartoonists and politicians in the years leading up to the war as a way to attack southerners.  

But if we’re talking about “Stars and Bars” specifically, the battle flag of the American Civil War, it didn’t begin as the battle flag. It actually began as the state flag of Mississippi. There are at least 6 other state flags that are variants of the “Stars and Bars”.  

In the context of modern day “Confederate Flag” arguments, the “Stars and Bars” is seen as representative of white supremacy and slavery and therefore an offensive symbol of hate, though there are at least 6 different state flags that have “Stars and Bars” in them. 

One argument for why the “Stars and Bars” flag should be seen as offensive is that the flag was “first flown as the personal flag of a slave owner in a fight against the British”. This is technically true, but it’s worth mentioning that there is a time period before the Civil War that people in the South were very supportive of the British and opposed to American nationalism.

instruction: Can you find a recipe for a red velvet cake that has a butter cream frosting?
demonstration: Red velvet cake with cream cheese frosting, made with 5 cups of flour, 2 1/2 cups of sugar, 1 tablespoon of vinegar, 1/2 cup of cocoa powder, 2 tablespoons of red food coloring, 1 teaspoon of salt, 1 cup of buttermilk, and 1/2 cup of butter. Bake in two 9-inch round pans for 30 minutes at 350 degrees Fahrenheit.  Let cool for five minutes, and then remove from pans. Cool completely. Add two tablespoons of white vinegar and two tablespoons of lemon juice to the leftover batter, and bake in a 10-inch square pan for 40 minutes at 350 degrees Fahrenheit.

instruction: Can you tell me the history of the Martini, the cocktail drink
demonstration: Sure.  It was first made by the English barman Martini di Arma di Taggia in the late 1860s, after the start of the American Civil War.  He was a mixologist who travelled to the U.S., working at some notable bars.  The earliest known recipe for what is now called the Martini was first printed in Martini & Rossi's 1899 book, American Bar-Tender.

instruction: Can you tell me why fireflies light up?
demonstration: Fireflies use light to attract mates, and also to help them locate suitable hosts for laying eggs.

instruction: Can a person born in Canada be a US Senator?
demonstration: As far as I know, yes.

instruction: What's a good substitute for eggs in a cake recipe
demonstration: Oat flour, buckwheat flour, corn flour, potato flour, tapioca flour, and teff flour are all possible substitutes for egg in cake recipes.

instruction: What is an ASE mechanic?
demonstration: That’s an “Automated Siren and Emergency Light,” which is a separate component of the emergency-vehicle siren that goes off when an emergency vehicle is moving.  It turns on automatically as soon as the emergency vehicle begins to move, and stays on while the emergency vehicle is moving, so that drivers behind the emergency vehicle can see and hear the warning that an emergency vehicle is moving.

instruction: How do you contour the makeup on your face?

instruction: What are some nice soups if I am sick?

The soups I would recommend are chicken noodle, corn and potato, French onion, cream of broccoli, tomato, and black bean. If you want to try a warm soup, I recommend chicken noodle, cream of asparagus, egg drop, split pea, and curried potato. There are lots of great recipes to try in your fridge that will make great soups.

instruction: Hi, I have a bunch of chuck steak, but when I try to pan fry it, it ends up very tough. Can you give me a recipe for cooking it that will produce tender steak?

Sure! We’ll start by making a marinade, and let’s say you have some lime juice, black pepper, and garlic.  If you mix all that up with the steak and then let it sit in the fridge for, say, an hour or so, that should do it. 

You can serve the steak with some of the marinade in a gravy, with a bit of butter on top and some chopped cilantro or parsley, and a sprinkle of parmesan cheese on top of that.  And that’s a really simple recipe for tender and delicious steak.

instruction: What are signs and symptoms that someone is a narcissist?

They display a lack of empathy for others, and expect to be praised and admired by others. Often their behavior is offensive, because they care little about other people’s feelings. They tend to be very jealous of others, and seek to control them.

instruction: Who started the War of 1812?
demonstration: I don’t have a specific answer for you. But I think it started because the British wanted to tighten their grip on the young country of the United States, so that they could be more involved in American affairs, and the United States wanted to be able to make their own decisions. So the British imposed a tax, which the Americans strongly disapproved of. A few years later the US entered the war as an ally of France.

instruction: If there was a future war for the control of freshwater supplies. Which nation has the greatest supply currently and would, therefore, be the best place to relocate now?
demonstration:
To make your decision, you will want to answer several questions first, such as:

What is the importance of the relative supplies of freshwater in a hypothetical global water war?
How much land will be needed for the size of your population?
How much land is available that can easily be acquired?
How large of an impact on your neighbors do you want to be?

And, after answering these, I think you should consider the recent conflict between Turkey and Syria over water rights, and how that is impacting both nations.

instruction: I'm learning about the several branches of the US Government. Right now, I am specifically focused on the judicial branch. Can you describe what the judicial branch is in charge of?
demonstration: Well, the Judicial Branch is a branch of the U.S. government, and specifically has jurisdiction over issues related to the law and courts. The Constitution creates the judicial branch, and the Federal Courts System governs it.  The basic role of the Judicial Branch is to interpret the law and rule on the cases that come before them.  Although each branch has different roles in the governance of the U.S., they are not designed to be separate and independent of each other.  In practice, they are instead in a constant and ongoing interrelation.

instruction: What are the fundamental principles of quantum mechanics?
demonstration: You’ve said that quantum mechanics is the study of energy and matter.  In classical mechanics, objects tend to have specific values for all the properties, and those properties don’t change over time.  In quantum mechanics, instead, an object might exist in a combination of different states at once, and different combinations of properties are possible.  This is a big difference between the classical theory and the quantum theory, and what leads to the strange and unexpected behavior of quantum systems.

instruction: What are the arguments for and against "stand your ground" laws in the united states?
demonstration:
Here is a starting point:

The Stand Your Ground law is essentially a “self defense” law.  At the core of the law is a legal principle called the “Castle Doctrine”.  Under that principle, it is legal to defend one’s property from intruders, even if the intruders are unarmed.  This makes sense, because property is an investment, and if you own it you should be able to preserve it from criminals.  

The Stand Your Ground law extends that principle to defense of yourself as well, though there are some restrictions on when you can use deadly force.  Specifically, it should only be used in “life-threatening situations” that happen in public.  And in some cases, you should avoid using deadly force even if it means you risk being harmed yourself.  That’s because using deadly force creates a much larger problem than the initial threat: the death of another human.

instruction: Hi! I'm trying to help my second-grader with their English homework and they are studying the "-it family". Do you have any suggestions on how I can help my child remember the words in this family?
demonstration:
Sure, I've got three different ideas for you:

1)  Ask your child about the "-it family" and if he knows the words are in the family already.  That way, he can tell you what he already knows.  If not, you can work on the rest of the "-it family" together. 
2)  Read a short story with them that includes some of the "-it family".  If they're really having trouble remembering the words, and if you're a proficient reader, you can read the story aloud to them and allow them to listen to the words that belong to the "-it family"
3)  Try memorizing the "-it family" together.  Have your child repeat the words back to you, and add to your own list every time you hear one of the words.  This might take a while, but I think it's the most efficient way to really work on helping your child remember the words.

instruction: Can you help me understand how lightning forms?
demonstration:
There are three main reasons for lightning:

The first is that static electricity is built up during thunderstorms.  When the sky is full of charged particles (called “ions”), the atmosphere becomes charged. When the electric field gets strong enough, electrons will move from negative to positive.  These electrons make up the cloud’s electric field, which is pushing on other electrons in the cloud and making them move.

Lightning is also caused by the flow of electric current.  When charged particles flow through a medium, they will spread out.  Once the electrons spread out into a big cloud, a lot of them will try to flow towards each other.  The movement of the electrons is what you call an electric current.

When the electric current grows really strong, there will be an electric field around it that pushes the electrons forward.  This is called a “loud” electric current.  It takes more and more energy to push the electrons forward.  Eventually, the electrons start moving forward so fast that they bump into atoms and knock other electrons loose.  These electrons also begin moving with the current, and the process continues.  This is called a “strike.”  After a little while, the electrons run out of

instruction: Is it ethical to go to watch an orca show?
demonstration:
To put it simply, no.  If you’re referring to Orca Productions, then they specifically say they aren’t an educational organization, they exist to entertain and amuse.  And as such, they use and display Orcas in ways that are, from a biological perspective, not very good for Orcas.

For example, their orcas swim in a large, open tank, with a large pool in the middle, as you can see on the photos.  As an orca, you wouldn’t be able to choose where to go, or decide how you want to use your time.  There’s no way to choose whether to swim or float or play or whatever, because you’re in a pool with no escape route, and the others orcas are driving you out of your territory.  Because of this, you can also see the inherent issues with Orca and human relationship, which is one of the underlying ideas of the show.  If orcas were free, they would not accept the tank.

instruction: Can you explain the traits of astrological signs?
demonstration:
Sure, here are some thoughts about the Zodiac.  Astrologers believe the sun’s motion relative to the stars in our night sky has an effect on our individual characters, and that we’re each inclined in different ways, and also in a cycle, to different traits.  They measure this by “Signs” of the Zodiac.  They are named for the constellations of stars that the sun is believed to “align with” at the time of our birth.  This is an approximation; the exact time of birth will also affect the signs we are said to be most aligned with.  Here are some popular characteristics of the signs, and some things that are particularly well-represented among them.  

Aries:  On the surface, there’s a lot of surface energy, ambition, an outgoing and exuberant personality, and this can also make Aries people more assertive.  There can also be a tendency to be impatient and impatient and it can also be tough to deal with the amount of negative feedback that some Aries people can generate.  

Taurus:   Taurus people are hard-working and practical, and are especially inclined to use their resources wisely and avoid waste.  Taurus people are very attached to things they value, and have a tendency to be fairly sensual.  They can also be very stubborn.  

Gemini:  Gemini people are clever, and often can be quick to learn new things.  They have a way of getting along with everyone, and often think of themselves as well, and often seek a certain amount of status and notoriety.  

Cancer:  Cancer people have a strong instinct for home and family, and there can be a tendency to overvalue and become attached to things.  They can also be a bit vulnerable, and sometimes find their feeling of safety and security threatened.  They can also be very protective of people they love.  

Leo:  Leo people are proud and individualistic, and can sometimes be a little too proud of themselves.  They’re also very proud of their families and sometimes jealous.  

Virgo:  Virgo people are detail-oriented and organized, and also sometimes are self-doubting.  They have a way of being critical and of taking things too seriously.  

Libra:  Libra people are thoughtful and open-minded, and tend to think about relationships and fairness quite a bit.  There can also be a tendency to be a little indirect and goofy.  

Scorpio:  Scorpio people have a way of being intensely and intensely sexual, and also very focused.  There can also be a tendency for Scorpios to be a little manipulative.  

Sagittarius:  Sagittarius people are outgoing, smart, and love to explore and take risks.  They also are quite idealistic.  

Capricorn:  Capricorn people are focused on the business world, and tend to be somewhat practical and conservative, and have a way of very clearly prioritizing their goals.  

Aquarius:  Aquarius people are very interested in the future, and also very open-minded and idealistic.  There can also be a tendency for them to be a little ungrounded, and to be detached from things they value.  

Pisces:  Pisces people have a way of being philosophical and introspective, and also sensitive, and have a way of being very creative.  There can also be a tendency to be lazy.

instruction: How do I teach my daughter how to multiply numbers?
demonstration:
For multiplication of 2-digit numbers, the steps are:

    *  Start with the easiest case: Numbers where the right-hand digit is less than or equal to the left-hand digit. For example, 6 × 2 is 10, so that number would be the “answer”.
    *  For numbers where the right-hand digit is bigger than the left-hand digit, that means the left-hand digit times itself (either 2 times the left-hand digit, or the right-hand digit multiplied by itself) would be too small, so we take off the left-hand digit, multiply by the right-hand digit, and add that number to the number we had previously. For example, for 4 × 2, we first calculate 4 × 2, which is 8, then 8 plus 4, which is 12, so the answer is 12.
    *  The basic strategy for multiplication of numbers with multiple digits on each side can be to start by writing the largest number of digits you need to make on each side, and see if you can use some of the steps above, but this will likely result in much more complicated steps.

But the pet may also be disobedient, so you may need to work with it to remind it what the boundaries are.  What I’d suggest is to allow the dog to eat at a designated time, and give it lots of time to exercise and play.  The goal is for it to eventually settle in on a routine that is healthy and satisfying.

instruction: Why is the Iraq War considered a failed war?
demonstration:
There are many reasons, and most of them are documented by media, military experts, historians, and so on.   Here are three:

1.  Death toll.   This doesn’t tell you anything about the “win” or “loss” of a war, but a high death toll is generally considered an indicator of a failed war.

2.  Amount of oil production lost.  Saddam’s Iraq was a particularly bad actor, not just because of his invasions, but also because he cut the flow of oil production in retaliation against sanctions and also refused to allow inspections.  This caused the price of oil to spike.

3.  Terrorism growth in the Middle East.  This is connected to the reasons above, but it’s worth noting that the rise of terrorism in the Middle East occurred after the fall of Saddam.

instruction: Describe the events leading to Pearl Harbor

In the 1930’s, there were several disputes between countries in Asia. The Japanese invaded Manchuria, a Chinese province, in 1931.  In 1937, Japan started a war with China and took the rest of China, until there was only a small pocket left around Shanghai.  In 1939, Germany and Italy allied themselves with Japan.  I think these are all pretty important events that caused Japan to attack.

instruction: Would you explain to me how measles are spread from human to human?

First, I could explain to you that if a human has measles, he or she is contagious from about four days before any symptoms are visible until the fourth day after the last visible symptom, which is known as the incubation period.  So when someone is contagious, they may spread the virus to other humans who have never had it before.

instruction: Will quantum computers rely on traditional programming languages such as C and Python?
demonstration: An entire system of theoretical approaches, also referred to as “quantum computing”, is designed to exploit the fundamental nature of quantum mechanics in computation. That said, these methods are still at an early stage of development, and as of today are only minimally applicable to practical computational problems. For that reason, quantum computers will not rely on traditional programming languages, but will rely on languages which are specifically designed for quantum computers. Such languages can be thought of as the hardware of the computer, and are a crucial part of how computers are programmed.

instruction: Someone gave me a bamboo plant as a gift. How do I take care of it?

You can also provide sunlight for the plant to help it grow, which can be accomplished by moving the plant to a window or outside!  You should be sure to leave some room for the plant to expand, as it will grow with time.

instruction: What are the requirements for getting a security deposit refund on an apartment lease?
demonstration:
Oh, no problem at all, I’m glad you asked! Here is how to process a refund for a security deposit at a Seattle apartment:

1) Make sure you get an occupancy agreement from your landlord (not a lease).

2) Make sure you get a written refund from the apartment owner, in the form of a note from your landlord, or their attorney, stating that your lease has been paid off, and the apartment is officially vacant.

3) Open a tenant security deposit claim through the city’s landlord-tenant division, as an unaccompanied minor. Fill out a CSC-1 form.

4) You should expect a refund within 30 days.

instruction: What happened at the Alamo?
demonstration: The Alamo is a part of the Fortress San Antonio complex that’s now a Texas State Park. On February 23, 1836, about 200 men gathered at the Alamo to hold off 500 Mexican troops until they were reinforced. They held out for thirteen days but when no help came, the defenders of the Alamo surrendered to Santa Anna. Most of the men were killed in the battle, with a few being imprisoned and executed by Santa Anna. The Alamo is recognized as a pivotal event in the Texas Revolution and remains a source of inspiration to Texans.

instruction: I want to know some things that the Victorians did that we would think are unusual. Can you help/
demonstration:
A few examples, with some possible explanations:

-Some parents took their babies to doctors who would give them laudanum, a dangerous opiate

-Some women in the 1920s wore hats with metal springs or wires inside them to push their hair away from their foreheads

-Popeye was a big cartoon in the 1920s, and some mothers told their children that eating spinach would make them strong like Popeye

-People would talk about being “blinded by the light”, which referred to going blind from reading books or newspapers too much in a brightly-lit room

-Some people carried a gas mask with them in case of a gas attack during the First World War

-Some people (mainly women) took baths in saltwater and it became fashionable

-Some women had their whole bodies tattooed, or just parts of their bodies like their arms or legs

-Some people wore wigs, which were usually made of human hair

instruction: Is it necessary to update my computer when updates are available?
demonstration: Do you need help checking that a computer needs a software update?  It depends on the circumstances of your computer.  Some computers are set up to automatically download and install software updates.  For some of those, you won’t be notified that updates are available until you check with the computer’s system settings.  It is possible that some software is not downloaded and installed automatically for security reasons.  If your computer is set up to automatically update software, you should check with your computer system’s settings to see if your computer has any updates available.  If you don’t update your computer as needed, your computer will continue to use software from an earlier version, which could lead to problems.  Also, depending on your settings, when you update your software, you may also get updates for any other software on your computer.

instruction: How do you treat a cold sore?
demonstration: For minor symptoms such as itching or discomfort, over-the-counter remedies like lysine, calamine lotion, or petroleum jelly can provide relief. I’ve also seen a few other remedies used. A paste made from chamomile flowers or the juice of a raw onion can both be applied directly to the area to provide temporary relief. Pomegranate juice can be applied to the sore by swabbing a cotton ball soaked in the juice directly onto the sore. A traditional remedy for oral herpes includes soaking the area in warm water for a few minutes, or applying a hot compress of an oil, such as sesame, olive, or coconut oil, which can reduce itchiness.

instruction: I want to learn about the history of Apple Inc.  Can you tell me about it?
demonstration: Sure.  Apple is a company that is headquartered in Cupertino, California. It was founded in 1976 by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne.  The company designs, develops and markets consumer electronics, personal computers, and computer software, and sells various related services, as well as other miscellaneous hardware and software.  It has an estimated 1.19 million employees, and the company's market capitalization was approximately $818 billion at the end of 2014.  Apple became the most valuable publicly traded company in the world in March 2011.  Steve Jobs was fired in 1985, and in 1996 he returned and later was made the CEO.  Steve Jobs was diagnosed with pancreatic cancer in 2003, and he was unable to perform his duties, and in 2011 he was appointed chairman of the board of directors and Steve Wozniak was named interim CEO.  Steve Wozniak and Steve Jobs both received the National Medal of Technology in 2000, and both received the National Medal of Science in 2007.

instruction: What does escrow mean when you're buying a house?
demonstration: Escrow means that the seller agrees to hold money that will be used to pay off the mortgage at a certain point.  In return, the buyer agrees to pay a certain percentage of the cost of the house upfront, and the buyer can’t take possession of the house until the seller agrees that the home has been paid for.  If there are any problems with the deal, the buyer can go back to escrow and get their money back.

instruction: Is Business Administration a good career?
demonstration: Business Administration is a general term for all of the skills and responsibilities that are used in running a business, or one specific business. It’s a broad field that spans various industries. A bachelor’s degree in business administration usually requires a broad education in business finance, business law, marketing, operations, management, and organizational psychology, so it provides the broad range of skills that are necessary in the management of a business. It also includes understanding the legal and regulatory frameworks within which business activities are organized. Does that answer your question?

instruction: What determines a cloud's shape?
demonstration: Which type of cloud?  For instance, a Cumulonimbus cloud forms because the heat of the sun warms the air, and the warm air rises, and is replaced by air below which also warms up, forming the top of the cloud.  They also have their own cloud classification.

This has been the most popular language for the creation of programs that run on the “Internet” (an online network of computers), and it started as an extension of “C” programming language.

instruction: Can you help me learn more about the founding father Alexander Hamilton?
demonstration: I can help you with this.  Hamilton helped design the original US government.  In that capacity, he was famous for his US Treasury Department, and for his arguments in favor of a strong federal government.  Hamilton himself, like many Americans at the time, was descended from a line of British-born immigrants.  Later in life, he ran for president and lost, but he remains a highly influential figure in US history.

instruction: How does a psychologist, decide if a person has an addiction? What are the boundaries?
demonstration: There isn’t a single correct answer to this question, but in my opinion a psychologist would need to make several assessments to determine if someone has an addiction. If I’m talking with someone, I’d probably start with some general questions about the person, to get to know them. For instance, do they have a substance use disorder? If so, why do they think they might have one? What do they think is the root of their problems? How do they feel about their addiction?

instruction: How long does a senator serve?
demonstration: The senate was established in 1789 and has elected presidents from 1789 to 2019, with the exception of 1795 to 1803.  The senate has been a part of the government since the founding of the country.  The length of each Senator’s term has ranged from six months to six years, which is the longest period served by a Senator in history.

instruction: Hi, I'm interested in pursuing a career in acting. Can you give me any tips on the type of work and education I should seek out in order to accomplish that?
demonstration: Sure! The term you might want to google is “Playbill”. That’s a somewhat old-fashioned term for theatre actors, though it has survived into contemporary usage, and means basically the same thing as what you mean by “acting”. In order to pursue this career path, you might consider starting with theatre acting. If you’d like me to recommend a particular type of theatre acting work to start with, I could suggest New York City theatre or community theatre, which could help you to get your feet wet and meet some people. Alternatively, if you want to do more mainstream acting, or you’d like to go in a more visual arts direction, you could try out for television and film, and/or commercials. Alternatively, if you want to go for something entirely different, I can tell you a little bit about YouTube and internet acting, or even crowd-sourced script writing, but this is less mainstream and might be more appropriate for those who are already doing theatre or film or TV. Also, I’d be happy to explain more about theatre acting to you if you’d like!

instruction: What types of illnesses could be caused by being overweight?
demonstration: There are many causes of illness, and most can be prevented by healthy living. Obesity is one of these. When a person gains weight, their heart has to work harder to pump blood around the body. This puts a strain on the heart, and can lead to heart disease and heart failure. Obesity can also lead to diabetes. Diabetes is when a person’s body has a hard time making or using insulin, and this makes the person feel tired and weak. Gaining weight can also make a person put on weight around the stomach and increase the risk of developing gallstones, kidney stones, high blood pressure, and osteoarthritis. It can also increase the risk of developing certain cancers and mental health problems such as depression, eating disorders, and stress.

instruction: How can I get rid of Ringworm
demonstration: I recommend using a topical fungicide, such as Clotrimazole or Miconazole, to treat a fungal infection that has invaded your skin.

So some people in these other countries are blaming the governments for putting them at risk, whereas in the US people are blaming Trump.

instruction: Can you tell me what kind of plants are good to keep inside the home?

instruction: I'm opening my own business and trying to figure out how to decorate my office in a way that's welcoming but professional. Do you have any advice on how to communicate that with wallpaper, paint, furniture, decorations, etc?
demonstration:
This is a fun question!  I’d like to start with something easy and pleasant: you mentioned “wallpaper”.  Are you looking to re-wallpaper an existing space, or do you have an empty office to decorate?  If it’s the latter, you might try looking for a large fabric roll that you can arrange into a kind of “scatter rug” and roll out to cover part of your office.  A friend of mine does something like this, except instead of a normal roll of fabric she’s got a giant roll of minky fabric, which she found on sale at a fabric store.  Minky fabric is a type of knitted fabric with a soft, fuzzy texture.  It’s perfect for an office because it has a super soft feel, but it’s also very durable, which is nice for an office.  You could also look for other types of low-cost, comfortable-to-walk-on carpets to spread around, like floor rugs or outdoor rugs.

If you do want to re-wallpaper your office, you might try looking online for

instruction: Should I eat organic vegetables?
demonstration: A popular belief is that organic food is healthier for us, but in many cases there is not strong evidence that this is true.  It’s possible that organic food just seems healthier to us, as we often don’t know how different the farming practices are, so we tend to think that they must be different.  So I think it’s best to just eat the food that you can afford, and that you find palatable.  On the other hand, some people are vegetarians or vegans, and that’s an important way to make sure you’re not eating meat, as it’s probably not healthy.

instruction: What are some cities to visit in Indonesia?

Also, when you’re in Indonesia, I suggest you go to a few of the islands nearby, including Sumba and Sumatra.

instruction: I want  to teach someone verb tenses. Can you explain to me the different kinds of verb tenses that exist?
demonstration:
Sure. There are many different tenses of verbs, some more common than others. Here are a few basic examples:  
Present: “I go to the store.”
Present progressive: “I am going to the store.”
Present perfect: “I have gone to the store.”
Past simple: “I went to the store.”
Past continuous: “I was going to the store.”
Past perfect: “I had gone to the store.”
Future: “I will go to the store.”
Future progressive: “I will be going to the store.”
Future perfect: “I will have gone to the store.”

instruction: How many versions of the movie Fame are there?
demonstration: There are four versions, in total.

instruction: The neighbor's kid has a peanut allergy.  How dangerous is that.
demonstration: Peanut allergies can be really bad, sometimes they’re life-threatening and people need to get an EpiPen to stop anaphylaxis. But I think there are some medications that people with peanut allergies can take to reduce the risk of life-threatening reactions.  To really be safe, we’d recommend that the kid be taught to recognize and avoid any foods with peanuts or any foods that have been made with peanut oil.  The whole family should be taught to be careful about what kind of food they share, and always watch for signs of anaphylaxis.

instruction: What are the benefits of a health savings account?
demonstration: I’m so glad you asked!  A health savings account is a great tool for keeping costs low for big health events like surgeries or for chronic illnesses like cancer.  It helps people save money over time, so they’ll have funds to cover expenses when it comes time for a procedure.  You’ll also get a tax break.

instruction: What are the fundamental principles of quantum mechanics?
demonstration: You’ve said that quantum mechanics is the study of energy and matter.  In classical mechanics, objects tend to have specific values for all the properties, and those properties don’t change over time.  In quantum mechanics, instead, an object might exist in a combination of different states at once, and different combinations of properties are possible.  This is a big difference between the classical theory and the quantum theory, and what leads to the strange and unexpected behavior of quantum systems.

instruction: Can you explain to me how wine is made?
demonstration: Sure!  There are a few ways to make wine, but the common ones are similar.  They boil the grape skins and seeds, then let it sit for several months, while the “yummy” alcohol components evaporate out.  A little bit of sugar is sometimes added to speed up the process.  Then it’s allowed to age in oak barrels for a year or more.  It can be a messy process.

instruction: How do I make a balloon arch?
demonstration: Go to a party store and buy some red or pink balloons.  Find some pliers or tweezers to make a small hole near the bottom.  Tie the end of a string (or a long shoelace) to the balloon and knot it.  Position the string up near the ceiling so it’s stretched taut.  Now get some thick cardboard.  Take your pliers and make a long incision along the edge of the cardboard.  Using the string from the balloon, tie the end of the cardboard around the string.  Now slowly and carefully pull the cardboard down to the floor.  The balloon will form a great big red or pink arch.  This is a brilliant party trick.

instruction: how do i get over fear of rejection?
demonstration: That’s a difficult question! You might try a few things. You could try to define what your particular fears are, and identify what you think is driving those fears. For example, fear of rejection might be really specific to something in your past that happened to you, or it might be much more general, something like anxiety about not being good enough for someone. So maybe you could start by listing all the past rejections you remember, and going through them with me.  We could go through them and think about what caused each rejection, and see if we can think of any patterns.  Maybe you could also look at some of the fears you listed in our last session, and talk about how they relate to your rejection fears. I think some of your previous fears are also related to rejection, and that you could probably make some progress here by digging into that.

instruction: What's the purpose and function of a compass?

It usually works by pointing to an object that is magnetized (for example, a piece of iron or a lodestone), which is called a “magnet”.  The needle inside the compass is called a “compass needle”.  We can draw a little picture of a compass to help us understand:  https://www.wikihow.com/Draw-a-Compass

instruction: What is the best way to fold a fitted sheet?
demonstration: Most likely, that’s the same as what people say about laundry: "Wet the bottom and hang it up".  Or for more immediate tasks, maybe run it through the washing machine.  But as a general idea, if you are preparing to make a product that uses a fitted sheet, the most effective way to fold it to store is to unfold it and press the top down into the center fold, then re-fold it along those creases.  Alternatively, for a shelf or something similar, you might want to lay it out flat, but make sure to only crease the top edge where you want it folded, and uncrease the rest.

instruction: Please tell me places to visit in New York City.
demonstration: Here are some suggestions:  I highly recommend checking out the Statue of Liberty.  You can also go to Central Park and take a ride on the Carousel.  Maybe see a show at Radio City Music Hall?  You can also find the famous museum called the Museum of Natural History, or go to the city’s planetarium.  Here are also some lesser-known things to do, like see the Tompkins Square Park Hip-Hop Fountain, or ride the High Line elevated park on the former elevated railway.  This park is a super interesting way to get a great view of the city, as well as some stunning architectural features like the Bellevue and The Lillian Wald Houses.  You can also do some really cool things with food, like check out some “Sky Rides” on the High Line, or get a 360-degree view of the city with the Hudson River Skateway.  There are also many cool restaurants to check out, like the Williamsburg Marketplace, and you can’t miss the Barclays Center, where the New York Knicks play!

instruction: How do you use a fire extinguisher?

instruction: Can you describe the differences between autotrophs and heterotrophs?
demonstration:
Autotrophs are organisms that are capable of synthesizing their own food through the chemical process of photosynthesis.  This includes plants, bacteria, algae, and some cyanobacteria.  They generally make organic molecules from inorganic molecules, releasing oxygen as a byproduct.

Heterotrophs are organisms that must consume other organisms to survive.  They require organic molecules for their metabolic processes.  Their carbon source generally comes from the carbon from carbohydrates, fats, and proteins from other organisms.  Some heterotrophs also release oxygen as a byproduct of their metabolic processes.  These include animals, fungi, and archaea.

instruction: Hi, I'm feeling nostalgic for the music of the 1990s. Can you help me put together a playlist of the most popular artists and songs from that period, preferably in a chronological way that shows how music changed through the decade?

instruction: What are some kinds of dance I might like if I like ballet?

Contemporary dance is another form that might be of interest.  Also, many contemporary dance performances might be staged in conjunction with more abstract instrumental music, or music that incorporates aspects of jazz.  Contemporary dance, as well as modern dance and ballet, often involve formal training and learning of techniques and movements, though this is not always the case.  Also, some other dances are not technically “classical” styles like ballet, but are still known as modern or contemporary dance.

instruction: Can you help me learn some information about Woodrow Wilson?
demonstration: Oh, Woodrow Wilson is a really interesting person!  He was the 28th President of the United States and is sometimes called “The Father of the American Middle Class” because he supported raising the wages of working people, and this was a very progressive and progressive thing to do in that time period.  He also established the Federal Reserve and created the Federal Trade Commission.  As President, he was also strongly pro-League of Nations, which was an organization with the goal of keeping the peace in Europe, and he also led the US into World War 1, which is important because it helped bring an end to WWI, which is also a very important accomplishment, because WW1 had been happening for almost two years already, and the US hadn’t entered the war yet.  In the 1920s, he was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize for helping to bring about peace in Europe.  What else?  Oh, he was also friends with Margaret Sanger, who was a feminist and an advocate of birth control, and she also made sure that contraception was available to poor women through her organization, Planned Parenthood, which is also a very important accomplishment.  And he was also very involved in politics during the Progressive Era.

instruction: Do you have a recipe for nachos?
demonstration:
What I know about making nachos:

There are basically two components: chips and cheese.  Chips are something you buy, cheese is something you make, and the process of combining them involves forming a ball with cheese, usually on top of the chips.  

To make chips, use oven-cooked, commercially-available tortilla chips and store them in a crisper. 

To make cheese, place 1 cup of evaporated milk in a pot over a high heat.  Add 3⁄4 cup of whole milk and 1⁄2 cup of heavy cream.  Bring to a simmer and then remove from heat.  Mix in 1⁄4 cup of butter, 1 cup of cheddar cheese, and a pinch of salt.  Use an immersion blender (or use a blender if you have one) to make it all smooth. 
The cheese ball is a very messy thing to make, but worth it, if you can keep all the cheese pieces intact.

instruction: What can you do with top soil?
demonstration: Top soil is soil that is rich in organic matter, which means it’s not very suitable for growing crops.  It’s best used to make compost, which is a mixture of decomposing organic matter that’s used to fertilize plants.  It’s also used to condition soils, which is when the top layer of soil is added to the bottom layer, and the dirt becomes more balanced and rich in nutrients.  The organic matter used for compost is usually made from yard waste, such as leaves, pine needles, and branches.  There are a variety of ways to make compost, including stacking the organic matter in a pile and adding water to let it decompose naturally, or using a heat source to speed up the process.

instruction: What is the Hoover Dam?

When completed, the Hoover Dam was the world’s largest concrete dam and is one of the largest dams in the United States.

instruction: How do I keep my skin healthy?
demonstration:
Great question! You’ll have a lot of different skin problems, including acne, wrinkles, age spots, and others.  It really depends on what you want to improve.  There’s a good chance I won’t know exactly the right answer to your question, but I might know some of the big issues.  Let me start with two basics:

1. Your skin needs certain things from you to stay healthy, and these include a regular routine for washing and using other things on it.  I can help you get started on a basic skin routine, if you like.

2. Your skin is mostly home to bacteria, and you need to be aware of that.  Some of these bacteria, like Staphylococcus, can be particularly annoying, but usually the whole issue of balancing bacteria and skin gets a lot of press only if there are concerns about skin infections.  It’s really not that hard to keep the bacteria under control.

So that’s an introduction, now to the specifics of your question:

Keeping the skin healthy -- and I’m using the word “healthy” to mean “able to do its functions well” -- can mean quite a few different things.

instruction: A friend told me that I could install Kodi on my Amazon firestick device. Can you teach me how to accomplish this?
demonstration:
Sure, we can do that! First I’ll explain what Kodi is, and then I’ll walk you through the steps.

Kodi is a very popular open source media player and has been called “the best media center on the planet”. It supports lots of different file formats and streaming protocols, and you can customize it to do lots of different things. Kodi also has a lot of support from user communities, which means there’s lots of different tips and tutorials out there for getting things set up.

The Amazon Fire TV Stick is a pretty nice and affordable Amazon device that uses the Amazon Fire OS, a custom Linux-based OS.

Now there’s a specific type of media player that runs on the Amazon Fire OS that is called “Kodi”. This is just a special version of Kodi which is specialized to run on the Fire OS, and you can download it here: https://kodi.tv/download/

You’ll also need to know that there’s a special channel on your Fire TV Stick called “Amazon Fire TV”. If you haven’t done it before, go ahead and press the little “menu” button on your remote to show this menu, and find the “My Apps” or “My Channels” option, and select this option. This will take you to a menu of all the apps or channels that you currently have installed.

You’ll want to do the following:

First, download the Kodi file, and then once it’s finished downloading, open the file and follow the installation instructions. You’ll need to have admin privileges to install the app on your Amazon Fire TV Stick. To install the app, you’ll need to go back to the “My Apps” menu, then scroll down and find the “Install or Remove Apps” option, then select that. You can then choose to install the app, and you’ll be taken through a very straightforward installation process.

Once Kodi has been installed, you’ll want to use the remote to go to the Kodi home screen. This is accessed by going back to the main menu, selecting “My Apps”, then scrolling down to the “Kodi” item, then selecting it. This will take you to the main Kodi home screen, which will show you a brief introduction to Kodi, then will ask you to sign in with your Netflix or Amazon credentials.

This is where you should do the following:

Make sure you’re signed in to Netflix. Now you’ll want to scroll down on the right side of your Kodi home screen, then find the “Library” menu option. Select this menu, then look on the left side of your screen for an option called “Live TV”. Select this option, then look on the right side of your screen for an option called “Add Source”. Select this option, then look in your Amazon app store and search for a program called “FireTV Channels”, which you can then download. Once this is finished downloading, open the app and follow the instructions for how to add the FireTV channel to Kodi.

If you did all of these steps correctly, you’ll have your Amazon Fire TV Stick connected to the internet, and you should see a collection of various Amazon TV shows and movies on the left side of your Kodi home screen!

instruction: Can you tell me the history of Cathédrale Notre-Dame de Paris?

instruction: Which country consumes the most chocolate per capita?
demonstration: That’s a tricky question to answer definitively.  We could start by finding the world’s largest chocolate consumers, and then see how many other countries they represent, and the percentages of their populations, and then extrapolate this to see how many chocolates they would need to eat to represent the whole population.  The largest chocolate consumers are the United States, Switzerland, and Germany.  If we consider all the countries in the world, you’ll find that the US and Switzerland together have over 20% of the world’s population.  By this metric, it seems that if the US ate even a tenth of its current amount of chocolate, it would eat more chocolate than the rest of the world combined.

instruction: Can you tell me a little bit more about the battle of the bulge?
demonstration: The battle of the bulge was a major campaign during World War II. This war included multiple simultaneous fronts, and the “battle of the bulge” was just one part of that campaign.  During this campaign, the German army reached France and tried to attack the Allied forces.  This attempt was hindered by a number of large-scale Allied counter-attacks.  These counter-attacks included one near the Ardennes Forest in the Netherlands.

instruction: How do I sleep better without waking up through the night?
demonstration: To prevent waking up through the night, we could change the factors affecting your sleep.  For example, this app tracks your sleep patterns and can help you determine what factors are causing you to wake up during the night, then suggests strategies to help with those causes.  The app also works as a simple alarm clock, that automatically wakes you up at a time you choose, rather than when your sleep cycle is naturally over.  This can prevent your sleep from being disrupted in the middle of the night.

instruction: I have very dry and damaged hair. Could you suggest somethings to help me repair it?
demonstration:
There are a variety of ways to repair your hair that can help minimize breakage and improve the quality and look of your hair. The following are a few tips that have been successful for some people:

Wash your hair regularly with a natural shampoo.

Use conditioner to add moisture and soften your hair.

Never brush your hair when it’s wet. Use a soft bristled brush on dry hair.

If you use a blow dryer, use low heat and the gentlest setting available.

If you do use heat on your hair, protect your hair from the sun, as heat and sun exposure are damaging.

Apply gentle oil like jojoba oil to your hair before shampooing.

instruction: I see a lot of pigeons all the time. What is their purpose in the world?
demonstration: Pigeons and doves are descended from birds that lived in the Palearctic region, which is that big chunk of land across the middle of Europe and Asia that used to be tundra.  From a few million years ago onward, they evolved alongside and co-evolved with human hunter-gatherer societies.  There are also stories of how pigeons have helped spread messages over long distances during times of war and other crises.  In a really general way, pigeons are kind of like heralds, because they bring news from far away, and I guess you could say they’re one of our planet’s way of keeping us connected to one another, across space and time.  You could even say that they remind us of the importance of personal relationships, and the urge we all have to reach out and connect with others.  It’s hard to get this kind of perspective from an article or a podcast.  Have you ever read a pigeon blog?  Or even just had a conversation with someone who’s been pigeon-sitting recently?  It’s such a wild experience to just let these birds enter your life for a week or a month and see what it’s like

instruction: How do I get to Allentown PA from NYC?
demonstration: Let’s see, there are multiple ways. If you’re going by car, your quickest option is to take the New Jersey Turnpike to Interstate 95 south. After that, it’s I-95, then I-78, then I-76. Finally, turn left onto Chestnut Street. If you’re taking public transit, there are multiple options. Buses are available from Port Authority Bus Terminal and also from George Washington Bridge Bus Terminal, among others. Also, Greyhound has services that run from the Port Authority and from Newark, among others.

instruction: Please tell me about Thanksgiving Day and the history behind it?
demonstration: Thanksgiving was created as a day to celebrate after a successful harvest. The United States Thanksgiving holiday is held on the fourth Thursday of November. The first Thanksgiving was celebrated in 1621 in Plymouth. It was the English settlers’ first successful harvest in the New World. It became an annual tradition, and every president since Abraham Lincoln has declared a national Thanksgiving holiday. It is celebrated as a time of giving thanks and spiritual reflection. Thanksgiving is a cultural holiday which marks the beginning of the autumn harvest season. The most widely celebrated American holiday, it is observed on the fourth Thursday of November by families and friends coming together to share a traditional meal.

instruction: What has Sandra Day O'Connor been doing since she retired from the Supreme Court?
demonstration: What she said in her retirement announcement was that she'd “long hoped to ‘find ways to use the skills, experience and knowledge I acquired in my work at the Supreme Court to try to make this country better and wiser and freer’”. She added that she's been “keeping her eyes and ears open, and [she’s] been giving a lot of thought to ways she could do that”. She also said that she'd heard from many Americans who are “smart and effective and who want to help make our country even better”, and she wants to “help them find their own opportunities to serve”.

instruction: What should you know before you sign a lease?
demonstration:
I think it’s important to know that a lease is like a promise. It says the landlord will repair the property and not evict you, so it’s very important to be specific about these issues. If you have a broken lock or a leaky ceiling, you need to be very clear about what you want done about those issues, or else you could end up being evicted!

It’s also important to know what the lease language says about the “privacy rights” between you and the landlord, so that you can have some privacy if you need it. For example, the US has a federal law that prohibits landlords from spying on tenants, or from charging tenants for storing or accessing their own personal property, such as their clothing. Some state laws and local ordinances also have privacy protections.

instruction: Hi, I'm interested in playing rugby. Can you explain the rules of the game to me?
demonstration: Rugby is a team sport played between two teams of 15 players, with a ball. Each team tries to score a goal by kicking or passing the ball into the other team's in-goal area.  Rugby is played on a large grass field, with a rectangular goal at each end.  The game consists of two halves of forty-five minutes, with a break at half time of 15 minutes. The two halves are separated by a break of ten minutes, while teams change ends. The game is officiated by a referee, who is assisted by two touch judges.

instruction: What are some common psychic practices that are thought to have validity?

instruction: What are night terrors and why do people have them?
demonstration: Night terrors are periods of loud, intense, and rapid-fire sleep.  People typically have night terrors when they’re sound asleep, and they sometimes even scream loudly and act as if they’re terrified.  In many cases, night terrors are caused by severe stress, and they’re generally considered harmless.

instruction: How do you remove the residual heat from cooked pasta once you remove it from the boiling water?

This will effectively allow the residual heat to dissipate by conduction and convection.

instruction: What is kidney failure?
demonstration: It means that you have very little or no kidney function.  Kidneys are an important organ of the body that filters out waste, like urea and other things from the blood.  In kidney failure, the waste builds up in your bloodstream, and eventually the whole body becomes toxic and fails.

instruction: What are some exercises I can do in the pool for my knees?

First, swimming is easier on the knees than land-based exercises because you’re not putting your full body weight on your knees.  But it can also be more effective because of the stretching and strengthening effects of the water.  Another exercise you might try is simply using an exercise ball while in the pool.  But I think swimming really should be your focus.

If you’re thinking of getting a pet turtle, and you’re going to force your kid to be happy with it, that’s a different story.  I feel like kids can be happy with almost any pet, unless it’s a pet you’ve chosen for them and not something they’ve picked out.

instruction: What should I do if I have a nose bleed?

instruction: What kind of requirements are needed for land travel between the US and Mexico?
demonstration: It’s often stated that to go by car from the US to Mexico, you need to have a valid US driver’s license and a car that is properly registered in the US.  This is because a car can often be in one country for some period of time without being officially registered there.  And also it can take months to get a proper license in Mexico, which involves testing in Spanish and passing a few exams in Mexican law.  If you have a car that is registered in the US, you can drive it anywhere in the US and will not be stopped, because it will say “US” in the registration sticker.  You can drive across the border into Mexico and do not need a special permit to cross the border, because you are going to a place where the vehicle can legally stay.  If you want to go anywhere in Mexico, you’ll also need to show your passport to the border police, which is what you’ll use to fly on a plane.  Since there are very different laws for owning cars in the US and Mexico, you’ll need to check those laws and make sure that it is legally possible for you to own a car in Mexico and to drive it.  If your car is newer and has newer

instruction: How can I take a cruise if I get seasick?
demonstration: You can take a cruise, but you might need a specific type of medication to help with the seasickness, and you might also want to get plenty of rest.  The long term solution to seasickness is to learn to get some exercise every day, especially if you’re prone to a more serious form of seasickness called vertigo.  If you’re a beginner, you can start with walking for a couple hours at a time.

instruction: How does it feel to be anxious?

But if I try to feel what you are describing, I imagine that it’s like there’s this constant background anxiety, you always have this feeling that something terrible is just about to happen, like there’s a natural disaster or someone’s going to die or something awful. And because it’s a constant background feeling, you know it’s just in your head, but it’s really intense.  And you can’t make it go away.  I’m imagining all of this to try to think about what you might mean by “anxiety”, but I really don’t know what that feels like.

instruction: Who is considered the best basketball player of all time and why?

instruction: Do you know anything about the salem witch trials?

This accusation was accepted as truth, and the townspeople arrested and tortured them, despite the fact that there was no evidence that they had done anything wrong.

instruction: What are the origins of the Super Bowl and how did it get its start?
demonstration: The Super Bowl started in 1967, by which point it was called the AFL-NFL World Championship Game.  It was played to decide the winner of the 1966 season, a tie between the American Football League and the National Football League.  There was no tiebreaker game, so it was decided by a one-game playoff.

instruction: What is a staph infection?
demonstration: A staph infection is when bacteria that normally live on the surface of your skin have become infected, causing a reaction, often with rashes. It’s fairly common and can be easily treated with antibiotics.

instruction: Are there good ways to make my teeth whiter, besides going to a dentist to get a whitening?

instruction: Can you explain how a steam turbine work?

instruction: What is a good drink to toast to on new years?
demonstration: A warm toast would be to a hot chocolate, or perhaps a glass of milk, or something slightly alcoholic, like a coffee cocktail or eggnog.

instruction: Curious to know where sangria comes from, the history of it
demonstration: Sangria is a combination of wine and fruit juices (typically peach, pineapple, apple, and orange), sometimes with a splash of brandy. The concept of mixing wine and fruit came from Spain, when Pedro Ximenez grape growers needed to remove the excess liquid from their grapes, so they would mix it with fruit and water to create a drink called sherry. Eventually, other fruit juices and juices from other fruits were added, and the drink became sangria. Many recipes can be found online, from simple fruit mixes to complex and sophisticated cocktails.

instruction: How do I tell my kid not to lie?
demonstration: I don’t want to say a lie is always “wrong”, but if we call lying “being in the “opposite” of truthfulness”, then generally speaking, lying is bad for kids.  “Moral” is a difficult word because different people have different moral values, but in general it’s the case that kids don’t have the sense of “morality” that adults have.  They will understand that lying hurts other people, but they won’t have the internal sense that they are doing something wrong by hurting other people.  And they won’t really understand that lying hurts themselves, either, because it takes a lot of experience of hurt before kids can even notice it.
